playStoreReviews.text: This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

Looked at several much more expensive apps and just could not justify the price tag. Thought I would give these guys a try since the premium was so affordable. Best. Decision. Ever. Has seriously helped me up my game and reach my goals. Too many positives to list here, but seriously, just try the free version and if you like it, drop the $25 for a year. I bet you won't regret it.

I've tried a lot of different workout logging apps and this one has quickly become my favorite. Very easy to use at the gym and the api and web interfaces make it easy to plan workouts. One thing I would love is a widget that would let me check off a set and see my rest timer for my active workout. Not sure how hard that would be, but it would be convenient for me. Thanks for a great app!

I was using AI for a while for my workout tracking, but it easily gets clogged with all the data and information being input over the course of weeks and months. Hevy addresses exactly what I need with logging weight and exercises, and it has a rich database of exercises. It would be nice to have the ability to complete my sets for exercises from the lock screen (Android), without having to buy a smartwatch.

I appreciate how easy it is to set up how I want to work out. I did a paper journal before and this is WAY better! I also appreciate (as someone new to strength training), how each exercise has a gif of how to do it properly and what muscle groups it is actually working. I paid for an annual membership, but I am so impressed that I plan to switch it to the lifetime option! This will ensure that I always have my past data to compare to.

Hevy is by far the best workout app I've used. It's easy for beginners and experienced lifters alike. Every exercise includes helpful descriptions, making it simple to learn proper form. The app is clean, easy to use, and the premium version is very affordable. Absolutely worth downloading! Love this app!!!

Usually really great. A wide variety of exercises and good "how to" instructions. However, every time I use the app for an extended period of time (such as when I am tracking a workout) I lose the ability to click on anything within the app. I can scroll through, but cannot tap the checkmark to complete the set, or tap the three dots to reorder my workout. The only way I have been able to fix it is by closing out the app. I have had to do it multiple times during a single 1 hour workout.

I really love the primary features of this app. I've been doing a recommended full body split with a few mods to swap out exercises I don't like. it's super easy to switch things up when machines are occupied or I'm in a different gym. I also really like the calculator. Occasionally, syncing with my Pixel watch gets weird, but that might not be the app's fault. I tried one of the AI recommendations, but I didn't find much use for it. The basics are great!

I have never been so consistent and so motivated to track my workouts! I love to track my personal records and be able to push myself. 💪 So many features, even in the unpaid version. Some of the math goes a little funny when you try to share it to Instagram stories (one rep max frequently doesn't make sense), and I do wish it had more calisthenic type exercises listed. But for free, it's an incredible app.

Almost perfect! So honestly this is a great app. very easy to navigate and use. as someone who's just starting the gym, it made it a breeze to log it. I also love how when I launch a workout with the app on my phone, it syncs it with my watch for heart rate and better calorie burn calculation. my only minor issue is I wish they would update the workout and keep adding more as if im doing a specific exercise and they dont have that one, I have to go for the closest similar one to track it

Great app! I highly recommend it. I became more consistent because of many reasons, like the muscle chart which shows which muscles I worked out over the week, and logging is pretty seamless. It's easy to replace an exercise in your routine while you're working out if a machine isn't available. Also, it displays your PRs and the amount of weight you lifted in a session, which is encouraging. I'm very impressed with the team. They're pretty active on Reddit too, constantly seeking feedback.

Decent app. Wish there were filters to follow people based on age, country, etc. Tough to find people at my age and fitness level otherwise. The profile comparison feature is nice, but limited to only barbbeĺl exercises which is a poor design choice. Statistics are fairly thorough but a tad clunky IMO. Restricted to four saved routines for free version (understandable) is a tad limiting. Very responsive team on the bug reports. Overall, a good fitness tracker.

Love the fact it works on my watch. Not having to bring my phone around the gym is really convenient. Sometimes it doesn't sync correctly. Not sure which product in the Google chain is responsible for adding the information to Google Health (formerly Fit) but more information would be nice. Finally, it is REALLY annoying to scroll through minutes and seconds to enter time. There needs to be a way to type time in. This was the first gym training app and I won't switch to anything else.

Great tool for keeping track of your routines, easily edit and add what you want per week or if your feeling a little gassed out removing sets and exercises is really easy but keeps your routine the same for thr next time if you want. I appreciate the alerts too after rest times are up so if your caught day dreaming to a song it gets yah back on track. going to buy the lifetime membership once my 3 month tracking runs up, just so I can keep the progress logged long term.

Best free app for tracking workouts and measuring progress. Used a different app for a while until they started locking exercises unless you paid for a premium account. This app has a huge library of exercises with a bunch of different variations and anything that is missing you can add yourself as a custom exercise.

Tried to create a routine and the moment I had more than a couple exercises in it I had to start saving and updating the routine after adding an exercise. Then going back in and editing it to be able to add sets or more exercises all the buttons in the UI of the routine would not let me add more sets or exercises after adding one or the other. Glad I didn't go with Pro and waste money on it.

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

Great app for tracking your workouts and inexpensive compared to other apps (You pay the same amount for lifetime access as you would for a year of other apps). Granted, this app does not create workouts for you... but, in my mind, ID rather go to a free AI product and create a workout there and pay less here than pay a stupid amount of money to have the apps AI make something up. Plus, this way I have a lot more control over the final workout. Definitely worth a download to check it out.

Really great app. The free version has literally everything I need to track my workouts. I love how easy it is to use. I can swap exercises in my program with a couple taps. I love the exercise library and pre-con programs. There's also a plate math calculator!! And you can customize it!! No ads, no bloat, just a great tool. The social aspect is an added bonus! Don't sleep on this app.

I love this app for tracking my workouts! It's so easy to use and customize. I love that it syncs with Strava, too, since the cardio tracking isn't quite as good as the weightlifting tracking. It's super fun receiving medals for progress! It provides helpful info on correct form and has pretty much every exercise you could want. If you have a lot of different routines, you'd need to upgrade, but I'm perfectly fine with the 4 free ones.

Absolutely love this app. I love that it includes animations about how to do specific workouts, and I love all the growth tracking features. I feel like I've made more progress in the last two months with this app than I have in years without it! There is also a certain "game" element that I enjoy, in that I am constantly trying to beat my own records. It also contains tons of premade exercise routines. I never pay for apps, but this one was worth it.

I would like a larger variety of exercises that don't require equipment as much. I only have a bench and some adjustable weights. So I feel like my overall options are limited much like the space I have to work out. so far for having the app for a week I do find it convenient and easy to use and they don't try to price gouge you every month with barely anything to offer. 🧐👍

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. It can also seamlessly sync with Galaxy Watch. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

This app is great! it has everything you need to track a workout. logging exercises is easy, reviewing a graph of your past weight on each exercise is available to track progress. I use it for two separate routines at different gyms where the available weight machines are slightly different and I have to adjust the weight - Hevy reminds me of the previous weight I lifted on that routine which makes it easy for me. And, all that is on the free version!

I recommend to everyone I meet! This is user friendly and helps you with exercises if you don't know how to do them. I love you can add in your own exercises or equipment that is not in the app already. I barely tried the free trial and loved it, so I signed up for a year, but after that I may upgrade to lifetime! I love all the real life weight comparisons!

Honestly such a great app. I have been working out for literally years and have not gotten the progress I've had since I started using Hevy. It makes tracking so easy and the muscle distribution chart and heat map help me to see what areas I need to work on and what are getting overworked. If you're a gym goer, this is the app for you. Tell your friends and compare progress and exercises!!

I love everything about this app. It gives you data about what you do, never its opinion about whether you've gone too hard or not hard enough. It tracks your lifts over time, and celebrates your PRs. Thoughtful features include rest timers, a thorough list of lifts with written and video instructions for each one, and very minimal social feed. I show this app to all my gym buddies, because it's so useful without any unnecessary bells and whistles.

Very easy to use and has all the exercises I'll need. I use free version. That being said, the reason for 3 stars is that how the app measures some workouts should change. Lunges should include body weight in volume lifted. It would be nice if there was a record for total volume lifted for one exercise in a workout, not just for one single set. Sled push should include weight added, not just distance.

I have always logged my workouts for the sake of keeping track of my progress. The problem is that I use to write everything down - the old fashioned way, pen and paper (I know, I'm old). I decided to give this app a try for the sake of efficiency and convienence. Long story short, this app did not disappoint. It has everything in it I could ask for. I don't have a lot of experience in workout apps, but I know I have no need for a different one. Great job Hevy! - A consistent user

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

I have the payed version, I like the selection of equipment it matches my gym pretty good, the trainer work really good, rotates exercises and equipment so it doesn't get stale and also gi e you replacement options if there's something you don't like or if a piece of equipment isn't available, seeing the past logs and set ratings helps alot, only thing i don't like is it doesn't have some of the past history if I switch routines.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

Before I downloaded Hevy I found it so difficult to actually track my progress. This app organizes it so well and makes each is so simple to track your progress in individual exercises, muscle groups, etc. And building your own workouts as a beginner is also incredibly easy as it will filter exercises for your needs to help you get started. I've been using it for about 2 years now and have put on significantly more muscle since starting the app.

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

This app is AMAZING im the type of person that when I go to the gym I want to get in and get out. The fact that you can load your workout and check it off is amazing no more guessing or trying to remember what you did ITS ALL RIGHT THERE and the fact that its free is insane. So far I've lost 25lbs and I know this app had alot to do with it by keeping me on Track😉

Great app that can track a full routine for free. No ads so far, and the subscription is inexpensive if you want to track more then one routine. Adding workouts to the routine is simple and they have have a good selection of routines for everything from beginner to advanced workouts. Each workout also shows a simplified gif and explanation of how to do the workout.

Honestly this was the secret sauce to seeing real progress in the gym as I use the app to record every weight and rep and am able to increase it every or every other week by 2.5-5lbs. On top of it you're also able to organize and create entire workout routines for yourself. Would definitely recommend, even better if you have other gym buddies so that you can compete a bit with each other using its social media features

I used to track all my workouts using an actual physical journal. one of the influencers that I follow on Instagram recommended hevy I made the switch and I'll never go back. the free version allows you to create four routines that you can utilize and if you pay for the subscription you can use even more it's worth every penny but the free version is great for beginners or intermediate lifters.

Really good app for tracking workouts. It's easy to setup routines, find the right exercises, and track your progress, what muscles you've used, etc. Everything is easy to navigate and use. I've been using the free version but might upgrade just to support such a great app.

Love the free, no ad functionality, been using it for a week and im considering buying the premium (option for one time purchase is exceptional). App functionality as well as tracking is intuitive and simple, integration with other health tracking apps and wearable support is straightforward, overall an app built for the user, I wish more companies used this kind of business model, they'd make a lot more money

I've been using this for 4 months. I like that I can set my own routines and it updates with the weight/reps I did in my last session. It makes it easy to keep track of progress. The app also makes it really easy to replace an exercise with something similar when the machine/equipment may not be available.

Been using this app since ~2021 and it has been amazing overall. Likes: Tracks historical data, showing progress and strength growth. Allows for customized workout plans you can craft around what is available to you. Fun little ribbon-achievements every time you hit a new high weight per lift. (It's very motivating!) Lifetime pass is reasonably priced (and I highly recommend it). Dislikes: AI coaching plan that comes with premium could be more customizable. overall highly recommend this app.

whatever happened to being able to change the rest time, completing sets, or anything while you have a routine running is broken. it's so frustrating to try to check off a set but even after 10 taps it doesn't go through. I tried turning the rest time off in case it was some sort of conflict in timers or something but this main thread be going wild here

Absolutely love this app, it works great in helping you track your progress. The only true issue that I have with this app is that I copy my previous workouts for routine and not always do I complete each exercise due to time but keep them in for the next week.. that being said even if those exercises are not checked off per set, the app is still tracking those towards my volume lifted at the end thus not truly accurate in thw end. Would love to see this updated.

This app makes it super easy to keep track of your workouts, gives you a monthly summary, and is overall simple to use, and all using the free version. They also reverted back to manual time entry, which is so much easier than using the scrolling wheel, so that's an easy 5 stars from me.

Easy to use and great for beginners. I liked the preloaded routines to start and create my own routines in the app now that I'm more comfortable in the gym. I like the progress tracking graphs, it motivates me to see that I've gotten stronger. A lot of great features for a free app!

I was looking for a new weightlifting log app that had more than just the basics. I love the simplicity of the interface and the ability to discover new exercises. The user interface has intuitive options to quickly record your sets and add new exercises on the fly.

straight up love this app. the ease of tracking workouts is amazing. I've tried quite a few and nothing comes close. I love the ability to easily see the weights Ive used just recently. I've always had issues I get to the gym and have to start essentially anew. 100% worth the price.

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

Great app. The free version is good enough for most people, supporting up to 3 workout routine rotations. I decided to upgrade to the paid version to have the flexibility of having more routines. I love the tracking, the timing between sets, ability to do supersets (no breaks between 2 or more workouts) and the tracking of your best overall lifts. This app helped me gain 10 lb of muscle in 8 weeks. Current total gain is 15 lbs of muscle. Goal is 30 lb 💪

after using the free version for a couple of months I moved on to the Pro version and updated my review from 4 to 5 stars. for a few bucks a month, it's a no brainer. the app works very well and syncs seamlessly with my watch during workouts. I track 3 90-minute sessions a week with 32 sets over 8 exercises each session, mainly all with my watch. love the stats and reports as well. haven't found anything major to complain about.

Super simple and straight forward. I just wanted a way to track my workouts like a spreadsheet but needed the spreadsheet to be on steroids, and this is exactly that. I barely found out today that the app also has a way to track not only your weight but measurements of multiple body parts and appendages, like my neck, my back, my... you get the point.. it's super dope. I bought the lifetime membership. 100% worth it cause I can have all of my routines and variations of them stored in there.

The app is really good, makes updating and tracking your progress workout to workout easy. My only complaint is that I wish their was a way to create a routine from your current workout, currently you'd have to remember your weight and rep ranges, plot them all down in the tracker, and save it. would be very convenient to just have an option added to the drop-down menu during your current workout, other than that though Hevy is great.

This app is great. I was using the bodybuilding app and when they updated it last year, it completely ruined it and I was on the hunt for a better app. This app is the best one I've ever used. It tracks your progression, personal bests, best volume, literally anything. It even adds up the total weight lifted and will tell you if you have lifted a truck, dinosaur, yacht, and more. One thing I would love to see in the future is badges or trophies or anything like that.

I switched from Strong to Hevy at the behest of a friend who wanted to see my workouts and it's been a real upgrade! I even upgraded to the paid version when they had an end of year discount and haven't regretted it. I'm a real sucker for data visualization and it's been cool to see myself improving over time in graph form. Have only used it for a few months but so far it's been fantastic!

Really great workout app. A wide selection of exercises even if you don't have that much equipment at home, you can easily find some exercises for every muscle group. I believe the app recently improved supersets workouts. I love the scroll to the next exercise feature, especially useful with supersets,so I don't have to wonder around what is next.

I really like this app. I use the free version for about 30 days before I decided I wanted to upgrade to Pro. I think the free version would be great for someone who only works out at one gym, but I use multiple gems so this allowed me to create different routines for each gym and Unlimited Custom exercises. you have to dig into the settings a little bit to get your rest timer working properly, and just to get things the way you like them to be as far as reading your effort and things like that.

I don't write reviews for anything, except Hevy. It's the only gym tracker I've stuck with. It's easy to use, definitely worth the one time purchase for the additional workouts and custom exercises. My favorite is at the end of a workout seeing exactly how much I've lifted and what that compares to (like a T-Rex!) Thank you for making an app for the gym that isn't bloated or gamified, just gains! ❤️

I'll start by saying I really like this app and recommend it to all my friends and students. But I'm starting to get irked with the lack of integration with Garmin because I can't track my heart rate otherwise. So then I have to have 2 trackers running at once and monitor both. I'm also annoyed that exercises like Kettlebell Swings aren't accounted for in the muscle group breakdown. I feel like that's an easy fix. You give primary and secondary muscles for everything else. Why not these?

Great app. One small problem. I love this app. It makes tracking my workouts super easy and it plays well with my other fitness apps, however, the WearOS version drains my watch so fast. I don't know if it's tracking more than the Fitbit app does, but I can track a whole hour and a half workout with the Fitbit app, and still have enough battery to last the rest of the day, but with this app tracking my workouts, I'll get down to 15% and have to charge again before I go to work.

solid workout tracker. you can use an AI plug in to work out a progression routine. overall just like my old notebook and pen. except much easier to read. for just wanting to track your weights reps and volume can't beat this. I like this app so much I paid for pro Even though the free version is completely functional for all my use cases. keep up the good work!

I absolutely love this app. It's given me a way to keep track of my lifts, build out plans, doesn't constantly ask me for money, and the free version does everything you need it to. Can take notes about my workouts, can add in modifiers, can even make your own excersizes if you know how to import the right files. Wouldn't have the progress I've made so far without it. Easy win dog.

Love it. Setup routines ahead of time. Track reps, weights and sets as you go. Notes are visible right where you want them. The data from the previous workout is right on the screen so you can modify if needed. Rearrange or modify the routine on the fly if a station is not available or a different hit is wanted. Keep changes or discard them after. Very large exercise library as well the ability to add custom exercises. This is the perfect tool to use in the gym and when planning at home.

I've used the free version of this app, for about 4yrs now. It has a large base of exercises and workouts. Options range from Free weights, machine weights, kettlebells, resistance, body weight, and cardio. All options have a "How to" for reference. It allows you to track workout time, Reps, Sets, weight, failure/warmup, supersets, has a section for notes, and you can save/view all prior workouts! I LOVE IT and I would highly recommend!!

I've used a variety of workout tracking apps, and this is the best one I've used. I've been using this app exclusively to track my workouts for over a year now. It's worth paying for the upgrade a you can use to customize and build multiple routines. It keeps track of all of my workouts for every type of exercise and allows me to track the weight, sets, reps, and volume since I began using the app. I really like how customizable and easy it is to adjust my plans.

Had the app for 3 weeks and I'm very impressed with it. It's what I thought I would get with Zepp and the new Fitbit interface, but they failed miserably. Heavy allowed me to build my own routines for each day and then it auto fills your previous weights and reps each exercise so you aren't having to keep a log in a notebook. That allows me to push my limits and get stronger and grow my muscles. Highly recommended!

really love the app and the wide variety of exercises to choose from when creating my workouts. the one thing that would move this to a 5 star would be the ability to have more than three workouts saved. it's not a huge deal but I'd love to be able to see older programs and know what I did so I can modify new programs. aside from that it's a great app

lots of exercises to choose from or create your own. let's you create up to 4 individual workouts for free version. being able to add notes to the exercise, set an automatic timer. and reflect at the end of your workout are great features. If you are new to workouts, use the reflect feature and be honest about your struggles, then come back to them after a few weeks of working out and compare yourself to that former person.

I have been using the Hevy app for over a year to track my progress lifting weights. I find it very helpful with planning routines, selecting equipment and exercises, as well as daily tracking completion. It helps me remember my routines and keeps track of previous accomplishments. The reports and statistic are also useful to evaluate progress.

I'm a week/ 3 workouts into the free version of Hevy. The best part for me so far is the motivation of seeing other people showing up! If they can do it, I can too... it's fairly intuitive, I was able to set up my first weight lifting workout without a problem, and u can log other things like walking, yoga, skiing... I'm very happy so far. editing to add I've made it to 50 workouts and paid for a lifetime subscription! nothing but great things to say about Hevy app

worth trying if you need a free app. will allow up to 4 routines and pro is unlimited. 3 months of data stored for free and unlimited for pro. tbh, simple, intuitive and allows synchronization of health connect and other apps that track health. definitely enjoying the free and most likely will purchase the lifetime option for the pro, but like I said free is pretty awesome too. btw, speaking as a beginner lifter

I really like using this app for my weight focused workouts. It keeps track of all my stats and the little how to videos for each exercise are great for when I need a refresher. The suggestions for similar exercises is also very helpful. I like how the app tells you what your workout volume equates to in a real life object like a helicopter or elephant!

A great tool for the gym. Without Hevy, I was relying on memory to track numbers of sets/reps/weight, and lacking real data for improvements. This app is easy to use and highly motivating for me, being able to track new records for volume or weights. Easy to setup workouts and super simple to track progress as I go. Love it!

Honestly so easy to use and intuitive. I feel excited to get back into lifting as this takes a lot of the mental effort out of it lol. Love looking at the little body map to see what muscle groups I should work next for balance. I would love if you could change the sizes of the widgets, finish workouts without entering sets/reps, and if there were pelvic floor/womens health exercises added as well as niche things like foot strength, finger strength. Overall really great!

an amazing app for workouts. provides visual examples of the workouts for proper form, and allows for some different forms of excercise like failure sets as well as notes so I can remember alterations I did in a previous week. it also connects to my watch and keeps track of my heart rate. sharing my workout with a workout group im in also helps clarify what we are working on that day! its also very good for what is offered for free!

I love this app. The free version has plenty of options for me (just starting out) and its been so helpful with keeping me motivated. I recommend this app to everyone I know who is going to the gym. The only issue I've had is my settings not saving for my workouts. It honestly might be user error since I'm still finding my way around the app. I was able to download and use immediately with the built in workouts and that has been instrumental for me.

I really like this app, I started working out with a group of friends and this app lets us all participate and be present for each other in our workouts. It's great for finding exercises based on muscle group as well. I think this app will be a great help as I am starting to workout by myself, would recommend this to anyone in similar situations

Love the Hevy app! It’s super easy to use, tracks your previous weights, and gives you those fun alert medals when you lift your heaviest or hit your longest hike which is super motivating. I also love that I can create workouts for friends and family to follow on their own. Makes training more fun, more social, and way easier to stay consistent. Highly recommend!

First review I've ever written for any app. This app is incredible. Well thought out. Perfect for tracking workout progress. No annoying in-app ads for free version- (which actually makes me more inclined to consider purchasing the "Pro" mode to unlock full features). Glitch-free. Very user friendly. This is exactly the app I was looking for and has become an essential tool in my workout program. Zero cons to this app. Great job to the team at Hevy. Keep up the excellent work.

I use this app to track my workouts on my functional trainer with a barbell. It has access to many different exercises. It's just good to keep track of what you've done, what your personal best is, and just keep track of working out. It's a nice app, very user friendly, and has some videos that show the actual exercise, so that's pretty cool.

I am a beginner weightlifter but knowledgeable of the weights. just haven't weightlifted in a while. I had used a couple of other apps but they were designed more for Sirius bro type weightlifters. when I found Hevy, it provided me with the platform to track my workouts and easily swap out exercises. I'm using the free app and it's truly incredible, but I'm thinking about upgrading in the next month or so. from a technical perspective, no glitches, no issues with the app! Love it

This is the best workout app I've used, and it beats my spreadsheets. I can track my workouts well with it and it gamifies it by giving me a "total weight lifted" for the workout and for the workouts that week. This just makes me feel like a rockstar, and pushes me to work harder. Wrapping up an arm day that lifted 3.5 tons makes me feel like a monster. I'm currently using the free version, and that is working well for me, but will probably go to paid when my next workout program starts.

I've used dozens of fitness apps over the years - and Hevy is far and away the best there is. The interface is very mobile friendly, the access to demo videos is a great feature - but my favorite is the memory that displays your last workout. I cannot imagine even one additional feature they could include to make it better.

Great app for tracking your workouts, don't bother installing the companion app on your watch though because the watch version is straight trash clearly programmed by a 3 year old. Not only that, if you're trying to track your workouts using Samsung Health on your watch the Hevy app forces it to stop tracking randomly because it's (Hevy is) such a piece of junk.

The Hevy app is nice! It is a great app for logging and tracking your workouts. I use the free version and it's good. I would like more options for bodyweight / calisthenics / resistance bands options for tracking. If you have that feeling of needing to report or log workouts to see your progress then definitely give the Hevy app a try.

Really like the app, the tracking and progression monitoring has been game changing. I have recommended it to many ppl. While the exercise library is extensive, its missing some common movements. However, you can add a customs that get added to ur personal library. the cons are minimal but mainly consist around random ui errors, like closing out an edited workout session and only getting half of the option to update or keep appears on screen or a formatting limitations when adding custom img.

Love this app for tracking my workouts. It has everything I need all in one place. Not only can I track my workouts, but I can also send them to others when planning a group workout. The sample workouts are great, as are the animated tutorials for newbies like me. I will definitely recommend this to others.

really great app to track progress and log workouts and measurements. I'm part of a fitness group on FB and already got about 8-10 people to sign up and get the premium subscription too so we can keep track of ourselves and show off our progress to each other. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that goes to the gym.

I love this app. I'm new to the gym and it's really helped me build a consistent routine & track the metrics that matter to me so I can see my progress every time, & adjust sets reps and exercises on the fly. I also value the small animations of each exercise in case I have form questions & the muscle heat map to show what I've worked in each set and over the course of the workout and week. The comparison of my total weight lifted to a heavy object like a truck or a yacht is awesome!! LOVE IT!

this is my go-to app for tracking all of my workouts. It keeps my rest periods consistent and I can implement progressive overload because it always shows how much I lift it the time before. for anyone. just getting started or who is currently a lifter. this is a great app to track your workouts. if only they add a circuit or tabata timer, then we'll be in business.

The app is pretty great, I like how it lets you compare your workouts over time. I wish my optional post workout summary photos would always give me the star chart that measures the muscle groups. 5 stars if you guys could make it a permanent choice. Other than that, the app is pretty great.

Really enjoying Hevy. I’ve completed about 25 workouts and have seen solid strength gains. I like how it suggests weights and reps based on past workouts—it pushes progress without overdoing it. The PR tracking is motivating (volume and max weight). Logging feels smooth, the rest timer keeps me on track, and workout time estimates are accurate. I upgraded to the lifetime version after about two weeks and have been happy with it.

As an advanced lifter doing this solo,meaning no trainer, this app helps tremendously with tracking and organizing my lifts. I love the rest timer aspect to keep me from exceeding my resting periods!!! 10000% recommend if you want to track/log your workouts. bonus : it tells you the total weight you lifted for the day!! fun!!

I bought the 6-month Premium plan, but requested a refund the same day after trying the app. The workouts felt unrealistic, too many exercises in too little time, constantly moving across the gym, and no clear structure. Even the custom workout generated random movements instead of a balanced plan. I wouldn't recommend paying for a long-term subscription. Try one month first to see if it fits your needs.

it's alright. app works as intended and has good workouts. i forget easily so i turned on push-notifications but I'm unable to change what time i get them, is it really so popular to workout before dinner? I'm always busy with things around the house so it's useless at this time in the end but there's no place I could find to change it. I'm also finding that the workouts given to me daily are 80% stretching and not actually working out, even after clicking the option that I am not satisfied.

Definitely read the reviews before purchasing! I felt all of the reviews were quite accurate and the app is great or horrible depending on the user. In most cases so is working out. Working out and finding the right resources to make it easier is complicated. This app is a bit funny in how it shows its interface. A woman discusses kegal exercises and another person discuss the illegitimate use of male body builders. Use for pre-workout or for someone who doesn't know how/where to start !

After subscribing to this app, I purchased a Samsung Galaxy Tab S10+ so I could use the app on larger screen. There is no way to log into the app using the subscription I already paid for. It requires me to go through the setup process again, only to get to a point where it tells me that I already have a subscription. But then offers no way for me to get into that subscription. Extremely disappointed that the exercise animations are just as tiny on a tablet as they are on the cell phone.

I've been using the for a few weeks now, and it's been a game-changer for my fitness routine. The app offers a wide range of workout plans, from beginner to advanced, making it suitable for all levels. The exercise demonstrations are clear and easy to follow, ensuring I maintain proper form. Instructions are great (video, written, including common mistakes), no complaints there. The multiple built in monthly plans is great for a beginner to just pick one and go. Love the app and it's easy to use interface! I also enjoy the ability to customize (most) of the workout plans to your preference! My main gripes: The "Full body workout" isn't really full body, but mix it up with the lower body plan and you can cover it all. The most annoying aspect is the fake encouragement they give you in the latest update. It doesn't sound authentic and is more a distraction than an encouraging. I need a way to shut that off without killing voice prompts.

This app is amazing! when I told it that the workouts were too hard the next day the workouts it gave me were just right! not to mention the ads are there but minimal, and the adjustments and tils it gives you really do work! it's like it can see you while you work out, pretty much every single workout felt like more of a challenge when applied. I would definitely recommend downloading as this is only my second day using it, it's definitely a must have if you want to get in shape.

I love this app so much!!! I was looking for a free at home workout and this is super simple, it's my dream app I was looking for. I love all the different challenges you can choose from and that you can also create your own (which was my main thing I was looking for!) I highly recommend!

playStoreReviews.text: This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

Looked at several much more expensive apps and just could not justify the price tag. Thought I would give these guys a try since the premium was so affordable. Best. Decision. Ever. Has seriously helped me up my game and reach my goals. Too many positives to list here, but seriously, just try the free version and if you like it, drop the $25 for a year. I bet you won't regret it.

I've tried a lot of different workout logging apps and this one has quickly become my favorite. Very easy to use at the gym and the api and web interfaces make it easy to plan workouts. One thing I would love is a widget that would let me check off a set and see my rest timer for my active workout. Not sure how hard that would be, but it would be convenient for me. Thanks for a great app!

I was using AI for a while for my workout tracking, but it easily gets clogged with all the data and information being input over the course of weeks and months. Hevy addresses exactly what I need with logging weight and exercises, and it has a rich database of exercises. It would be nice to have the ability to complete my sets for exercises from the lock screen (Android), without having to buy a smartwatch.

I appreciate how easy it is to set up how I want to work out. I did a paper journal before and this is WAY better! I also appreciate (as someone new to strength training), how each exercise has a gif of how to do it properly and what muscle groups it is actually working. I paid for an annual membership, but I am so impressed that I plan to switch it to the lifetime option! This will ensure that I always have my past data to compare to.

Hevy is by far the best workout app I've used. It's easy for beginners and experienced lifters alike. Every exercise includes helpful descriptions, making it simple to learn proper form. The app is clean, easy to use, and the premium version is very affordable. Absolutely worth downloading! Love this app!!!

Usually really great. A wide variety of exercises and good "how to" instructions. However, every time I use the app for an extended period of time (such as when I am tracking a workout) I lose the ability to click on anything within the app. I can scroll through, but cannot tap the checkmark to complete the set, or tap the three dots to reorder my workout. The only way I have been able to fix it is by closing out the app. I have had to do it multiple times during a single 1 hour workout.

I really love the primary features of this app. I've been doing a recommended full body split with a few mods to swap out exercises I don't like. it's super easy to switch things up when machines are occupied or I'm in a different gym. I also really like the calculator. Occasionally, syncing with my Pixel watch gets weird, but that might not be the app's fault. I tried one of the AI recommendations, but I didn't find much use for it. The basics are great!

I have never been so consistent and so motivated to track my workouts! I love to track my personal records and be able to push myself. 💪 So many features, even in the unpaid version. Some of the math goes a little funny when you try to share it to Instagram stories (one rep max frequently doesn't make sense), and I do wish it had more calisthenic type exercises listed. But for free, it's an incredible app.

Almost perfect! So honestly this is a great app. very easy to navigate and use. as someone who's just starting the gym, it made it a breeze to log it. I also love how when I launch a workout with the app on my phone, it syncs it with my watch for heart rate and better calorie burn calculation. my only minor issue is I wish they would update the workout and keep adding more as if im doing a specific exercise and they dont have that one, I have to go for the closest similar one to track it

Great app! I highly recommend it. I became more consistent because of many reasons, like the muscle chart which shows which muscles I worked out over the week, and logging is pretty seamless. It's easy to replace an exercise in your routine while you're working out if a machine isn't available. Also, it displays your PRs and the amount of weight you lifted in a session, which is encouraging. I'm very impressed with the team. They're pretty active on Reddit too, constantly seeking feedback.

Decent app. Wish there were filters to follow people based on age, country, etc. Tough to find people at my age and fitness level otherwise. The profile comparison feature is nice, but limited to only barbbeĺl exercises which is a poor design choice. Statistics are fairly thorough but a tad clunky IMO. Restricted to four saved routines for free version (understandable) is a tad limiting. Very responsive team on the bug reports. Overall, a good fitness tracker.

Love the fact it works on my watch. Not having to bring my phone around the gym is really convenient. Sometimes it doesn't sync correctly. Not sure which product in the Google chain is responsible for adding the information to Google Health (formerly Fit) but more information would be nice. Finally, it is REALLY annoying to scroll through minutes and seconds to enter time. There needs to be a way to type time in. This was the first gym training app and I won't switch to anything else.

Great tool for keeping track of your routines, easily edit and add what you want per week or if your feeling a little gassed out removing sets and exercises is really easy but keeps your routine the same for thr next time if you want. I appreciate the alerts too after rest times are up so if your caught day dreaming to a song it gets yah back on track. going to buy the lifetime membership once my 3 month tracking runs up, just so I can keep the progress logged long term.

Best free app for tracking workouts and measuring progress. Used a different app for a while until they started locking exercises unless you paid for a premium account. This app has a huge library of exercises with a bunch of different variations and anything that is missing you can add yourself as a custom exercise.

Tried to create a routine and the moment I had more than a couple exercises in it I had to start saving and updating the routine after adding an exercise. Then going back in and editing it to be able to add sets or more exercises all the buttons in the UI of the routine would not let me add more sets or exercises after adding one or the other. Glad I didn't go with Pro and waste money on it.

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

Great app for tracking your workouts and inexpensive compared to other apps (You pay the same amount for lifetime access as you would for a year of other apps). Granted, this app does not create workouts for you... but, in my mind, ID rather go to a free AI product and create a workout there and pay less here than pay a stupid amount of money to have the apps AI make something up. Plus, this way I have a lot more control over the final workout. Definitely worth a download to check it out.

Really great app. The free version has literally everything I need to track my workouts. I love how easy it is to use. I can swap exercises in my program with a couple taps. I love the exercise library and pre-con programs. There's also a plate math calculator!! And you can customize it!! No ads, no bloat, just a great tool. The social aspect is an added bonus! Don't sleep on this app.

I love this app for tracking my workouts! It's so easy to use and customize. I love that it syncs with Strava, too, since the cardio tracking isn't quite as good as the weightlifting tracking. It's super fun receiving medals for progress! It provides helpful info on correct form and has pretty much every exercise you could want. If you have a lot of different routines, you'd need to upgrade, but I'm perfectly fine with the 4 free ones.

Absolutely love this app. I love that it includes animations about how to do specific workouts, and I love all the growth tracking features. I feel like I've made more progress in the last two months with this app than I have in years without it! There is also a certain "game" element that I enjoy, in that I am constantly trying to beat my own records. It also contains tons of premade exercise routines. I never pay for apps, but this one was worth it.

I would like a larger variety of exercises that don't require equipment as much. I only have a bench and some adjustable weights. So I feel like my overall options are limited much like the space I have to work out. so far for having the app for a week I do find it convenient and easy to use and they don't try to price gouge you every month with barely anything to offer. 🧐👍

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. It can also seamlessly sync with Galaxy Watch. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

This app is great! it has everything you need to track a workout. logging exercises is easy, reviewing a graph of your past weight on each exercise is available to track progress. I use it for two separate routines at different gyms where the available weight machines are slightly different and I have to adjust the weight - Hevy reminds me of the previous weight I lifted on that routine which makes it easy for me. And, all that is on the free version!

I recommend to everyone I meet! This is user friendly and helps you with exercises if you don't know how to do them. I love you can add in your own exercises or equipment that is not in the app already. I barely tried the free trial and loved it, so I signed up for a year, but after that I may upgrade to lifetime! I love all the real life weight comparisons!

Honestly such a great app. I have been working out for literally years and have not gotten the progress I've had since I started using Hevy. It makes tracking so easy and the muscle distribution chart and heat map help me to see what areas I need to work on and what are getting overworked. If you're a gym goer, this is the app for you. Tell your friends and compare progress and exercises!!

I love everything about this app. It gives you data about what you do, never its opinion about whether you've gone too hard or not hard enough. It tracks your lifts over time, and celebrates your PRs. Thoughtful features include rest timers, a thorough list of lifts with written and video instructions for each one, and very minimal social feed. I show this app to all my gym buddies, because it's so useful without any unnecessary bells and whistles.

Very easy to use and has all the exercises I'll need. I use free version. That being said, the reason for 3 stars is that how the app measures some workouts should change. Lunges should include body weight in volume lifted. It would be nice if there was a record for total volume lifted for one exercise in a workout, not just for one single set. Sled push should include weight added, not just distance.

I have always logged my workouts for the sake of keeping track of my progress. The problem is that I use to write everything down - the old fashioned way, pen and paper (I know, I'm old). I decided to give this app a try for the sake of efficiency and convienence. Long story short, this app did not disappoint. It has everything in it I could ask for. I don't have a lot of experience in workout apps, but I know I have no need for a different one. Great job Hevy! - A consistent user

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

I have the payed version, I like the selection of equipment it matches my gym pretty good, the trainer work really good, rotates exercises and equipment so it doesn't get stale and also gi e you replacement options if there's something you don't like or if a piece of equipment isn't available, seeing the past logs and set ratings helps alot, only thing i don't like is it doesn't have some of the past history if I switch routines.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

Before I downloaded Hevy I found it so difficult to actually track my progress. This app organizes it so well and makes each is so simple to track your progress in individual exercises, muscle groups, etc. And building your own workouts as a beginner is also incredibly easy as it will filter exercises for your needs to help you get started. I've been using it for about 2 years now and have put on significantly more muscle since starting the app.

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

This app is AMAZING im the type of person that when I go to the gym I want to get in and get out. The fact that you can load your workout and check it off is amazing no more guessing or trying to remember what you did ITS ALL RIGHT THERE and the fact that its free is insane. So far I've lost 25lbs and I know this app had alot to do with it by keeping me on Track😉

Great app that can track a full routine for free. No ads so far, and the subscription is inexpensive if you want to track more then one routine. Adding workouts to the routine is simple and they have have a good selection of routines for everything from beginner to advanced workouts. Each workout also shows a simplified gif and explanation of how to do the workout.

Honestly this was the secret sauce to seeing real progress in the gym as I use the app to record every weight and rep and am able to increase it every or every other week by 2.5-5lbs. On top of it you're also able to organize and create entire workout routines for yourself. Would definitely recommend, even better if you have other gym buddies so that you can compete a bit with each other using its social media features

I used to track all my workouts using an actual physical journal. one of the influencers that I follow on Instagram recommended hevy I made the switch and I'll never go back. the free version allows you to create four routines that you can utilize and if you pay for the subscription you can use even more it's worth every penny but the free version is great for beginners or intermediate lifters.

Really good app for tracking workouts. It's easy to setup routines, find the right exercises, and track your progress, what muscles you've used, etc. Everything is easy to navigate and use. I've been using the free version but might upgrade just to support such a great app.

Love the free, no ad functionality, been using it for a week and im considering buying the premium (option for one time purchase is exceptional). App functionality as well as tracking is intuitive and simple, integration with other health tracking apps and wearable support is straightforward, overall an app built for the user, I wish more companies used this kind of business model, they'd make a lot more money

I've been using this for 4 months. I like that I can set my own routines and it updates with the weight/reps I did in my last session. It makes it easy to keep track of progress. The app also makes it really easy to replace an exercise with something similar when the machine/equipment may not be available.

Been using this app since ~2021 and it has been amazing overall. Likes: Tracks historical data, showing progress and strength growth. Allows for customized workout plans you can craft around what is available to you. Fun little ribbon-achievements every time you hit a new high weight per lift. (It's very motivating!) Lifetime pass is reasonably priced (and I highly recommend it). Dislikes: AI coaching plan that comes with premium could be more customizable. overall highly recommend this app.

whatever happened to being able to change the rest time, completing sets, or anything while you have a routine running is broken. it's so frustrating to try to check off a set but even after 10 taps it doesn't go through. I tried turning the rest time off in case it was some sort of conflict in timers or something but this main thread be going wild here

Absolutely love this app, it works great in helping you track your progress. The only true issue that I have with this app is that I copy my previous workouts for routine and not always do I complete each exercise due to time but keep them in for the next week.. that being said even if those exercises are not checked off per set, the app is still tracking those towards my volume lifted at the end thus not truly accurate in thw end. Would love to see this updated.

This app makes it super easy to keep track of your workouts, gives you a monthly summary, and is overall simple to use, and all using the free version. They also reverted back to manual time entry, which is so much easier than using the scrolling wheel, so that's an easy 5 stars from me.

Easy to use and great for beginners. I liked the preloaded routines to start and create my own routines in the app now that I'm more comfortable in the gym. I like the progress tracking graphs, it motivates me to see that I've gotten stronger. A lot of great features for a free app!

I was looking for a new weightlifting log app that had more than just the basics. I love the simplicity of the interface and the ability to discover new exercises. The user interface has intuitive options to quickly record your sets and add new exercises on the fly.

straight up love this app. the ease of tracking workouts is amazing. I've tried quite a few and nothing comes close. I love the ability to easily see the weights Ive used just recently. I've always had issues I get to the gym and have to start essentially anew. 100% worth the price.

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

Great app. The free version is good enough for most people, supporting up to 3 workout routine rotations. I decided to upgrade to the paid version to have the flexibility of having more routines. I love the tracking, the timing between sets, ability to do supersets (no breaks between 2 or more workouts) and the tracking of your best overall lifts. This app helped me gain 10 lb of muscle in 8 weeks. Current total gain is 15 lbs of muscle. Goal is 30 lb 💪

after using the free version for a couple of months I moved on to the Pro version and updated my review from 4 to 5 stars. for a few bucks a month, it's a no brainer. the app works very well and syncs seamlessly with my watch during workouts. I track 3 90-minute sessions a week with 32 sets over 8 exercises each session, mainly all with my watch. love the stats and reports as well. haven't found anything major to complain about.

Super simple and straight forward. I just wanted a way to track my workouts like a spreadsheet but needed the spreadsheet to be on steroids, and this is exactly that. I barely found out today that the app also has a way to track not only your weight but measurements of multiple body parts and appendages, like my neck, my back, my... you get the point.. it's super dope. I bought the lifetime membership. 100% worth it cause I can have all of my routines and variations of them stored in there.

The app is really good, makes updating and tracking your progress workout to workout easy. My only complaint is that I wish their was a way to create a routine from your current workout, currently you'd have to remember your weight and rep ranges, plot them all down in the tracker, and save it. would be very convenient to just have an option added to the drop-down menu during your current workout, other than that though Hevy is great.

This app is great. I was using the bodybuilding app and when they updated it last year, it completely ruined it and I was on the hunt for a better app. This app is the best one I've ever used. It tracks your progression, personal bests, best volume, literally anything. It even adds up the total weight lifted and will tell you if you have lifted a truck, dinosaur, yacht, and more. One thing I would love to see in the future is badges or trophies or anything like that.

I switched from Strong to Hevy at the behest of a friend who wanted to see my workouts and it's been a real upgrade! I even upgraded to the paid version when they had an end of year discount and haven't regretted it. I'm a real sucker for data visualization and it's been cool to see myself improving over time in graph form. Have only used it for a few months but so far it's been fantastic!

Really great workout app. A wide selection of exercises even if you don't have that much equipment at home, you can easily find some exercises for every muscle group. I believe the app recently improved supersets workouts. I love the scroll to the next exercise feature, especially useful with supersets,so I don't have to wonder around what is next.

I really like this app. I use the free version for about 30 days before I decided I wanted to upgrade to Pro. I think the free version would be great for someone who only works out at one gym, but I use multiple gems so this allowed me to create different routines for each gym and Unlimited Custom exercises. you have to dig into the settings a little bit to get your rest timer working properly, and just to get things the way you like them to be as far as reading your effort and things like that.

I don't write reviews for anything, except Hevy. It's the only gym tracker I've stuck with. It's easy to use, definitely worth the one time purchase for the additional workouts and custom exercises. My favorite is at the end of a workout seeing exactly how much I've lifted and what that compares to (like a T-Rex!) Thank you for making an app for the gym that isn't bloated or gamified, just gains! ❤️

I'll start by saying I really like this app and recommend it to all my friends and students. But I'm starting to get irked with the lack of integration with Garmin because I can't track my heart rate otherwise. So then I have to have 2 trackers running at once and monitor both. I'm also annoyed that exercises like Kettlebell Swings aren't accounted for in the muscle group breakdown. I feel like that's an easy fix. You give primary and secondary muscles for everything else. Why not these?

Great app. One small problem. I love this app. It makes tracking my workouts super easy and it plays well with my other fitness apps, however, the WearOS version drains my watch so fast. I don't know if it's tracking more than the Fitbit app does, but I can track a whole hour and a half workout with the Fitbit app, and still have enough battery to last the rest of the day, but with this app tracking my workouts, I'll get down to 15% and have to charge again before I go to work.

solid workout tracker. you can use an AI plug in to work out a progression routine. overall just like my old notebook and pen. except much easier to read. for just wanting to track your weights reps and volume can't beat this. I like this app so much I paid for pro Even though the free version is completely functional for all my use cases. keep up the good work!

I absolutely love this app. It's given me a way to keep track of my lifts, build out plans, doesn't constantly ask me for money, and the free version does everything you need it to. Can take notes about my workouts, can add in modifiers, can even make your own excersizes if you know how to import the right files. Wouldn't have the progress I've made so far without it. Easy win dog.

Love it. Setup routines ahead of time. Track reps, weights and sets as you go. Notes are visible right where you want them. The data from the previous workout is right on the screen so you can modify if needed. Rearrange or modify the routine on the fly if a station is not available or a different hit is wanted. Keep changes or discard them after. Very large exercise library as well the ability to add custom exercises. This is the perfect tool to use in the gym and when planning at home.

I've used the free version of this app, for about 4yrs now. It has a large base of exercises and workouts. Options range from Free weights, machine weights, kettlebells, resistance, body weight, and cardio. All options have a "How to" for reference. It allows you to track workout time, Reps, Sets, weight, failure/warmup, supersets, has a section for notes, and you can save/view all prior workouts! I LOVE IT and I would highly recommend!!

I've used a variety of workout tracking apps, and this is the best one I've used. I've been using this app exclusively to track my workouts for over a year now. It's worth paying for the upgrade a you can use to customize and build multiple routines. It keeps track of all of my workouts for every type of exercise and allows me to track the weight, sets, reps, and volume since I began using the app. I really like how customizable and easy it is to adjust my plans.

Had the app for 3 weeks and I'm very impressed with it. It's what I thought I would get with Zepp and the new Fitbit interface, but they failed miserably. Heavy allowed me to build my own routines for each day and then it auto fills your previous weights and reps each exercise so you aren't having to keep a log in a notebook. That allows me to push my limits and get stronger and grow my muscles. Highly recommended!

really love the app and the wide variety of exercises to choose from when creating my workouts. the one thing that would move this to a 5 star would be the ability to have more than three workouts saved. it's not a huge deal but I'd love to be able to see older programs and know what I did so I can modify new programs. aside from that it's a great app

lots of exercises to choose from or create your own. let's you create up to 4 individual workouts for free version. being able to add notes to the exercise, set an automatic timer. and reflect at the end of your workout are great features. If you are new to workouts, use the reflect feature and be honest about your struggles, then come back to them after a few weeks of working out and compare yourself to that former person.

I have been using the Hevy app for over a year to track my progress lifting weights. I find it very helpful with planning routines, selecting equipment and exercises, as well as daily tracking completion. It helps me remember my routines and keeps track of previous accomplishments. The reports and statistic are also useful to evaluate progress.

I'm a week/ 3 workouts into the free version of Hevy. The best part for me so far is the motivation of seeing other people showing up! If they can do it, I can too... it's fairly intuitive, I was able to set up my first weight lifting workout without a problem, and u can log other things like walking, yoga, skiing... I'm very happy so far. editing to add I've made it to 50 workouts and paid for a lifetime subscription! nothing but great things to say about Hevy app

worth trying if you need a free app. will allow up to 4 routines and pro is unlimited. 3 months of data stored for free and unlimited for pro. tbh, simple, intuitive and allows synchronization of health connect and other apps that track health. definitely enjoying the free and most likely will purchase the lifetime option for the pro, but like I said free is pretty awesome too. btw, speaking as a beginner lifter

I really like using this app for my weight focused workouts. It keeps track of all my stats and the little how to videos for each exercise are great for when I need a refresher. The suggestions for similar exercises is also very helpful. I like how the app tells you what your workout volume equates to in a real life object like a helicopter or elephant!

A great tool for the gym. Without Hevy, I was relying on memory to track numbers of sets/reps/weight, and lacking real data for improvements. This app is easy to use and highly motivating for me, being able to track new records for volume or weights. Easy to setup workouts and super simple to track progress as I go. Love it!

Honestly so easy to use and intuitive. I feel excited to get back into lifting as this takes a lot of the mental effort out of it lol. Love looking at the little body map to see what muscle groups I should work next for balance. I would love if you could change the sizes of the widgets, finish workouts without entering sets/reps, and if there were pelvic floor/womens health exercises added as well as niche things like foot strength, finger strength. Overall really great!

an amazing app for workouts. provides visual examples of the workouts for proper form, and allows for some different forms of excercise like failure sets as well as notes so I can remember alterations I did in a previous week. it also connects to my watch and keeps track of my heart rate. sharing my workout with a workout group im in also helps clarify what we are working on that day! its also very good for what is offered for free!

I love this app. The free version has plenty of options for me (just starting out) and its been so helpful with keeping me motivated. I recommend this app to everyone I know who is going to the gym. The only issue I've had is my settings not saving for my workouts. It honestly might be user error since I'm still finding my way around the app. I was able to download and use immediately with the built in workouts and that has been instrumental for me.
