I really like this app, I started working out with a group of friends and this app lets us all participate and be present for each other in our workouts. It's great for finding exercises based on muscle group as well. I think this app will be a great help as I am starting to workout by myself, would recommend this to anyone in similar situations

Love the Hevy app! It’s super easy to use, tracks your previous weights, and gives you those fun alert medals when you lift your heaviest or hit your longest hike which is super motivating. I also love that I can create workouts for friends and family to follow on their own. Makes training more fun, more social, and way easier to stay consistent. Highly recommend!

First review I've ever written for any app. This app is incredible. Well thought out. Perfect for tracking workout progress. No annoying in-app ads for free version- (which actually makes me more inclined to consider purchasing the "Pro" mode to unlock full features). Glitch-free. Very user friendly. This is exactly the app I was looking for and has become an essential tool in my workout program. Zero cons to this app. Great job to the team at Hevy. Keep up the excellent work.

I use this app to track my workouts on my functional trainer with a barbell. It has access to many different exercises. It's just good to keep track of what you've done, what your personal best is, and just keep track of working out. It's a nice app, very user friendly, and has some videos that show the actual exercise, so that's pretty cool.

I am a beginner weightlifter but knowledgeable of the weights. just haven't weightlifted in a while. I had used a couple of other apps but they were designed more for Sirius bro type weightlifters. when I found Hevy, it provided me with the platform to track my workouts and easily swap out exercises. I'm using the free app and it's truly incredible, but I'm thinking about upgrading in the next month or so. from a technical perspective, no glitches, no issues with the app! Love it

This is the best workout app I've used, and it beats my spreadsheets. I can track my workouts well with it and it gamifies it by giving me a "total weight lifted" for the workout and for the workouts that week. This just makes me feel like a rockstar, and pushes me to work harder. Wrapping up an arm day that lifted 3.5 tons makes me feel like a monster. I'm currently using the free version, and that is working well for me, but will probably go to paid when my next workout program starts.

I've used dozens of fitness apps over the years - and Hevy is far and away the best there is. The interface is very mobile friendly, the access to demo videos is a great feature - but my favorite is the memory that displays your last workout. I cannot imagine even one additional feature they could include to make it better.

Great app for tracking your workouts, don't bother installing the companion app on your watch though because the watch version is straight trash clearly programmed by a 3 year old. Not only that, if you're trying to track your workouts using Samsung Health on your watch the Hevy app forces it to stop tracking randomly because it's (Hevy is) such a piece of junk.

The Hevy app is nice! It is a great app for logging and tracking your workouts. I use the free version and it's good. I would like more options for bodyweight / calisthenics / resistance bands options for tracking. If you have that feeling of needing to report or log workouts to see your progress then definitely give the Hevy app a try.

Really like the app, the tracking and progression monitoring has been game changing. I have recommended it to many ppl. While the exercise library is extensive, its missing some common movements. However, you can add a customs that get added to ur personal library. the cons are minimal but mainly consist around random ui errors, like closing out an edited workout session and only getting half of the option to update or keep appears on screen or a formatting limitations when adding custom img.

Love this app for tracking my workouts. It has everything I need all in one place. Not only can I track my workouts, but I can also send them to others when planning a group workout. The sample workouts are great, as are the animated tutorials for newbies like me. I will definitely recommend this to others.

really great app to track progress and log workouts and measurements. I'm part of a fitness group on FB and already got about 8-10 people to sign up and get the premium subscription too so we can keep track of ourselves and show off our progress to each other. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that goes to the gym.

I love this app. I'm new to the gym and it's really helped me build a consistent routine & track the metrics that matter to me so I can see my progress every time, & adjust sets reps and exercises on the fly. I also value the small animations of each exercise in case I have form questions & the muscle heat map to show what I've worked in each set and over the course of the workout and week. The comparison of my total weight lifted to a heavy object like a truck or a yacht is awesome!! LOVE IT!

this is my go-to app for tracking all of my workouts. It keeps my rest periods consistent and I can implement progressive overload because it always shows how much I lift it the time before. for anyone. just getting started or who is currently a lifter. this is a great app to track your workouts. if only they add a circuit or tabata timer, then we'll be in business.

The app is pretty great, I like how it lets you compare your workouts over time. I wish my optional post workout summary photos would always give me the star chart that measures the muscle groups. 5 stars if you guys could make it a permanent choice. Other than that, the app is pretty great.

Really enjoying Hevy. I’ve completed about 25 workouts and have seen solid strength gains. I like how it suggests weights and reps based on past workouts—it pushes progress without overdoing it. The PR tracking is motivating (volume and max weight). Logging feels smooth, the rest timer keeps me on track, and workout time estimates are accurate. I upgraded to the lifetime version after about two weeks and have been happy with it.

As an advanced lifter doing this solo,meaning no trainer, this app helps tremendously with tracking and organizing my lifts. I love the rest timer aspect to keep me from exceeding my resting periods!!! 10000% recommend if you want to track/log your workouts. bonus : it tells you the total weight you lifted for the day!! fun!!

It's hard to commit long-term to something like an exercise program, but I've been using the sweat app since 2017, and it's helped me spend these past 9 years consistently working out when I never did before. I've spent stretches obsessed with yoga, obsessed with dumbbells and resistance bands at home, and committed to lifting heavy at the gym, and no matter what I'm interested in, there's a program for it. there's dozens of options to choose from, and they're always adding programs as well.

I like the sweat app and the variety of workouts. I often get bored of the same workout format, so as to not lose motivation I change my program up or find on demand workouts. I find it annoying that I can't see sample workouts when I am browsing the programs. To see if the workouts are something that I would enjoy, I have to set it as my program and then go in and view the first week's workouts. Please add sample workouts as the descriptions are quite vague.

I've worked with a personal trainer on and off for 4 yrs now and I usually do my own thing during the off moments. Thought i'd give this a try even though i've been skeptical of apps... the PWR is great! It's just like having her right there. The timers, the workouts, keeping track of your progress and what's left. I just use it for workouts but the recipes look really good, too

I've used the app for a month so far and I love it. The only thing I would change would being able to track the amount of weight used & to be able to skip around in the workouts. Once you start, you have to do the workout in order and sometimes the equipment isnt available to do so. I'm currently doing PWR and I love it. I do wish there were modification options to some of the exercises (leg press?!). Also the new update has made it difficult to hear the trainer cues. Overall it's worth the $$!

I've been using this app for the past 3 years and I love it! I appreciate the amount of user feedback they incorporate into regular updates. I still find that the app crashes fairly regularly (about 1/5 uses) but it's not a huge deal. One thing I would love to see.. with the new weight tracking for each exercise is that the weights would automatically populate the next time you do that specific exercise so you could know where to start with pyramids etc. I refer to a notes doc to check this.

This app is amazing. When you purchase a subscription you gain access to a half dozen different training and a number of different workout programs and styles. There are also multiple home workout options. Buying the yearly subscription is excellent value, given all the options. I also love that there are rigorous programs, which tell you exactly what to do depending on your goals. All you have to do is open the app and do what it says.

Great app bad customer service. Okay, love the app. I have been using it for at least 2 years. My beef is with their customer service. There is no direct number to call to speak to a rep about managing your account subscription. I have tried many, many, many times to email them about cancelling my subscription form apple pay to Google pay to no avail because I no longer have an Apple account/phone. I'm so sick of trying to explain why i can't do it and still they refuse to listen to my problem.

Review for Android users: when the app works, it's great. However I've had multiple instances where the app just will not open for whatever reason for days at a time. Not because of an update and this isn't because of my phone. Super frustrating when you have to opt out for other workouts for days at a time until the app decides to work again. Installing and reinstalling the app doesn't help at all either. I've reached out to support and they don't really help/have answers.

This app has been extremely helpful with being consistent with exercise. I'm often on a regular program so it has set workouts each week, varying in difficulty. I love that I can also choose express workouts or one from another program depending on where I am, or what equipment I have on hand. Each exercise has a detailed description of how to complete it. I also like that I can log workouts, and I now use the app as a sort of health tracker to hold myself accountable. Highly recommend!

Love this App. I was looking for one on nutrition and came across this app. Let me tell you it has helped me loose 10 ibs in 3 weeks. Amazing support and trainers. You choose a program that works for you. My first trainer wasn't my style and after a week I knew I had to change. So I found a trainer for my taste. Either way they make it easier on you to find what you need. Ohh and the meals amazing ideas. It serves 1 but double and you serve 2. There are apps to help count the fats and caleries.

I love this app and the trainers. I have been using it for almost four years. It would be AWESOME if you could make it so that if you forget to say you completed a workout one day you can still go back and say "completed on this day". Right now you are limited to only adding workouts to the in app calendar on the current day. So for example, if I completed LISS on Wednesday but forgot to add it, I can't go back and say I did it on Wednesday. PLEASE fix this it has been bugging me forever!

The app allows you to follow only one official program at once. Granted you can add in a limited number of workouts from other programs from "on demand" but you can follow only one complete program at a time. I would like to be able to follow more than one complete 12 week program... for instance the PWR AND Yoga. And I would like to be able to save recipes! Some are realllly good and you can *star them but you still have to search for them each time.

Pretty good! I like all the workouts but it'll freeze occasionally and I have to uninstall and reinstall to get it to work again. Also, I really wish I could backlog workouts if I forget to document it. I'd like if we could pick low or high intensity workout. Sometimes I have hard days at work and I'm tired or if it's late and I live on the top floor apartment it'd be nice to choose. And opposite, if I'm high energy one day and can withstand something more intense, it'd be nice to choose.

Love the workouts! This app has helped me so much to improve my fitness. My only complaint is that I can't connect my fitbit to it to track my steps. I wish they would let you use a fitbit instead of only allowing Google Fit. Otherwise, I have no complaints, I've lost 20 lbs with the help of this app and improving my diet. The hiit workouts are great and I've noticed such a change in physique and muscle gain and haven't had to "lift heavy" to do so. I've mostly done it all with 15 lb dumbbells!

I have had the sweat app since 2019 and although I don't always stick to it, I think it is a great deal considering the cost of a gym membership. I do wish there was a feature that would allow all program workouts to have a shorter ~20 minute version. The biggest obstacle for me sticking to the programs is the time commitment. most workouts take 45min - 1hr to complete from warmup to cool down. I think shorter workouts would reduce the barrier to entry for so many people.

This app has potential but I feel it alienates some groups by lacking more detailed search options. It would be nice to see previews is the programs and what types of workouts are included. I have a foot injury and need to sub for low-impact. Unfortunately, there's no way to know what's in the programs until you start one. You also can't adjust program start dates. The app assumes Sunday as a rest day when Sundays are my longest workout days.

I love this app. I have been using different programs over the past few months and have gone from being a relative couch potato to looking forward to work out. I also use the on demand section to schedule other types of workouts than the program I am currently on. I don't really use the food section because a lot of the ingredients are not in my grocery store and I don't want to cook a totally different meal for every single meal. My only complaint is that you cannot connect to Garmin yet.

I've been using this app for about six years (back when it had a less socially acceptable name), and it's really great. I've bounced between gym classes, small group training and personal training, and I keep coming back to the app because it's so affordable and the workout routines are great. I use the on demand workouts whenever I have less time or dont have access to a gym. 100% worth it.

I think this app is great for people who have little workout experience or dont feel comfortable working out at a gym. I personally have a lot of experience in the gym and am in pretty decent shape - for me and people similar, it's not the best. I originally signed up because I'm in medical school and dont have time to write my own workouts, so this took the stress off for me, however, some of the workouts are a bit too easy even if I lift my heaviest (I end up modifying the workouts sometimes to make them tougher). I also dont like that there is only 1 leg day and 2 upper body days. For my body type I need a lot of legs - at least 2 times a week, so I have to add in my own workouts for the results I wanna see. But for beginners and people just starting out - go for it.

This app is low quality, and very limited. The workouts are basic, and a bit boring. My steps do not connect so every day it says I've taken 0000 steps when I live a very active lifestyle. It does not pick up on a working you are doing and while it has a way to add a workout manually it won't be able to "calculate" this into your progress. The app is very basic, there is little to no coaching on form, no coach interaction, and repetitive workouts. For how expensive the app is, I am disappointed

$20 a month is like the price of a single yoga class. And you get 12 dozens of weekly workouts, a robust tracking app, plus a community of like-minded ladies. It's been instrumental in staying fit the busier I get. At this point, I know I can commit to the annual subscription (which is even more affordable than the monthly plan). Love this app!

Good But Too Expensive I enjoyed the workouts and convenience of the app but just couldn't justify spending twice the cost of my gym. I would rather choose 1 program and pay $20 per YEAR. The meal plans is too difficult to be useful. Every meal of every day was a different recipe with no week-long meal prep option. If this was the only fitness item a person paid for then it might be worth it. For people that go to a gym and other active classes its too much. Also, too many ads for the $.

Despite what the app admins say, it's very disappointing to see that a form of payment is even needed for a free 7-day trial. What they should do also or instead is have an option that has a limit to what programs are visible and not accessible until after you sign up for a membership. What it looks like they are preying on with this current tactic is that you'll forget to cancel right away and bam instant hit. This isn't friendly.

this app is great to cover a good quick workout. If you're looking for something that involves a bunch of cardio this isn't for you. you can build in cardio but this app is primarily for a quick 28 minute work out focused on arms, legs and abs. Just like the name of the app, you will sweat and is aimed at all abilities. would highly recommend.

signed up for 7 day free trail, only worked for one day, tryed using the app other days in that week it never worked also took forever to load. gave up waiting on for it to load any workouts. never tried using it again till I ended my subscription the day BEFORE trial ended.. which took very long. But concerned because I got a message saying that I will be charged 19.99 on phone bill! I don't understand why, I cancelled my sub. didn't use it, and the price is very high .. for it be not worth it.

I love this app. I've been using it since 2018. The only complaint that I have is that when doing the Pilates program, when switching, from left to right in some parts, it doesn't say to switch, I end up working out one side, and then it switches to the next session then I have to go back.When resting, it would be nice that it would say what equipment to use for the next session. Usually, I'm rushing to put on the band and pause the session or rewind it. Hopefully, this could be fixed.

I'm not crazy about this app. The BBG workout seems like it would be effective but the way the app is set up is hard to follow and you have to manually click through each move in both the warm up & cool down as well as the workout itself. It also has a cardio workout on the calander but dosen't give you a cardio workout to follow along with. I'm not sure if there is something I'm missing or a way to change some of the settings but right now it is very frustrating to use.

I love this exercise app, worth the money!! Its easy to use and follow, the exercises always leave me feeling like I got in a good workout and it keeps track of my progress. I really like it. I dont use it for the meal/food portion but it has a section for that if you want...it doesn't appear to have any nutritional info on the suggested meal plan items tho.

These workouts will kick. your. ass. into shape. For me, this is definitely worth the money because if I have something put into it, I'll be more motivated to get everything out of it that I can by sticking with the workouts. My one criterique is that I can't link the Sweat app to the Fitbit app, which tracks more accurately than the google fit app that's linked. I haven't gotten into the recipes much, but I'm excited to check those out.

Love this app! Have been using it for over a year and got amazing results from it! Everytime there is an update, it is better than before! Love that there are so many different trainers with different styles to choose from and that you can still use the other programs on days you want to change things up. Only thing I would like is if you were able to record ALL weight amounts, adjust reps or sets if necessary, & to be able to look back at what they were on following weeks.

Overall, pretty darn good, but needs WAY more verbal cues on timer based exercises, (halfway there, keep going, 1min left, you got this, 10 seconds, 3,2,1,etc.). Check out sworkit if you like more audio cues. Hopefully this team focuses on the audio instruction for there next build. That would really elevate the experience. It's annoying that you have to "click through" the routines manually, but I get that every user goes at there own pace. I also think the exercises should start automatically after breaks. There is room for improvement, but the app is incredibly convenient and really well done. I have faith this will keep getting better overtime.

Immediately after signing up you are required to pick a plan. Either you get 50% off by paying for the year upfront or you can have a 7 day trial and then pay $20/month after that. I can't do anything until I pick one. I'm more than happy to pay the yearly price upfront once I check it out but I'm not ok with losing that discount because I want to check it out first. Apply the 7 day trial to the yearly option too if you want people to pay.

Hi, Love the app! I use it all the time and it makes things a lot easier by just giving me set work outs. I like that there are videos to follow as you go along the work outs in real time. I am a visual learner, so it's nice not having to pause the workout to figure it out in a slow video tutorial like other apps. I also like not having to constantly obsess over what I'm going to work out and what exercises to do that day, it takes a lot of stress and anxiety off of your shoulders.

I debated long and hard about taking the plunge to pay for this app, but I am SO glad I did. The workouts are challenging but doable and varied enough to keep me interested, and I'm super impressed with the interface and the overall app functionality. It gets a bit laggy on my gym's lackluster Wi-Fi, but it's not a big deal. I couldn't recommend it more highly. Edit: more than 2 years later, still loving the app! It's absolutely transformed my fitness journey!

Close but not flexibility. Love this app but it's annoying that you cant swap out workouts for a different category if you are sore in a certain spot . You can't even go out of order if the equipment you want is taken at the gym . I wish they could fix this and also let you add on for days you are trying to hit a little something extra.

BE WARNED: THIS IS NOT FREE. I'm okay with subscriptions/in app purchases but how am I supposed to try out the app with a "7 day free trial" which afterwards, you pay monthly. I can't even see the app itself because YOU NEED to make an account, AND choose a plan. I wanted a free but limited app, so I could experience the basic features and decide whether or not to pay. You may defend this business practice and say that you can cancel anytime but that's not the point. I shouldn't feel pressured

I used this app back in 2019 and purchased the subscription again this year after 5 years of being away. It is surprising to me that even after 5 years the bugs are still the same! You could still not sync the app with your ANDROID watch which means to see your daily steps on the app you need to take extra step and download a 3rd party app. Second, when it is on "WALK" workout you need to stay on the timer screen،if the screen turns black for Battery usage، it stops counting steps & time

I'm loving this app! When I first was looking at the workouts, before I started, I was thinking "these are a little too easy...". Boy was I wrong! Nearly everything can be modified to make it easier or more challenging, if I want to make anything more challenging I just add a band or weights! It's only been a week and I'm already seeing results and down 2lbs!

$20/month to even log in, should have it clear in the description so people aren't wasting their time. I'm not subscribing to something I can't even see without giving you payment first. You keep replying to comments complaining about getting over charged or charged before the 7 day trial or suddenly their accounts are unrecognizable with "we don't have your email" or "sorry about your experience it free for 7 days as long you give us your card info beforehand. We strive to be the best".

Love this app! It challenges me to push myself while doing the Kayla Itsines workouts. I've been doing the BBG program on and off since college and since I took the plunge to purchase the app I've been more consistent than ever working out. Only 2 things I wish it had are: 1. Connecting to the Fitbit app. There is a way to connect through google but it would be so easy since I already have a Fitbit to link the two. 2. Less repetitive food for the program, but they do give great dietary options.

I love this app & variety of workouts offered! I don't like the challenge aspect. I hate that I can only pick one at a time. Some of them are only a few days a week. I like to alternate with other workouts. Doing more than one program at a time doesn't count towards completing the program. I would also change a workout with a timer. For instance, back to back moves that are 1 min long. The time between moves is not enough time to set up for the next move. I always have to pause it.

Love the workout plans, but the meal plans lack any nutritional information and are way too random! I should not have a shopping list 107 items long that includes things like "3/4 of a sweet potato" for 1 week of meals. you can have plenty of variety with half that many ingredients. Also, HAVE YOU NOTICED THE PANDEMIC? I'm not going to be in a grocery store long enough to buy over 100 individual items! Slightly frustrating that you can't scheduled workouts more than 1 week in advance

I love the workouts and the community! But the app is not very user friendly in a lot of ways. Can't sort comments on posts to see new first (you can do this on the web version but not the app), can't go back and see favorite recipes, progress pictures don't work all that well, etc. The trainers and videos are awesome, the only reason it doesn't get 5 stars is because the user interface needs some work.

I appreciate the workout program for what it is - a good way to build the core and lose weight on arms and legs, and it would have suited my goals perfectly just a few years ago. My workout goals since then have changed completely and I want to build strength. I'm keeping this app for a month because it has good plyometric exercises and I feel it's very good if you are looking for core strength but it won't suit my long-term needs unfortunately.

This app and the workouts within are great. First program to get me on a habit of being active. I enjoy the explanations of moves and ability to follow along. I wish you could log past workouts, especially rest days. Often forget to log rest days and the only option is to log them on the wrong day/week which makes the tracking history less accurate. Otherwise all around great app and programs.

Great app, good workouts that are tough, varied and the perfect length, like the programs that help keep you accountable throughout the week, like the Spotify integration. -1 star because the only step count tracker is Google Fit. I wish it integrated with others, I use Samsung Health. -1 star because I don't think it's a customer first practice to give you a better price if you buy the app before doing a free trial, not cool.

I really enjoy this app. I have had it for several years now and I use it almost everyday. The workouts are awesome, the planner is great and the recipes and tips are pretty cool as well. It is adjustable and I love that you can substitute certain exercise, and also choose from a variety of different instructors which help mix up your workout routine. Also a great community of women supporting women. I also really enjoy the challenges. 10/10 recommend this app for any woman looking to workout.

I just downlaoded this app, and im already a bit disappointed in the meal planning. The only dietary preferences are huge categories: standard, vegan, and so forth. But what if i dont like a specific meal that is chosen for me? Or what if the prep required for that meal isnt feasible for me at that time of day. The 8fit app gives suggestions, but if you dont like the suggested meal, you can access the recipe catalog and simply swap it for another one. i can hardly see myself using this feature.

Honestly this app has been out way to long to have all the issues it has and for how expensive it is. It should work without requiring internet, its a fitness app that just seems logical as many people go outside or to gyms without access to web. cant track your improvement by weight and measurements. Only pictures. The dashboard is poorly done. syncs to calendar by manually adding each item. why? the help center doesn't know how to fix problems. i could go on but i don't have enough space

I enjoyed the post pregnancy from Kelsey Wells in the past. I recently tried the Power program from Kelsey, and it was very frustrating. It seems to be tailored for an empty gym. I go to the gym in the morning when there is not a lot of people, but it is hard to bounce from one machine to the next (on other side of floor), then go back for a superset. I hate being the person holding 2 machines. I am going to look for a different training program.

Came back to this app after 4 or 5 years and it's improved SOOO much with weight training, visuals, and customizations. I'm just still wary of the food portion. Last time it was expensive, out-of-season foods that weren't being re-used well (eg leftover dinner for next day's lunch). Plus it was not adjusted for height/weight/fitness level. And I also am just realizing how much food tracking or plans lead to restriction/binge cycles rather than intuitive eating.

I'm giving this app a 4 out of 5 stars, only because I haven't been using it long enough to see crazy significant results. But I definitely enjoy the workouts, they're not too difficult, but they are challenging in a way that allows you to push yourself! There's minimum equipment required and a lot of it can be substituted with things in your house! Again, I really enjoy the workouts and even though it's too early for results, I feel good doing them!

EDIT: I have now been using this app for over a year and I really enjoy it. It's the best app I've used by far. ---- I have been following the BUILD program using the Sweat Wpp. The app is great although I'm not sure if it's different for other programs. There are multiple programs to choose from, different types of exercise programs and individual exercises. They have written and video examples. I have made major gains using the BUILD Program.

I am so glad I finally joined the Sweat family! There are so many programs and on demand workouts available to choose from. The app even provides you with recipes and access to the Sweat online community. App is super easy to use, I never have issues but you do need data/wifi. I play music on a separate app because the in app connection is not available. Audio and GIFs show you each movement and additional descriptions are available. Highly recommend for home or gym!

This is the only app where I actually have seen results from the exercises I am doing. I love the fact that you can play your own music through the app. It's so customizeable, the programs make sense and have a normal progression for a beginning... They make me feel motivated and encouraged without any sense of shame. I have not been "dieting" or changing my eating habits. I just do the workouts and my body looks the best it ever has. I love this app.

I really like how her programs are so accessible as a beginner but challenging at the same time. Basically you do as many reps as you can in the allotted time and increase over time as you improve. It's definitely long game progress but Kayla seems to really encourage balance in both exercise and food. I feel it's worth the price. The workouts are adaptable for home or the gym and use minimal equipment. Also, once you're in the app, there are a bunch of other programs other than BBG.

This app provides a structured routine of exercise for 30 minutes per day. I put on the app along with Pandora and do the workout of the day. Having everything planned out for me, makes it so easy. Anyone can be a good exerciser with this amazing tool. And it cost so little. Added bonus, you don't spend time going to gym. And in a pandemic, it's great to have such a wealth of at home workouts.

Disappointed. Other apps available are easy to use over this one. I struggled hard to understand how and what. The timer keeps running. No voice over to provide guidance through the workout. You need to read through which sucks when you are short on time. I recommend Nike Premium which is more interactive and guidance on how the app can be built better. This is definitely not worth 20$ a month if I have to struggle so much.

I was so excited to try this after following some of the trainers for years on social media. Im SO disappointed in what this app offered. Ive used almost every fitness app out there. This was by far the most expensive one i tried and it didnt offer anything that other apps dont. The workouts are very generic, nothing special. Its not at all engaging, and overall i think they could do way better for the price you have to pay. I wish i couldve given more stars, but its honestly not good.

I love this app, I love the plans and exercises but my only complaint is a little too pricey, but I understand why, I know they work hard to help women with their fitness journey and they have so many programs you can choose from, it helped me lose so much weight and it help me to do it step by step without giving up, so overall it's a great app but I'm afraid I can not afford it for long, I wish they had payment options!

I think this app is the best I've seen so far. It's user friendly and it totally is like having a personal trainer in you phone. They keep adding programs and trainers so there's something for everybody Now, it seems most of the programs rely on timed exercises, which I hate, it's really hard to get in position for the next exercise, 3 or 5 seconds it definitely not enough. I would really like they had more program with JUST repetition exercises, but overall, the app it's really great.

I LOVE this app! first started out a few years ago with the ebooks, which were challenging but great. I've had this app for about 6 months now and I absolutely love it. It keeps me accountable and there are SO many options for work out programs. dont be scared by the price, it is worth every penny. I have a work out plan and also access to so many meal plans! this app is amazing and I'm so grateful for the trainers that put in all the time & effort for us!

I really like the workouts and the layout so I decided to pay the $20. However, this application only records things when it wants too. I have imput all the information to detail (weights/rpe and equipment) just for it to get deleted and I keep getting the message " no history you haven't done this exercise" even when the exercise is marked as completed. No reply to emails, no support. App is clearly cheap made and they charge that much - not to mention the spam emails. I'm requesting a refund.

Great app, excellent work out, there's only a couple of things I'd change. First off, I LOVE the gif videos showing proper form of movement and the written explanation of the how to do it. The audio cues for moving to the next step is amazing, and I love being able to connect to Spotify. However, if you move away from the app on your rest, the timer doesn't sound off. Also, when recording the weights you've used, it doesn't populate the weight based on what you did last time. But I recommend it.

This app has a lot of great information (workouts, recovery, food) but it has some shortcomings in terms of user customization. Specifically, I would like to set my week start day to something other than a Monday. It would also be useful to have a single vibrate or tone notification when the time for an exercise/stretch has ended. It is difficult to look at your phone when holding streches to see when its time to move on to the other side or new stretch.

This is an excellent app. One of my favorite things about it is how transparent they are. I was on a trial period for 3 months that was going to expire soon and instead of sneaking the $20 a month program on me they sent me multiple reminders to either stop the program or change my subscription. It was also super easy to cancel my subscription any time, which I only did so I could subscribe again for 6 months! I love all the different options for workouts at home and at the gym!

I've been using Sweat for 4 weeks. I had to restart my first week twice but now I'm hooked and I do my workout every day, except the rest days obviously. I already feel stronger and more confident in my ability. The programs absolutely work. I love it. The app is really easy to use, I love the verbal announcement when changing exercises. The animations are good too. There seems to be a weird bug with the Spotify integration where it will get stuck playing the same few songs during my workout.

After a year of using the app I think it is incredibly well done. I do find the beginner workouts too challenging as I start again after a long break.. but as long as I modify and do something similar during the allotted time/reps, strength soon follows. The food section would be more helpful if it didn't have a different recipe for every meal, every day, if there was room for leftovers, and if recipes had some overlapping ingredients. So I don't use the food section at all. Overall 🤩

This app is really well done. There are so many trainers, exercise styles, and alternatives to choose from. I love that it has home (with no equipment) and gym options, so you can use it anywhere! The scheduling calendar is a bit "meh" and I wish it would sync to my Fitbit. Other than that, it's a great app and I've gotten great results from it!

two stars bc it's not user friendly. It also tells me I've completed an exercise when i haven't and there is no way to undo it. i wish i could give it 2.5 stars though because when you are actually able to do one of the exercises, you really sweat. The exercises are definitely on point. Also, it doesn't let you change the day you did an exercise, isn't linked to other fitness apps i.e. fitbit, and no music. alarm doesn't sound at end of exercise either. $20/month should allow for such things.

I love the workouts, the recovery and all of the support. I do like that they give you meal plans and a shopping list, but I'd really like an option for meal prepping a few days at a time with a list. My biggest issue is when I click out of the app during a workout it pauses my time. I clicked into a music app to change my music and Sweat paused my time, so I worked out for 15 minutes unrecorded.

So far, so good! You can choose from video workouts to follow along or pre-created ones where they just give you instructions for each circuit. I loved the latter because I could go at my own pace. There were video and written instructions for each move, and the option to log your weights to keep track of progression. Updated 3/1/23: I love this app and use it every time I work out. I've gotten so much stronger with it!

Loved the app... then they updated it! I don't like the new way you preview the days workouts now. It was so much easier when it was all on one page and you only had to scroll down to see what the workout entailed. Now you have to scroll down and left/right just to see what the schedule is. And they added an extra picture for the rest periods which totally wasn't necessary. Please put it back! Or at least give me the option to view it the old way.

This app is cumbersome and has many functional flaws. If you forget to log a workout you cannot add it to your calendar later, you can't add notes or explanations about a workout anywhere, you can't even access the content unless you have an Internet connection, and there isn't an option to download a workout to use offline. Some of the workouts are missing basic instructions like telling you to alternate sides or when to switch. The workouts are just ok - very repetitive. Poor value for $20/mo.

Exercises were great, UX was clunky but easy to use. Unfortunately I gave up after 3 weeks because I'm lazy, but my favorite part was how EASY they made it to cancel my subscription. It sounds like a strange reason to rate an app, but it's so common to make your cancellation experience difficult. All I had to do was go to my profile and hit cancel. I was told I could use the rest of my pre-paid month, and immediately got an email that my cancellation went thru. I will reuse again when I'm ready.

Really enjoying the PWR workouts & the over all layout of the app. I think it could be cheaper, most people are having to pay the $20 subscription + gym membership fees. There needs to be a count down in the last 3 seconds of the timer! I feel Sweat is very biased towards apple. Wondering when it will work with an android watch. Would also be convenient to be able to control the app from the lock screen. Go to the next exercise or see the timer instead of constantly having to check the phone.

I'm a slow beginner when it comes to staying healthy. I blame the weather, work, school, etc. for why I don't work out everyday. The nice thing about SWEAT is that regardless of what my days bring or my excuses, the app allows me to fit in whatever exercise style fits my energy level, time frame and equipment availability. Plus, it offers ways to keep me motivated and new workouts are being added CONSTANTLY. I think the annual subscription for an at-home gym membership is totally worth it!

Update. I've been using the app for about 5 yrs. I would love to see more "Build" prog. I workout in the a.m. I don't have time to run all over the gym. Ex: Burpees to cable back to another body weight exercise. Also, it would be fantastic if the time suggested to complete a workout would be more accurate. Ex: Str& Res workout says 45-60min. Not even close, more like 90+min. Most ppl use a public gym, which are typically very busy. Please consider that. I really enjoy the app otherwise!💝💪🏾

I downloaded the app 3 days ago and have not been able to complete even one workout. The app keeps freezing on the second circut or sometimes it wont even go past the warm up.. I've uninstalled and reinstalled twice and even went to the desktop version hoping it would work but it doesn't show me how to do the workouts on the desktop version which is not helpful at all. I am very frustrated with it. 😒

I really enjoy this app and have been using it for the past 3 months. I do wish there was example of modifications for people who cannot do certain types of exercises. I also agree about the food planning and recommendations being unhelpful and ridiculous. I wish there were better options and more reasonable meals. Other than that I love the app, but I think for $20 a month, there should be way more options.

Extremely disappointed. They may offer a 1 week free trial, but you still have to put in your credit card info before you can even see whats in the app. Why would i ever give away my credit card when i have no idea what im getting in return?!?! Like really?!! Seriously? Obvious scam. The Fit52 app is under half the price of this ($3.99-$7.99/month) and offers more than this app offers, with a 3 week free trail and no credit card required until your 3 weeks is up.

I stumbled upon Sweat by chance in social media and I am SO glad I tried it out. This app has everything you could possibly need for your fitness journey. You have access to an array of trainers/ various styles from yoga, barre, HIIT training, lifting, cardio and resistance..or a combination of any and all of those at your disposal every day. There are video clip guided exercises..built in timers and substitutions to accommodate advancement, equipment availability, injury and more. 1yr + user.

Love this app so far! Two suggestions: make a way to record the weights you lifted with the prescribed exercise so that the next time you get the exercise again you know where you were with it last time. Secondly, make it possible to see what exercises are coming up next in the workout so that if, for example, the bench press is already in use I can skip to the next exercise and go back to bench when it is open again!

The app has great exercise programs. Really awesome ones. But unfortunately the food component is SORELY lacking. You can't customize the shopping list ornchange the amount of servings. You have no way of knowing how many calories are being ingested either which may make your workouts for nothing if youre over or under eating. Can't change the amount of people you'll be cooking for. The weekly menus have a lot of wasted food using just 3oz of a whole 16Oz bag of spinach for example feels wrong.

I was so weak when I started this app. I started it in February and had to do the pre BBG because the first week of the normal BBG was way too hard. I haven't stuck with working out consistently but I've been doing it on and off for 8 months and the results are still amazing. I went from hardly being able to do 1 pushup to 64!! I would have gotten to this point faster if I had followed the app closer.I can't recommend this program enough. It's like having a personal trainer in your back pocket.

I love this app! I love that you have so many workout options from so many great trainers. I do wish there was beep after the time finishes for each stretch and for each warm up move. I would also really like if you could swap out meals not just ingredients in the meal planner. Another feature that would be awesome would be the ability to record what weight you used for the exercise. But seriously, a fantastic app!

I did not receive a 7-day trial as expected, but was charged up front. I reached out to the developer within 24 hours, but have had no response. Now, just a few days later, I've reached out to Google Play Store to request a refund, however they send an automated email stating that it's past the time frame to request a refund (48 hours).  I'd like my subscription refunded! Otherwise, I'll have to take the matter to my credit card company to reverse the charge.

I really love the workout options however I wish I could more easily manually schedule my weeks out instead of it automatically assigning days to workouts. Ideally I'd love to pick and choose and be able to move things around easier because every week is different for me. I also wish I could connect it to my Samsung watch! But the workouts are great, I like that they vary even within each program.

this program is very helpful even if you don't use all the features. I honestly can't afford to use the meal planner it has with it but it's still great for ideas. you can customize it for your diet. the workouts are great and challenging. The only downfall is I think it should have an option to enter your own custom workouts. this app is more like a program you follow and it's pretty customizable. but... if you are looking something more as a log or tracker this probably would not be for you.

I absolutely love this App, the training but most importantly the results. I've never experienced noticeable changes in my body in such a shot time, and I have tried everything. This app is the best! If you are like me and struggle to get yourself to workout or to feel the desire to get moving this is the app for you. Now I look forward to working out for 30 minutes every day. I use every part of my body, I feel challenged by the workouts and my confidence had gone through the roof. ❤

App is well laid out and the workouts are relatively easy to understand. But it seems like a workout app for people who are already in shape and workout 6 days a week. Not beginner friendly or for people with physical limitations. Even the substitution exercises are difficult. Would love to see a workout program designed for everyday people and folks with chronic illness/disabilities.

There are tons of features and it's pretty easy to navigate. I am definitely excited to be doing this program! I like how it had a timer built in and it shows and tells tells you how to do everything! I can do it even without anything corrective helping my terrible vision! - i would define a few of their food terms, though. Like, i don't know what the difference is between a lacto- vegetarian and a vegetarian.

I love the app, they do listen. They always seem to be updating and making changes; better and user friendly. Although it's not perfect and still has glitches it's a great app. It has plenty of choices low level exercise to more intense. You have a choice depending on your strength and abilities. The meal plan is not so easy to figure out your macros or calorie intake, for that you have to use a food app.

I love how many programs are available in the app and for so many fitness levels! makes it super user friendly and adaptable for different situations/locations. Where I see an opportunity to grow is having the option of personalizing it a bit more: like personal goals and days or sessions one can make per week and based on that have a program. for instance I would love a 3 full body strength training sessions so we can combine with other disciplines but still have a good strength program.

Its a good app for beginners but needs more for the price. $20 a month is steep when you cannot even track weights and there is no way to input your reps, just follow the app which is very generic. It comes with an amzing community though. Good workouts for beginners but after one month I had to start doing multiple challenges on top of a workout to feel I was getting a workout.

If you have to have a subscription, it should be noted in the description of the app. I was excited to use it after hearing good things, and I realize and respect that people make money off of their ideas and routines, but was not happy that it wasn't brought up until after I had made an account. Now I'm getting emails and notifications about an app I cant use because I'm not in a position to pay $11/mo for an app

I've really enjoyed using this app. Shows you all the moves and has meal ideas and shopping lists. I'm used to it now but I will say it wasn't the most user friendly and I had to learn how to navigate. I also dont think it's the most cuatimizable, like changing days of the week. I wish they had a beginner orientation or tutorial. But overall this had been a great workout tool especially when I'm going out of town. if you're consistent and actually do it, this is so worth it.

best app ever! I love having Chontel Duncans workouts and meal plans (with shopping lists, recipes) and calendars all together! Theres mini clips visible of each exercise during workouts and I love that I can select "already did workout" or "start workout" and use the timer. That's really convenient. THE ONLY THING that could make this app better is if it were a little bit cheaper.

It's an ok app and workouts are xomplete BUT it has it's glitches: 1. It does not let you record past workouts. Like if I go for a run on Monday and don't take my phone it doea not let me record it on Tuesday 2. It lacks sirens to alert you in these circumstances Nd then you HAVE to be constantly looking at your phone: Siren when warmup cardio is up and movement begins. Siren when activation time is up for each set. Siren when a superset time is up. I hope changes are made soon.

The App is not immediately intuitive, but the program is amazing. I have had it for over 2 years now and I really enjoy the workout program. I have introduced it to a handful of people and the ones who installed the app all had troubles and confusion when starting. An introductory walkthrough would probably be helpful. I did not struggle with the app because I had the program back when it was a downloadable pdf file so I knew what I was looking for.

2nd Review: It seems they listen to their customers. They fixed/changed things from my last review and I appreciate it; however, I do want to give more feedback. If we could set the max weight we own, that would be awesome. Example: I only own up to 20 lbs in dumbells, but I cannot set that anywhere I find. It always adds extra weight and I have to set it back. Not a big deal, but it can get tedious after a bit. Last feedback: the "log all sets" button should have a warning after press.

glad I used the free trial in its entirety before making my decision. the first workout was amazing. the second and third workouts were terrible. the second one was way too easy and the third had things I simply can't do. for example (though not the only issue) I am not capable of doing push ups. my wrists cannot support that movement. I tried to replace the exercise and the only options were harder versions of push ups. I stopped the workout part way through because of that. disappointing.

Seems pretty good but unfortunate you have to pay for it to unlock all the benefits/full range use (which I know is common these days) but for a workout app, being 8$/ month or almost $100 annually seems too high. It might show it but I haven't seen yet where it shows your reps and sets when you log your workout which is too bad since that's a decent part about tracking workouts.

Used this for over a year. Had a great time utilizing it for tracking and an idea of what muscles were utilized. Took a break from working out (last time I logged was around late 2025/early 2026) and tried to use the app again. It is now completely unusable. I cannot get pass a screen that tries to make me subscribe. I cant even see the progress I made for two years. Absolute useless subscription trash. Undownloaded.

I don't mind paying for subscriptions, let's start with that. BUT making the user set up the app + personalize the available equipment + create and account and AFTER THAT you realize that you can't even start the trial without adding payment info? You guys are crazy. Made me lose 20 minutes. Uninstall and never again... Oh, and apparently you can't even delete the account because you need to enter to the settings inside the app, which YOU CAN'T without adding the payment info. Bravo.

I was just about to do my 400th workout, we'll passed my 25 free workouts or whatever and never had to pay to use the app and now I can't even access any of my workouts or records! it's enough to charge so much just for the added ability to automatically log a workout but now I can't do anything without having to pay. I tried alot of workout apps too and I really liked this one until now.

The action of logging past or recent exercises is a little cumbersome (entering workout sets as you do them doesn't save automatically, so hitting the back button will close that menu and lose your progress for that group). Other than that, a very solid way to track workouts, as well as progress or fatigue on different muscle groups.

I've used this app for over a year and loved it. The free version was perfect for me: daily workout plans and the ability to log workouts. It helped me stay consistent and track my progress. I'm very disappointed that EVERYTHING is now behind a paywall, especially since there was no warning to existing users. I understand apps need revenue, but removing the core functionality people relied on feels unfair. Such a great app, but this change is disappointing.

this site is great! or it would be if I could get past the SUBSCRIPTION SOFTLOCK. I made an account not knowing about the subscription; my fault probably, but if I knew this beforehand this I'd just never had made my account. Btw you can only delete your account INSIDE Of the app. I had closed the tab and you can figure out what happened from here. I'd love to at least see if it was worth buying but not with the software override of doom.

A truly one of a kind workout app. Tells you everything you need to know about a workout. Also includes customizable tabs for gym or home usage. EDIT: The previous review was from 4 years ago when they offered their free tier. Imagine my surprise coming back after using the app consistently and being met with an immediate pay wall. I can't even view my previously logged workouts, a damn shame. Now I'm definitely not paying and will just revert the app update back from an apk. Shame.

Coming back from a long time not working out, this app is great. I like how it randomize my workouts and keeps things fresh. It's also a good feature that let's me remove exercises because my gym doesn't have the equipment and the app has replacements for equipment that I have access to. Love the goals and badges. Only request, can you add a connect a partner feature to help my wife and I keep each other boosted?

I am currently using the free trial. Not sure if I will continue on with a paid membership. I recently purchased a 150 lb stack weight home gym. I find it very limiting that you do not offer this as an equipment option on your app. I have to choose some from your list that have similar functions that my home gym offers, but I sometimes get in my routine options that do not correspond to what I have on hand. Thankfully, I can sometimes use the swap out options, but it frustrating and limiting.

a few simple improvements to reduce the number of clicks needed would be good. When you finish a workout, go to the next one in your session. Don't make me go back to the name menu and scroll down and find the next one that doesn't have a checkbox on it and click it every step. When you have a cool down session that says to do something for EG 30 seconds, not having a timer available is ridiculous. It would be so easy to implement a timer or on those exercises.

Absolutely love this app, been using around a year or so. Like the feature where it asks at the end of each set how many reps you would have been able to do more of outside of the suggested reps/set so it can adapt for the next time. One thing I so wish they added is the notes option where it is added to the bottom of the exercise and it keeps track of whatever you add from previous times for that particular exercise. Right now it just has a notes feature and it's a pain to get to.

I've been using Fitbod for years to stay consistent in my routine. Lately there has been a bug when trying to see saved workouts — the screen just stays blank. Closing the app and clearing storage doesn't fix it. The saved workouts do appear after a few minutes or sometimes not at all, it can be random. I'm hoping an update fixes the issue.

For the most part a decent App. However, in recent weeks leg information is not being updated. It tracks recovery and let's you input and save. However, set progress is not receiving any information. I personally submitted a trouble ticket but have yet to receive any response. Frustrating when you have a paid subscription.

I was tricked into signing up and giving credit card info. To address your response; a free trial doesn't negate the fact that you were dishonest in how you tricked me into signing up. I was glad that once I realized I had been tricked into installing your app instead of the one I wanted, I was able to cancel the subscription. I first just uninstalled your app, the later realized I had to go into my account and remove the subscription also

I want to give a good rating but the app still puts the wrong exercise in the middle of a workout. One day I told support that it was now creating half of my workouts in Spanish and they sent a "We hope your issue is resolved l" email the next day. None have been resolved and I'm still paying full price while doing half of the work by editing my own workouts.

subscription only app 🥀 (after your response) thank you for responding to my review, but, I have zero money i am a literal minor, don't care about no damn free trial, I'll use a different app without a subscription, biggest issue was that I had to email to delete my account, without paying for subscription or getting free trial, I couldn't even get in the app to delete it.

Looks like it woulda been great. Took the time to fill everything out then couldn't proceed without accepting a free 7 day trial. Meaning I'll have to pay later. Wish that transparency was up front so I didn't waste my time filling everything out. I don't like having to give my information, then surprise... can't have it without paying. Feels untrustworthy to give money. Knowing up front - I know what I'm getting into.

Decided to check out the app I've been seeing everywhere. got signed up, put in my details and before it even showed me anything it popped up with the "Try premium NOW!!!" with a week free. I hit the "x" and then it pops up again with an extended trial offer with NO option to X out. immediately uninstalled. stop pushing premium without letting users try the app first. 🙄🙄🙄

not enough gym machines list which should be easy. ability to tailor the app to yourself is laughable. repeated tried to get machine only workouts in vain. it does what it wants to do and does not accept what you actually want! For developer. not interested on trying to add custom exercise for something I am paying for. Also you didn't answer why recommendations cannot be machine only. If it was a free app ok but for me to pay never.

Predatory billing practices. After downloading the free one week trial, the subscription automatically renewed into a yearly membership, resulting in a ~$130 charge. The charge wasn’t discovered until two weeks into the membership, the app was never used, and Google declined to issue a refund.

Concept is great, but execution is half-baked. Been using this for a year or so(?) They keep saying they're excited to launch the new Android version while we're still without a lot of the (apparent) iOS features and integrations... still paying for a full app though. I do like how the workouts adjust based on the calculated fatigue of each muscle group and the easy-to-use rest periods during exercise. Maybe other apps do this, but I've been too lazy until now to look into it; this does good enough, but it's not as cool as you see it advertised.

Great application, with some caveats. The Good I like the way you can select a workout by intensity, muscle group, available equipment, etc. I like the instructional videos and historical tracking The Bad You are limited to the exercises included in the database. There's a spot where you can request an exercise be added, I have done this a couple of times, but none of them have been added. No combo moves, limited variations, etc. customer service not responsive. Bummer for $80/year.

It is easy to swap out exercises, and I like the rest timer. However, it takes a very long time to show up in my Fit app after syncing. It also installs an app on my watch, but doesn't stay open or give me relevant information (like heart rate and calories burned) during the workout. That did end up showing up in the workout log, but would be good to see during the workout.

I've been using the app for years and love it. The quick ability to replace a routine is key when I'm looking to tweak the recommended workout. And I love that it pushes me over time with more weights, sets, or exercises. My biggest complaint (several years running now) is that Strava Sync still doesn't exist on Android. Given all my other apps have this, this may eventually become a deal breaker for me.

The ability to switch workouts you don't want with ones you do is a plus and I like the variety, but I've been having Health Connect issues. I can't seem to get the calories burned to show up correctly on Samsung Health with all permissions granted. I also like the Android Wear app, but a suggestion I have is when you finish a rep and click it on the watch, it should automatically go to the next one on your phone (unless I don't have it set up correctly). Overall, it is a great app

I loved the app. Paid the premium. Arm and chest workouts are awesome but the legs and hamstrings are killing me. It's all Romanian, they make no sense, not simple for beginners, and if someone has back problems you automatically work the wrong muscles because you can't push the way they want you to or you can't feel the burn at all. Put in actual squats as I can't find them. And please add real variety for legs. Well rounded workout is the expectation of the app. Not just upper body.

Great app, been using it for a few months now. I have an android and it sounds like IOS gets better features, but if I didn't check the reviews I wouldn't know any better. Great at tracking, demonstrating, and auto calculating. Only 4 stars because it glitches out rarely. (Got logged out, erased my days data) only recommendation is to add a meal and diet side to the app. Separate, but the same. I will note that the replacement option is limiting but can be manually entered in otherwise.

Excellent!! Definitely worth the price! Easy to personalize your workouts and make adjustments as needed. Bad knees? Switch out your exercise. Great for different fitness levels. Being currently out of shape, it's easy to step up the intensity as I get stronger. I also enjoy being able to see the total pounds that I lifted during any given workout.

Haven't used it for long yet, but my impressions so far, as someone who is just starting to take workouts seriously, are pretty good. They ask the important questions, but don't expect you to shell out a ton of money for extra features or gym equipment you don't own right from day one, even though I was expecting and willing to pay at least some small amount. Excited to see how it works once I can get started.

I like the app. Particularly the home gym where you can list your equipment. It doesn't seem to track my progress when I manually update my weights, as the same exercise the next week will go back down in weight even though I upped it in the previous workout. It froze up today and wouldn't let me start my workout, wouldn't let me log in to my account, etc... I had to uninstall and then reinstall to get it to work. For the cost this thing should run flawlessly. There are other apps cheaper...

I am new to lifting and this app has given me the confidence at the gym and the insight to know how much I should be lifting and the proper form at my fingertips. I really like it. It could use an option to switch between generated routines that are home based (or little/no equipment) to gym based. I wish there was an easier way to customize what you have access to. You can build your own routines so there is a work around. Overall a great app.

So far, I'm giving this 3 stars. If it works well, I may increase the rating. But, so far, it seems very finicky. After putting in all of my information initially, it gave me a workout requiring equipment I said I don't have, for muscle groups I did not pick to work on. I had to go back through, and change it all again. Not a great first impression of your app.

This app has truly been life changing for me. I was never able to stick to a workout routine. This was mostly because I really didn't know what routine to do. When I found a routine I liked, I would repeat it until I got bored with it...and then quit altogether. I have been using fitbod consistently for almost 2 years now! I feel better and look the best I have in my life! Fitbod switches up your workout every day based on the criteria you input. It is completely customizable. Best app!

I've tried a lot of workout apps, and so far this one is the best. I honestly really like this one. I've been working out for years. I have ADD and it has always been frustrating and overwhelming trying to put workouts together and ones that im happy with. This app, don't just give you one set way to work out and be like, "ok, here you go". If gives you many options to pick and choose from to taylor around what you are looking for, and also equipment that is available to you. GREAT app!

Fitbod has organised my workouts in a way I don't have to think about it too much. Once the goals are set, it is almost autopilot from that point on. Only minor changes regarding time, equipment, and workout options are needed as per user needs. As of today, I am going through my 3rd subscription, and I don't think I'll change it. Thanks for the efforts 👌🏼

Pretty good workout app. Few things I think could use work is the muscle group variations. Really like doing a PushPullLeg split but can't do that effectively through here when it recommends an unequal balance of muscle groups, ie legs only once a week. Also, some muscle groups never get recommended unless you go in and change it, ie traps, calves, forearms. Another thing that would be nice is more equipment for home gyms, like sandbags. Overall a good app.

I really like the app and saw some gains using the app. I wont give it less stars because the issues can be fixed or one can adjust on the app but that takes away from the time i already had to carve out of my day. I would like to see some of these things adjusted. I work out alone and some of the workouts require I have a workout partner. Also, some beginners workouts start at 30 lbs. No way i can start with that. Some of the weights suggestions are weird. i don't have a 7.5 lb. weight.

I've been using this app for 3 years now and love it. It's very user friendly and functional. I love that I can select specific equipment I have access to so no longer the days of a workout where I don't have half the equipment. I also really like the ability to select a step and select to do it more often, less often or never again. Update: I mentioned I'd like to be able to set gym profiles/locations and they let me know you can!!! I love this even more now. Thanks for the instructions!

I'm enjoying the app and the variations on workouts. I wish it allowed for 2 things though. #1 - it would be awesome if I could save and "merge" workouts. Specifically when I have a shorter workout related to rehabbing an injury. Being able to track and add those in would be nice. #2 - some days I want a variation of workouts before picking one. Keeping a history of generated workouts would be an awesome value add.

I've only used this app for two workouts so far and it's already better than any I've tried. Straight to the point. Simple interface. Good variety of exercises based on the equipment I own, and it seems to smartly choose exercises based on what I've recently worked out. Takes all the guesswork out of my home workout. However, there are no integrations on the android app like there are on iOS. I'm really missing Google Fit or Health Connect integration. 5 stars if Health Connect is added!

Really a fantastic app! I used to carry around a paper and pen and write my workouts in, but planning workouts was not my strong suit. This app takes out the guess work and gives you exercises with the equipment you currently have at home or have available at the gym. I've been using it for two weeks now and going with the monthly option for now. If I can stick to the schedule, I'll do a full year membership. Can't wait to see my results this time next year!

it was better before the recent update. i liked being able to add exercises NEXT, instead of LAST, in the list. at least let us choose our preference. now i have to drag each thing up into the correct order after adding. it's cumbersome. also not a fan of it asking additional questions every time i remove/change something. i understand this is to help your algo; but it's too intrusive, and it frequently doesn't even work. if i select "delete and don't recommend," it doesn't delete.

I love this app! It is exactly what I was looking for as a beginner starting to weight lift after loosing 195lbs. I was able to cancel my gym membership and buy two adjustable dumbbells, resistance bands, a medicine ball and other various equipment to fully do my workouts from home. I would give the app 5 stars if your workout information (reps and sets) transferred from one device to the other via cloud servers. Other than that it's pretty awesome 👐🏾

Very good app, but there's some kind of glitch where once a week or so it will give me a workout with insanely high reps with almost no weight (4 sets of 20 bench press at 45 pounds, 4 sets of 20 lat pull downs at 30 pounds, that kind of thing). Recalculating weight and reps doesn't work. The only way to fix it is by manually entering the amount sets, reps, and weights I want to do. The whole point of the app is so that I shouldn't have to keep track of those things. Please fix.

I've been out of shape for a very long time and never really worked out in general, even when I was young. I've been wanting to get more active for a while but gym memberships are expensive and most workout guides for at home routines are intimidating. This app is great, it asks several questions to get to know you, then builds a daily routine for you. Even as a beginner I can complete most the workouts it gives. Highly recommend for those in similar situations!

This would be a five star app, if there were parity with Apple. But there isn't, and frankly that's a little bit messed up. We're paying the same amount, but getting far less usage out of this otherwise excellent app because we're part of a different ecosystem. I know it's hard as developers to export to two different platforms, but parity has to become a major priority. Otherwise, charge us less.

Three days free, have to pay after that. Well worth it. l love the ease of use for this app. If I'm working out from home, rather than gym, I can change the settings to only incorporate body weight movements. If a machine is being used in the gym, I have options to find a comparable move that uses different equipment. This app gives me the direction I need, along with the flexibility of every day life.

I was intrigued with the machine learning aspect of this app. While I like the idea of some of the concepts, I feel that this app just fails to deliver...especially since you have to pay for it. You'll see the reviews mention the same "cons" that I noticed. There seems to be a better ios version that android version. For example, if you want to copy a workout that you did previously...apparently you can do that in ios but not android. Why would you need to do that if the app intelligently creates your workout? Well, there are a few reasons, but sometimes it creates odd superset combinations--pairing 2 completely different exercises that wouldn't fly in a normal gym like bouncing between the barbell bench and dumbbells or pairing 2 similar exercises that work the same muscle and defeat the purpose of supersetting 2 exercises that work different muscles to cut down overall rest time. Another reason to create your own workout would be if you had a specific set of exercises given by your doctor or physical therapist, but that's a challenge if not all of those exercises are available in the app because you can't add your own and you can request an exercise to be added, but I haven't seen even one of my requests added over the few months that I've been using the app. Also, one of the features that I looked forward to was calculating an increase in reps/weights, but it does some weird stuff sometimes...like increasing from 20 pound dumbbells to 22.5 pound dumbbells...I'm not sure I've seen 22.5 pound dumbbells. A nice feature is that you can edit the equipment in your gym, but it doesn't help when you can specify if your gym has "dumbbells" but not indicate that your gym doesn't carry the standard 22.5 pound dumbbell. One last issue that I would mention is that while the concept of selecting exercises for a specific muscle is nice and the idea of managing muscle fatigue by generating a workout for muscles with the most rest is nice, this app just fails at it. While it may be doing possibly more work in the background, I can give an example of what I'm talking about. Let's take the deadlift as an example since it works multiple muscles--hamstrings, glutes, traps, and abs just to name a few. The only muscle group that the deadlift is available on the app is for the hamstrings. So if you want a glute workout, it's not an option. I can only imagine that this logic is carried through by not calculating any fatigue on the glute and only for the hamstring when the deadlift is recorded. That's just one example, but there are tons of exercises that work multiple muscle groups...I just don't see this app being that intelligent. Bottom line, this app would be good if some of the logic was cleaned up, but it's not free so it's definitely not worth it. I feel compelled to use it since I paid for it, but it's really not the best app that I have for working out. A note for the App author, it would probably be better if it were free and maybe had a little advertising in it to provide the revenues, or at least some free version to help get more subscribers with more workable data for you to use.

Love the app. Very easy to use, helps provide structure to the wayward. I do not like how there isn't a free plan, or that that fact is not disclosed in the description. It does say there are in-app purchases, but it does not tell you you only get 3 workouts before being prompted to pay. I understand and respect a company needs to meet their bottom line, I just want to know how before I have given them my email address, rather than after.

First thing to say is that I really enjoy the app. It has made working out so much less boring and repetitive, for me. I really wish that it would give me the chance to give more details about my home gym though - i can't do 30lb bar work, I only have a 45lb bar. Similarly, my dumb bells go up in 5lb increments from 5-30lb. Would be great if it would let me input things like that and not recommend sets I can't do.

So far, I really like it! Since it creates workouts for me, allows me to change the weights/reps/actual workout, and it allows me to preview the workouts - I am definitely a lot more consistent and comfortable going to the gym. I wish there was a constant free version where a person could get half of the app like gets 2 free days a week and limited targeted muscle groups instead of having to purchase a membership because I only work out 1-2 days a week and only use targeted muscle groups.

This has made weightlifting so much easier and fun. It has taken all the guesswork out of workout routines. Love it! Two improvements: (1) perhaps move the timer "Done" button over to the middle and the pause symbol to the left. I've accidentally double clicked the Log Set button and the hit Done on the timer which popped up right underneath it. A little shift would solve it. (2) Is there any way to select a subset of machines to make a workout? For example, just hi lo cable workout?
