Not only that, if you're trying to track your workouts using Samsung Health on your watch the Hevy app forces it to stop tracking randomly because it's (Hevy is) such a piece of junk.

The Hevy app is nice! It is a great app for logging and tracking your workouts. I use the free version and it's good. I would like more options for bodyweight / calisthenics / resistance bands options for tracking. If you have that feeling of needing to report or log workouts to see your progress then definitely give the Hevy app a try.

Really like the app, the tracking and progression monitoring has been game changing. I have recommended it to many ppl. While the exercise library is extensive, its missing some common movements. However, you can add a customs that get added to ur personal library. the cons are minimal but mainly consist around random ui errors, like closing out an edited workout session and only getting half of the option to update or keep appears on screen or a formatting limitations when adding custom img.

Love this app for tracking my workouts. It has everything I need all in one place. Not only can I track my workouts, but I can also send them to others when planning a group workout. The sample workouts are great, as are the animated tutorials for newbies like me. I will definitely recommend this to others.

really great app to track progress and log workouts and measurements. I'm part of a fitness group on FB and already got about 8-10 people to sign up and get the premium subscription too so we can keep track of ourselves and show off our progress to each other. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that goes to the gym.

I love this app. I'm new to the gym and it's really helped me build a consistent routine & track the metrics that matter to me so I can see my progress every time, & adjust sets reps and exercises on the fly. I also value the small animations of each exercise in case I have form questions & the muscle heat map to show what I've worked in each set and over the course of the workout and week. The comparison of my total weight lifted to a heavy object like a truck or a yacht is awesome!! LOVE IT!

this is my go-to app for tracking all of my workouts. It keeps my rest periods consistent and I can implement progressive overload because it always shows how much I lift it the time before. for anyone. just getting started or who is currently a lifter. this is a great app to track your workouts. if only they add a circuit or tabata timer, then we'll be in business.

The app is pretty great, I like how it lets you compare your workouts over time. I wish my optional post workout summary photos would always give me the star chart that measures the muscle groups. 5 stars if you guys could make it a permanent choice. Other than that, the app is pretty great.

Really enjoying Hevy. I’ve completed about 25 workouts and have seen solid strength gains. I like how it suggests weights and reps based on past workouts—it pushes progress without overdoing it. The PR tracking is motivating (volume and max weight). Logging feels smooth, the rest timer keeps me on track, and workout time estimates are accurate. I upgraded to the lifetime version after about two weeks and have been happy with it.

As an advanced lifter doing this solo,meaning no trainer, this app helps tremendously with tracking and organizing my lifts. I love the rest timer aspect to keep me from exceeding my resting periods!!! 10000% recommend if you want to track/log your workouts. bonus : it tells you the total weight you lifted for the day!! fun!!

playStoreReviews.text: The total weight lifted does not match the phone app version. Today the watch says I lifted 15730 but the phone version says 28090...which is correct!? Also the scroll wheels are hard to use often end up scrolling past the rep or weight number you actually want to hit. Other than that, I like having the watch version. it's much easier than carrying around a phone in the gym

I have been using this app for years and it has been great, until recently. I usually look at the progress index to see about how my workout has been going over the week. Lately that has not been counting my weight lifting. I have had other issues getting the app to even record daily workouts in the progress.

Been a great app that I used for tracking my workout for 2+ years. I even paid premium subscription for it. this latest AI based approach has made it literally unusable for me. lost all the workout plans I had saved. and now it keeps changing my workout plans on a weekly basis. this adaptive approach should have the alternative to go back to original plans. literally unusable now

i used this app for about 2 years. this last year I haven't used it, let alone working out. I really did enjoy it. using it again, rate 4 stars because on my progress page, go to a certain date, the notes tab is gone on top of the screen. I wrote down what i did, how I felt during the workout on that day and now it's gone. it kept me motivated when I needed to look back on what I wrote down. it was like a journal. bummed it's not there now

I've been using this app for years and it's been getting progressively more annoying to deal with. The database for exercises is still great, but the overall interface has gone downhill. Editing exercises and plans is clunky and the cloud sync is horribly broken on workout plans. It's still ok for tracking workouts, but it seems like it's been tweaked with to death.

Fantastic. But wish timer wouldn't disappear when you leave a screen and that the times were more accurate. I haven't used it in a while and just re-downloaded it. I'm going to ding it one just because of how annoying it is when they artificially slow something down and show some cute graphics as though calculating something. Just calculate what you need to and don't get cute.

This was a great workout tracker when I paid for a subscription. Originally, the app showed me values for past workouts, and I had control. Recently, it changed to give me randomized values for weights and rep counts. There's also a new step after workouts to rank your effort level before getting to summary. There's an option in settings to disable this feature, but it doesn't work and my reps and weights continue to randomize, and it takes more time to end the workout.

I've been using this app for years but the recent auto-progression bug has made this app too unreliable for me. I'm just looking for something that will allow me to ACCURATELY keep track of weight, reps, and sets for each exercise. I've sent as much information as I can to the developers, but they keep asking for more and, after many months, haven't been able to fix the bug.

Would rate this app alot higher but there are quality of life items they desperately need to add to set themselves apart from all the other workout trackers 1 - Why don't you have alternate names for exercises? The amount of exercises that are the same thing but are called different things depending on where you learned it is pretty staggering. And im not saying the wrong names are being used, but it gets incredibly annoying and completely disrupts the flow of a workout when you have to stop an

Seems fine at 1st, then you find out tech support and features don't work. Strava integration is bogus... Jetfit does not share data back to Strava, Tech support expects you to be onthe payroll and basically do all their troubleshooting for them. You don't find these things out until you pay for elite access and need the app to work as advertised. If you don't need tech suooprt or any of the elite features the app works fine... just don't pay for it, because you do not get what you pay for

I've used Jefit for over 6 years now. I can honestly say it is the best. I have an online coach but need focus in the gym as I get distracted easily. This keeps me on track and records previous weights so I'm always progressing. it tracks your rest and give a little alert to move you on. It celebrates personal bests and give clear instructions. highly recommended.

I've always stayed away from workout apps because I thought they were unwieldy. This one is very easy to use within the workout and entering data is itself motivating. The built-in exercises provide good alternative ideas. I've recommended this to a number of my gym buddies 9 years later I still agree

The best fitness app ever. I've been using this app since 2013 whenever you think you can barely make it through a workout just stick to your Jefit and it will get you through I wish I could rate it a million stars because it's worth it. So many days I was barely in the gym and Jefit was the only thing that got me through. It's a wondrous wonderful app that will take care of you as long as you respect the app. I don't know where I'd be without it and I dont want to know.

great app to use. the only negative thing i have on this: when their are workouts based on machine assist equipment (like pull up assist) and workouts where you have to alternate working out one side of your and equally do the same on the other (like a body lunge), I wish it gives better data or tell what to input in to make the progress look appropriate.

Phone and desktop apps are good. I've used them for years. The watch version is almost useless. I was hoping to record my workouts with just the watch, leaving the phone in the locker room. Unfortunately, this doesn't work. I contacted the Jefit help desk, and they said the phone needs to be close by. The app functionality on the watch is limited, so it is of questionable use. Jefit needs to find a way for the watch to sync without the phone. The watch has wifi, so perhaps that could work.

This is the best workout app I've found. It's easy to create a workout schedule. The app does a good job of guessing the focus for each routine ("abs", "upper body", etc.), so I often just use the description summary the app comes up with, though it's simple enough to customize when needed. The app has decent animated depictions of the exercises so I can confirm they are the one I'm looking for. I was also able to add a few custom exercises when i couldn't find them in the app. Overall great!

Used to like the app, I longer have it installed. I have no idea when these new updates occurred. you have to pay for dark mode, and the small tutorials of the exercises now. The app is no longer user friendly. It is not for me, I am sure it is still useful as a spreadsheet but even that has become more difficult to track. Back to using notes i go.

Just started using the app so if there is a way to do some of these, let me know. Easy to input workouts. While it allows me to link exercises when creating a routine, I have not figured out how to create a Superset when manually entering my workouts after the fact. Also it would be nice to be able to enter an entire routine rather than one exercise at a time. More analytics by exercise (line chart) would be nice to scroll through so I could see trends in how I'm doing. I like it so far.

I first used this app over 10 years ago, and it actually introduced me to proper lifting, and different workouts based on equipment. back then, it was a different layout and you would only select which muscle groups you wanted to target, but now I am glad to see the changes in the app, and it's still amazing. not a fan of the membership prices, but I respect it nonetheless.

Best app ever!! UPDATE: I still think this is the best app I've used for tracking my workouts. I was having an issue a few months ago, I emailed the developer and they responded in a timely manner. After a couple of days, my issue was resolved. I'm a newly starting personal trainer, I was given information about how to use the app for training clients. I will have to look more into it. It's still the BEST APP EVER!!

I've been using the app for years and it's been an essential part of my workouts. However, a few weeks ago the rest timer no longer started after logging a set. It's not a big problem but it does reduce the smoothness of using the app for my workouts. I also requested help from support when it happened but they stopped replying.

The app has everything you need but the new formatting has made it a pain to use and navigate and no it's not something you get used to, its just bad. Instead of using days of the week I label my workouts based on muscle group, the labels on the app just show ANY, ANY, ANY, ANY, ANY since this is where the day would normally go so you have to click on each "day" to figure out which workout each one is. Finding the history of each exercise is also buried to see the last time you did it.

I used to rate this app a 5/5. It's great to track your workouts and learn about new ones. I'm even a premium member. However, the last couple updates have just completely destroyed the app. I don't know why they're releasing so many updates that actually make it worse rather than better. For example, logging weight is now broken, it keeps logging the date as February 1st 2021, so I'm not longer able to track my weight. There's other bugs but I'm out of space to review. Caution when downloading.

I've been a die hard user of this app for a long time. For the most part it fits all my needs. Within the app though, I've changed my profile picture several times but it never actually changes. I also have never understood how they calculate wasted time and actual workout. If you were to add up all the time it doesn't even add up to the session length. I notice other users have 0.00 wasted time. Doesn't make sense. Maybe someone can shed some light on these issues?

This app has almost everything I need for my workout. It has a large database of exercises, making exercise selection easy, although if there is an exercise missing, you can create a custom exercise yourself. You can upload progress photos, keep track of body stats, and follow the progression of your lifts in a simple and easy to understand graphing tool. The only thing I think needs changing is the fact that hamstrings and quads are lumped together in the 'Upper Leg' category.

This app is as basic as possible. If you're new to working out and don't know anything, this is the app for you. If periodization is important to you and you rotate your cycle every month or use a method like the cube method or anything more advanced than a basic workout, this app is not for you. Copying your days is not possible unless you pay. My workout routine days just disappeared. This app should be simpler than using paper, pen, and a calculator, but it feels much more difficult.

The app needs to get out of the way, and let me actually exercise. It is so cluttered with junk that it's unusable. You can create your own workout routine - but the app will bury it somewhere in a list of 100 existing routines, and you'll never find yours. You can specify a lift you'll do in a routine - a deadlift for example. But when you start to enter "deadlift", it doesn't exist - your options are Romanian deadlift, Hungarian deadlift, one-legged deadlift..., etc. It should be simple.

I find this app difficult to use while working out. When you're on an exercise machine, there's really no way to hold and watch your phone to keep track of the timers, and there's nowhere to set it down except on the floor where it could get stepped on. Also, if you accidentally start part of your workout, there doesn't appear to be any way to re-start your workout. My first trial at using the app at the gym was a frustrating melange of trying to work the machines while fumbling with my phone. Also, there needs to be a "I'm working out by myself" mode that avoids exercises that are dangerous to do alone like dead lifting and bench presses.

A very good work out tracker and always improving! The only suggestion I have is that I wish the app could temporarily replace an exercise in your work out rather then have a permanent replacement. I have my set routine I follow and sometimes there is someone one the equipment I want so I'll do an alternative exercise, but there (as far as I know) isn't an efficient way to track that. I could add the exercise into my workout routine but I'd have to delete it later.

Great app for the use. You can totally get by without having to buy a subscription, which is great if you're just getting started. My only gripe is that is needs a larger library for lifts. Granted, the library's is pretty massive already. However, the absence of sled lifts (workouts) and other free movement lifts (sledge hammers, incline runs, etc...) are apparent. Love the app. 4.5 Stars

Edited to address developer response: 7 is hardly 'generous'. I'm using all of them which is the issue. If an exercise is not in your database then the only option is to create a custom one. As far as paying the outrageous monthly fee you ask for what this app offers, no. If I were interested in what the paid things offer I'd be doing it. I'm not going to sign up for a bunch of services I don't want just to be able to add a few exercises to the program. Make it a one-time charge for unl custom.

Love the app and been using it for 3 years now, but recently I cant update my body stats... I'll put them in, hit save, and it just goes back to my body stats from like 6 months ago when I was 151 lbs. I'm 160 now and grew a lot from that time, but I'm tired of having to screenshot my progress instead of just being able to log it on here. Besides that it's a 5 stars, just the body stats ain't working on my phone and my computer.

Continually getting worse. I've been an elite member for YEARS. The app is getting worse with every iteration. The problem is where they're trying to move towards the social aspect. This means I now have extra steps to get to my workout track. Defeating the purpose. I'm all for expanding features but at least giving (paying) customers the option!

Almost 5 stars, but not quite. I like that I can create workouts/routines and it is very easy and seamless to do so. The only additional thing that would make it 5 stars for me is if for the running exercise they allowed you to set a distance goal along with the time. For example running 1 mile in 7 minutes. My only other gripe is the time entry only allows whole numbers. I would suggest incorporating a format of mm:ss

App is going downhill real fast. Trying to be another social media platform when all I want to do is log my workouts. I don't care about Tim and what he's up to. Also, I wait to save my workouts, like I sit there and wait until the app saves and thinks it does only to open it up and see that I have to sync AGAIN. Time to find a better app. One more, stop reminding me about a membership I would never utilize. I know it exists, I don't need a Google replica to remind me of useless options.

The new update destroyed years of data. Please please please please stop trying to "fix" what was never broken. This was by far the best workout tracking app there was...and today after trying to log an exercise after some incredibly non-intuitive updates, it overwrote every workout in my decade-long history with that one exercise. I will likely never use this app again. This is inexcusable.

8.27.25 Update This used to be a great app when I paid for it. as most exploitative companies do, they tried to force the user base into their new product instead of starting new so I lost my purchase. It allowed you to track things well and dynamically make small tweaks to workouts before, during, or after. Now they have made it harder to make changes in workout and easy swaps aren't allowed unless you subscribe to their crazy expensive option. There's not even a cheap option for subscription.

This app has potential. I like being able to keep track of my time in between sets with it, but it has one major flaw. They delete your entire program if you make it yourself. I'm assuming this is because they want you to buy their programs (that are absolutely not written by strength professionals of any sort). Nothing is a slap in the face like planning out your next 12 weeks of training, which took you hours, and when you go to start the first workout, all of your work is gone.

One of the best lifting apps, however, it used to have a feature where it incentivised your workouts by offering "Elite points" for your effort (which could get you well-curated workouts and extra stats about your lifts), but now it seems they're just after your money like everyone else. It's sad to see free tools slowly disappearing one-by-one, especially since physical fitness and health are so prevalent in our current times.

Another update another round of unnecessary and frustrating changes. I've used this app for years because it used to do exactly what you wanted for a workout tracker but there is now so much bloat here and I'm tired of being frustrated when everyone speaks up and they just tell them they're wrong. I'll go back to tracking in a notebook before I use this app another day.

The latest update sucks. Obviously designed by some moron who thinks graphics and style matter more than actual functionality or ease of use. Where used to you simply clicked on a dare and could easily find an exercise that you could enter date into. Now there are so many exercises and instead of being readily available to enter information you have to find the edit button and add sets. And then there is no timer to simply hit. It's so bad I've deleted the app to find something else.

Broadly really love this app, I find logging and tracking my workouts as I do them, really easy. However the recent loss of the ability to add historic logs using my pre- configured workout plans is really frustrating. I don't use the app during my PT sessions, but I capture the details afterwards - now I have to hunt for each exercise separately to add them... feels like a step backwards..

This app has all kinds of workouts!! I have a home gym so naturally I am missing quite a bit of equiptment needed in most workout routines. With Jefit, you don't need machines. They have alternative workouts for everything! I am very please and give 5 stars! I especially like that this app works in airplane mode. I am in the Navy and while on deployment this app worked with no issues overseas and while at sea!

I really like the app. The majority of the features are free and it has a great database. It really helps keep track of workouts and I've been using this app exclusively for a while now, and even decided to get premium. I also love the calculation of how many trucks or elephants you lifted that workouts. My only complaint is that I don't care how much the whole community lifted together. I wish I could toggle it to only look at my stats.

It took me awhile to figure everything out, but I continued to use the app even when I only had a basic understanding of it. Now that I know how to enter my own workouts including putting in exercises not included in the database as well as setting the number of sets and reps and rest time I am really happy with it. Now all I have to grab when I head to the gym is my phone and headphones!

Update: I've been using jefit for 2 year now. I did like the older layout but i understand the need for change. I recommend this app to everyone. I really love it and was and is a staple for my work outs and progress. I do wish there were some more exercises. Like for dumbells there is no waiter curls. Hope they add some more exercises at some point.

I've used Jefit for 13 years. The new version fixed some bugs, but got rid of just being able to record sets without doing a workout. The charts are still rudimentary - can't zoom in on a date range or adjust the Y axis. The "24-hour elite sale" that happens seemingly every other day is disingenuous and annoying. There have to be better fitness trackers.

This is a great app to use for your workouts. it has a variety of exercises to keep a log of cardio, calisthenic, and/or muscle strength workouts. The app also has a variety of exercises to target specific muscle groups. The only reason I gave it four stars is because I can't create my own exercises. Perhaps it's possible and I just couldn't find out how, but I would like to be able to customize my workouts using the exercises that I'm actually performing.

I really enjoyed using it so much that I purchased the premium upgrade from the free. When I bought it for $15 or so a few years back it was supposed to be a purchase for life. Now they charge a monthly fee for premium and i cant access any of the workouts I would like to do. ** Update I am giving it another shot. Purchased the Elite Subscription. Bonus is that there is kettlebell workouts, this is the first tracker with kettlebells that I have found.** I will update in a month or 3.

I enjoy this app. I love that you can pick worksout plans and modified them to fit your gym equipment. I do have some ideas for this app. First, i use YouTube workout mucis while Im at the gym and these two app do not work with each other. I have to exit one or the other to operate both. A bit aggravated. So, if this app could app workout music per workout it would be great. Also, I am in the Army amd it your be great if there was a workout for the new Combat Army Fitness Test ( CAPFT).

great app I've went through about 12 apps before this one. only reasons I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 is it's a little hard to figure out at first plus it could use a bigger library of workouts and it would be great if I could sync this to Samsung health. but other that that best workout tracker I've ever used and the point insensitive is absolutely wonderful. I would recommend this app to anyone.

I love this workout app, it got plans all laid out for you better yet most of them are free to use and if you want more out of it you gotta pay which isn't that expensive. This is the best workout app I have ever used and I've been using everyday for the past 17 days now. ***Update, I am 3 months into using this app and I see a lot of changes in terms of fat loss and muscle growth. Keep up the works into this app. 👍

Really good app. I just have the free version and it's honestly really good This one is really simple easy to use. You can add multiple workout programs that you can personalize yourself. The only thing I wish was different is you could add specific notes to each workout or set. For instance sometimes I use resistance bands instead of weights and I would like to be able to keep track of which one they use so I know when I'm leveling up and stuff. But overall good workout tracker.

Has potential. Website is horrible. Needs overhaul and modernized. For example, spent 10 min typing a forum post, but the website timed out by the time the submit button was clicked. All lost. No search bar function on website (on the exercise database). Many other changes needed as well. I'm personally going to move on and try other workout trackers. I've been using JeFit for a couple years now plus paid membership.

A decent app though the ads are annoying. Unfortunately to get rid of the ads costs you US$40/year. If you want/need the other things you get for the subscription it might be worth it but if all you need is the basic functionality without the ads then it is seriously overpriced. A one time fee to remove the ads would move my rating to 4 and possibly 5 stars. Unfortunately, it looks like these developers are following the subscription model used by companies like Adobe to inflate their bottom line. I'll only be using this app until I find one comparable that doesn't overcharge its users.

The $40/yr subscription is just a perpetual unlock fee that restores features that used to be free, like the exercise video examples. The other features it adds are not worth $40 a year, especially when it already has ads in the app. There really is no justifying the cost, not even with the cloud sync (which I don't need) or the "elite routines" that you can just make yourself. This app should either be ad supported or have a permanent unlock available, not a subscription.

Not a fan of constant pressure to be "friends" with people you don't know. You need to be able to turn the Community feature off. Needs Health Connect integration. Fit integration works okay, but takes longer when using other apps for total health tracking. You need to sync to Fit, then Fit syncs to Health Connect, then other app needs to sync with Health Connect.

Absolutely love this app! Having a busy schedule I am unable to get to a gym often so I set my garage up ...but then I didn't have the knowledge and easy got discouraged in my "what am I doing" mindset. This app has removed that issue for me and allows me to set a plan and stick to it. Shows you how to properly do the exercises and tracks your time, progress and activity! LOVE THIS APP!

This is a great tool for tracking your fitness progress. The free version is excellent and not annoying. I think it reflects that they are about getting paid for their work but also about people being fit and healthy and that is cool. What it needs for me to give it 5 stars: Stretches!! Sretching is a critical component of proper training of any kind, and for over all health. To have a myriad of strength building exercises without the appropriate complementary stretches available seems lacking.

It's a good app usually but suddenly editing exercises is impossible. it says they have locked day edit to keep my log safe, which I don't think was ever in danger, and then it says you can edit from the workout screen, but when you try on the work out screen, you get the same message. please undo this freeze and let me edit my workout! ** no, the logging screen says the day logs are locked to keep them safe.

Re-rating because support told me to. Still a great fitness tracker, but as long as a 24/7 push notification saying,"time for your next set" doesn't bother you, it's nice. Went and updated the app. It still keeps saying my workout is still running. Even well after syncing it. Tried uninstalling the app and re-installing. Still says I'm working out.

Convenient and has most exercises, but there are some bugs, for instance: The app is supposed to allow supersets of as many exercises as you want to link, but it can only link two max. There's no reply from the support as to how to fix this, and while it's hardly a deal breaker it IS annoying for my workout routines, and moreover is an advertised feature that doesn't work, so...

Trash. They recently keep giving me account problems and even though I paid for the premium, its not a reliable service. I keep contacting them for support but the problem happens every couple weeks. Only reason I'm still forced to use it is because my logs for the last few years had been here, but as soon as my membership ends, I'm canceling permanently.

I used the free version for yrs & decided to pay because its an awesome app. You can add tons of custom exercises & pics/gifs. Overall it's great. Some *minor* negatives I've found are that It's a battery hog (1hr workout drops me down 50%), interval mode doesn't usually work & won't load half my exercises, when you search through the library it doesn't combine their and your exercises (you have to click to another tab to see yours) & when making an exercise you can't choose weight and duration.

I have used Jefit for years and on the phone it's fine. But with my Pixel 2 watch and Pixel 7 phone, it just doesn't work well. The app is frustratingly just not ready for prime time. The dream of having your workout on your wrist is not fulfilled b/c it requires a sync with the phone app after your workout, which I'm fine with conceptually, but the sync frequently doesn't work, even after re-loading the app many times, being sure I'm on latest phone and watch firmware, etc.. Soooo frustrating!

I've been using and enjoying this app for years, but each update over the last 8 months has made it worse and worse. We've lost the ability to edit in-progress workouts, rep goals no longer hold, and how it appears impossible to manually add workouts to the calendar. It might be time to return to the notebook.

I originally gave a lower review because I didn't like the spin wheel for entry of weights and reps, but I'm glad to know that I can manually enter those numbers. This is extremely functional and easy to use for my workouts. It took me a long time to switch from the old-school notepad and pencil that I used to carry with me around the gym. This app made the transition very easy.

I've been using the app for ten years. Really like it. However the latest version is very very buggy. Also it would be nice if they included many common exercises and left out so many obscure ones. I have to use a LOT of custom exe removercises, and I've noticed, through the social features of the app, that many others do too. I'll be happy to give more stars if they fix the bugs.

Most excellent! Very easy to use and it has an extensive list of exercises to choose from. Been using this for about 4 months and definitely 5 star worthy. I would love to still see integration with MFP or the ability to track food and calories. But that's not a deal breaker. Edit: latest update removed my plans. Where the F are my plans?

The removal of the workout count down timer really killed it for me. I rely on this for metabolic overload exercises. I also use it to do split workouts. This needs to come back. I tried the work around but it doesn't work for me. 😔 Update 1/19/21-still no count down timer functionality. I need the audio alert to let me know when a set is done. I can't keep looking at the screen to see when an internal is up. Super unintuitive.

I love this app. I use it to plan and track my workout as well as set workout goals. My only negative is with stretches and yoga poses it's kinda hard to track; instead of repetition it should be tracked in seconds and you really don't need to rest between stretches. The timer should be used for the stretch rather than a rest period after the stretch.

Most impressive fitness app I've dealt with. EXTENSIVE workout lists with body part breakdowns, equipment variations and many workout splits to choose from. Or make your own custom list. Even the free version is better than 99% of other rip-off apps. Free version feel very complete. I don't see ever bothering with another app. Give it a try without any hassle. I didn't have any bloatware or email spams when I downloaded it years ago.

Love it! I've been using JefIt for almost 6-7 years now.... it is very user-friendly, it's easy to find and use their many prearranged exercise routines, and/or customize to your own lifting/exercise sessions ... I have recommended it to many many people, and most end up letting me know they use it and thanking me for the recommendation... :)

Can neither plan nor track. I needed a replacement for another app that was removed, but this one doesn't work. I added a community template. Clicked download and it tells me to sync with the mobile app. I found a sync button... somewhere on the app but it doesn't do anything that I can see. I tried, "Try it out" on the website.. takes me to a page I can't edit. Terrible. If they can take the time to make the app coherent to use, don't waste your time using this.

I was hoping to replace my pen and notebook with a decent app. However, this isn't it. First, it requires a login, and you are required to give it access to your contacts in order to use it. That's enough to leave a bad taste in one's mouth. The app us difficult to navigate if you aren't choosing one of their workout programs, most of which are focused on beginners. Creating your own custom workout proves difficult. For example, you can't delete an exercise that doesn't work for you or that you clicked by accident. You have to delete the entire day. This app is probably best for those who prefer canned plans, such as beginners and intermediates. It just isn't that friendly to the experienced who need custom workout plans.

I've used this app for prob 10 years. The rest timer has always been weird in appearing and reappearing. I've had it successfully disabled for months at a time, but lately I can't even get it to stay off until the next set. It gets in the way, and sometimes the pop-ups interfere with logging sets. Please just make a simple master toggle switch to turn it off everywhere.

Pros: The app has a good structure to let you set up custom workouts and keep records Gives you a total of weight lifted, new PRs, etc Cons: it's buggy. If you switch between apps during your workout it will mess up the numbers you have entered. Watch app is useless unless you are doing the same thing every without (no progress) It's not quick and easy to view past numbers on a lift for comparison. Form videos are useless at best. No actionable form tips. Lots of good lifts not included

Not 5 stars because for some reason, when I change screens during a workout, the timer stops. Also, when I skim ahead to check the next exercise so I can prepare, the timer automatically starts. other than that, I love the app because it has helped me lose 70 lbs so far. Update 19 Aug 2022: Still not 5 stars for the above, and when I do cardio (walking and elliptical training), the Insight section does not reflect any percentages under muscle groups worked.

Only really worth it if you upgrade to the paid version- even then its just a few premade workouts you can probably find online anyway. the other option is you could just make all your own workouts, which is probably what I'll do. downloaded this app because I hate carrying pen and paper and wanted something to track my weights. There are only a handful of free plans and they are mostly beginner level and short workouts, plus it's too complicated of an app for me to use just to track weights.

It does the basics extremely well. It has a great library of movements so you can build your routine and modify the sets and rest times, and will auto fill your last set. So far I've yet to really come across another app that managed to nail something so basic, they're just glorified paper logs. It will also let you set goals for the big movements, however something happened and it erased my goals and progress, and I can't seem to get it back, hence the 3 stars

Calories are overreported when syncing with Google fit. Eg: At the end of a workout, this app will say 176 calories burned. But the Google fit app with report a 379 calorie weightlifting session. Please fix. Manual entry is annoying. In response to developer's response: I've found another app that syncs calories correctly, so it's definitely possible with Google fit's API. Moving to that app. If you're a user that isn't concerned about syncing, consider this a 5 star review.

Jefit offers a free version, but only directs you to a paid version that forces you to sign up to an overpriced annual plan with no other option to proceed. An app like this should only need to be purchased once. There is no additional value that warrants a subscription model. You're better off with pen and paper than giving into another greedy company that provides "access" instead of a product..

I'm changing my review. The new UI is confusing & cluttered. The rest timer takes up too much of the screen. It was much better when it was just an overlay. I also much prefer the set timer auto starts & counts up. I'm thinking this update falls under the if it's not broke don't fix it category. It would be great if the option to edit current exercises in the database to add weight to those without a weight option and vice versa.

This app provides everything I need for a fitness tracking app. The free version is very generous. To the point I almost feel guilty not paying for it. In fact, I would consider paying for it, were it not for subscription model. Not a a big fan of that model and avoid doing business with companies that implement that pay scheme.

I'm giving this a low rating for misleading marketing. After spending two days entering custom exercises that weren't in the database, I find the free app caps the number of exercises you can mark as favorites, which limits greatly your ability to organize and scan exercises. That's fine, but not disclosing a limited feature until after a user has spent significant time on the app is not a business practice that I would reward with a monthly subscription.

Workout app that does what it says. I recommend this app(along with Cronometer for tracking)to family, friends, clients, and peers as one of the strongest tools in their arsenal to help with their journey. *1.3.24 5 stars to 2. I detest this latest version and want to go back to what it was before the app autoupdated. Editing weight/reps, 1RM #'s, and the set timer no longer work as seamlessly as before. Though I sent polite feedback, I too recieved the same generic reply in the recent reviews.

overall this is a better app than the one I was using, mosly because I can have more than three workouts without paying for it. What I would like to see is; if you put an exercise into a workout day and you like to change your sets & reps in another plan, that it not reset them in all the days or workouts that exercise is also in. It's extremely difficult to remember what you want to do if they keep resetting.

Hands down the best workout app I've ever used. You can create your own weekly workouts for each day of the week. The amount of different exercises in the app is crazy. And it shows you how to do them. Makes it super simple to track my progress and it's all free. There is even a cool points system you can use to upgrade your account if you workout frequently enough. All in all, an amazing app

I use this everyday for every workout. And I have yet to find an app that is more efficient and as detailed as JEFit. The data visualization is amazing and the metrics are a nice way to see progress over time. I would love to see extra modifications for more HIIT focused exercises. (logging sled sprints for instance is difficult since you have to chose between time/distance or weight).

Great app. Only thing I would add to it is the ability to shift exercise order once you add an exercise to a custom workout. Unless I'm missing how you do this, which is possible, the only way I see to change the order of exercises is to remove the exercises and then re-add them in the order you want.

Highly recommend this app- so, so useful in planning a workout routine. They have all kinds of pre-made plans in case you're not sure where to begin, and they make it really easy to make a completely personalized one as well. I haven't paid for the full version, so all that and more is available for free. The tracking features are also very useful!

This app just keeps getting better. I have been using it for literally years. I can't say enough goof things about this. You MUST have this if you want to make any progress at the gym. I wish I could keep giving this app 5 stars, I am always checking out other options for tracking weight lifting and this app just can not be beat Edit: I'm on like year 10 and this is still the best. Don't waste your time elsewhere. Just don't.

Great app! It's just hard to navigate if you want to deviate from a predetermined workout plan. It's confusing to make a log of exercises that you did for the day but not have a way to post your activity UNLESS you are following a preset routine. It's like 3 different ways to log exercises but only 1 or 2 actually give you the option (once) to share your process

Really in love with the app, thinking on becoming premium but the thing is that, when I'm about to be done with my workout on the app it ALWAYS freezes/slows to a snail's pace, I either have to wait for long periods of time for it to respond or get out of the app terminate the process and open it back up, my only complaint about it, everything else is awesome 👍

Love the app, been using it for like 7 years now? It's great for tracking your workouts, keeping you moving while you're at the gym, never really used the social aspect but I've heard that's good too. Since the last update I haven't been able to manually input amounts in the weight or rep box though I have to scroll up and down to the desired number. That's kind of annoying. Other than that it's great, 5/5 stars.

Great app which I've recommended to my friends. Some constructive criticism or features to work on: - Add the ability to have synchronized workout plans for those exercising together. - Function for adding friends should improve. Using the Jefit username is fine but should be more of a search rather than single stage request. - Separate units selection ie meters for distance, lbs for weight, inches for measurements

I've used this app for the better part of a year and though it gets buggy from time to time, in my experience, the inconvenience is short lived. I wish yall would being back the charts showing what you did for that particular day's workout (now it's just week, 14 days etc) Anyway. Thanks for all you do to make a user friendly app. Keep up the good work!

I've been using the app for eight plus years and got a smart watch about two/three years ago to use with the app. I only use the basic feature(s) of choosing the exercises and logging sets; nothing else. Being forced to pay $70+ JUST to utilize the watch as a companion, and additional features I will never use is a bit of a cash grab. If you all had a price point of say $1-2.99/mo to use the watch, or some sort of tiered approach, that would be worth it, but not $70. You lost a loyal user. 😔

I've used this app for 7 or 8 years, give or take, and the new update is TERRIBLE. WAY TOO COMPLICATED now. You can still do individual exercises on the fly but it doesn't log them for you. You have to do your set then go back and log it manually. Also, there's a timer feature that allows you to either add or reduce the time but if you do that, the alarm no longer sounds. There's many things the new update messed up but we're allowed only 500 characters. WAY too many other options. Moving on.

Extremely sluggish lately. Makes my entire phone slow down. Especially in the app switcher. What's going on? I have already tried reinstalling the app as suggested by support. Besides the performance issues that happened recently, this is a great app. But I can't fully recommend it until the sluggishness is fixed. Version 10.5, Android Pie, OnePlus 5t.

This is one of the best apps I've come across. It's certainly the best one for tracking workouts. You can pick pre-loaded exercises or create your own. You can create your own workout routines. It then tracks weight, reps, and/or time. The free version is essentially the same as the Pro version. The main difference is that Free allows you to create and track three or four routines, whereas for $30 a year, you can have unlimited routines. Either way, it awesome. Highly recommended.

I use the monthly paid version ($2.99 a month), and it's exactly what I needed in a workout tracking app. Plenty of pre-loaded exercises with animations and how-to descriptions if you need a reminder, plus the ability to create custom exercises. I also enjoy being able to take a workout plan from the app and edit it as needed. Support is also responsive and timely!

not perfect. it doesn't have every possible exercise or every variation, but it does allow you to create exercises, and though the creation method isn't %100 comprehensive, it works with most exercises. also, it won't allow you to put more than two exercises in a single superset, which is slightly annoying, but doesn't really affect the results. overall not bad, and helps track improvement. edit-just found that I can only save 4 workouts and create 7 exercises until hitting paywall. down 1 star.

Looked at several much more expensive apps and just could not justify the price tag. Thought I would give these guys a try since the premium was so affordable. Best. Decision. Ever. Has seriously helped me up my game and reach my goals. Too many positives to list here, but seriously, just try the free version and if you like it, drop the $25 for a year. I bet you won't regret it.

I've tried a lot of different workout logging apps and this one has quickly become my favorite. Very easy to use at the gym and the api and web interfaces make it easy to plan workouts. One thing I would love is a widget that would let me check off a set and see my rest timer for my active workout. Not sure how hard that would be, but it would be convenient for me. Thanks for a great app!

I was using AI for a while for my workout tracking, but it easily gets clogged with all the data and information being input over the course of weeks and months. Hevy addresses exactly what I need with logging weight and exercises, and it has a rich database of exercises. It would be nice to have the ability to complete my sets for exercises from the lock screen (Android), without having to buy a smartwatch.

I appreciate how easy it is to set up how I want to work out. I did a paper journal before and this is WAY better! I also appreciate (as someone new to strength training), how each exercise has a gif of how to do it properly and what muscle groups it is actually working. I paid for an annual membership, but I am so impressed that I plan to switch it to the lifetime option! This will ensure that I always have my past data to compare to.

Hevy is by far the best workout app I've used. It's easy for beginners and experienced lifters alike. Every exercise includes helpful descriptions, making it simple to learn proper form. The app is clean, easy to use, and the premium version is very affordable. Absolutely worth downloading! Love this app!!!

Usually really great. A wide variety of exercises and good "how to" instructions. However, every time I use the app for an extended period of time (such as when I am tracking a workout) I lose the ability to click on anything within the app. I can scroll through, but cannot tap the checkmark to complete the set, or tap the three dots to reorder my workout. The only way I have been able to fix it is by closing out the app. I have had to do it multiple times during a single 1 hour workout.

I really love the primary features of this app. I've been doing a recommended full body split with a few mods to swap out exercises I don't like. it's super easy to switch things up when machines are occupied or I'm in a different gym. I also really like the calculator. Occasionally, syncing with my Pixel watch gets weird, but that might not be the app's fault. I tried one of the AI recommendations, but I didn't find much use for it. The basics are great!

I have never been so consistent and so motivated to track my workouts! I love to track my personal records and be able to push myself. 💪 So many features, even in the unpaid version. Some of the math goes a little funny when you try to share it to Instagram stories (one rep max frequently doesn't make sense), and I do wish it had more calisthenic type exercises listed. But for free, it's an incredible app.

Almost perfect! So honestly this is a great app. very easy to navigate and use. as someone who's just starting the gym, it made it a breeze to log it. I also love how when I launch a workout with the app on my phone, it syncs it with my watch for heart rate and better calorie burn calculation. my only minor issue is I wish they would update the workout and keep adding more as if im doing a specific exercise and they dont have that one, I have to go for the closest similar one to track it

Great app! I highly recommend it. I became more consistent because of many reasons, like the muscle chart which shows which muscles I worked out over the week, and logging is pretty seamless. It's easy to replace an exercise in your routine while you're working out if a machine isn't available. Also, it displays your PRs and the amount of weight you lifted in a session, which is encouraging. I'm very impressed with the team. They're pretty active on Reddit too, constantly seeking feedback.

Decent app. Wish there were filters to follow people based on age, country, etc. Tough to find people at my age and fitness level otherwise. The profile comparison feature is nice, but limited to only barbbeĺl exercises which is a poor design choice. Statistics are fairly thorough but a tad clunky IMO. Restricted to four saved routines for free version (understandable) is a tad limiting. Very responsive team on the bug reports. Overall, a good fitness tracker.

Love the fact it works on my watch. Not having to bring my phone around the gym is really convenient. Sometimes it doesn't sync correctly. Not sure which product in the Google chain is responsible for adding the information to Google Health (formerly Fit) but more information would be nice. Finally, it is REALLY annoying to scroll through minutes and seconds to enter time. There needs to be a way to type time in. This was the first gym training app and I won't switch to anything else.

Great tool for keeping track of your routines, easily edit and add what you want per week or if your feeling a little gassed out removing sets and exercises is really easy but keeps your routine the same for thr next time if you want. I appreciate the alerts too after rest times are up so if your caught day dreaming to a song it gets yah back on track. going to buy the lifetime membership once my 3 month tracking runs up, just so I can keep the progress logged long term.

Best free app for tracking workouts and measuring progress. Used a different app for a while until they started locking exercises unless you paid for a premium account. This app has a huge library of exercises with a bunch of different variations and anything that is missing you can add yourself as a custom exercise.

Tried to create a routine and the moment I had more than a couple exercises in it I had to start saving and updating the routine after adding an exercise. Then going back in and editing it to be able to add sets or more exercises all the buttons in the UI of the routine would not let me add more sets or exercises after adding one or the other. Glad I didn't go with Pro and waste money on it.

I used to rate this app at 5 stars, but now I'm giving it a 1 because of multiple bugs or other issues. I filed a support request but just received an AI-generated email. I often can't save workouts or even log exercises. Usually the app doesn't tell me why: it just silently fails to register the button press. Other times their servers seem to be down. When I save a workout, I might see a spinner for a long time.

I absolutely recommend this app, makes tracking my daily workouts super easy, you can see your progress easily, see stats, get monthly reports,... and no ads, no horrible paywalls for premium version (there is a premium version for this app, but the standard version is very comprehensive and super easy to use that it doesn't feel like you're missing out). will keep using this app, and keep telling all my friends about it.

Great app, but needs more exercises and content: different grip handles for machines, exercise variations, rest-pause logging, maybe even logging partial reps, etc. It also needs to allow you to save past notes you submitted for a previous exercise without having to retype them (it shows a preview only)! Unfortunately I will probably have to move on to another app that has more content near the end of my subscription. Hevy is great, but it needs some love.

This is the best workout app by far. It's like Instagram but it doesn't rot your brain. Not only is it easy to use, accurate, and free; it also encourages you to keep going to the gym by tracking your workout streak. The app also offers short gifs showing proper workout form and the muscle groups beind worked in a given exercise. It even shows you tips on proper form, and allows you to leave notes for yourself. I personally use it to remind myself to focus on my form.

Great app for tracking your workouts and inexpensive compared to other apps (You pay the same amount for lifetime access as you would for a year of other apps). Granted, this app does not create workouts for you... but, in my mind, ID rather go to a free AI product and create a workout there and pay less here than pay a stupid amount of money to have the apps AI make something up. Plus, this way I have a lot more control over the final workout. Definitely worth a download to check it out.

Really great app. The free version has literally everything I need to track my workouts. I love how easy it is to use. I can swap exercises in my program with a couple taps. I love the exercise library and pre-con programs. There's also a plate math calculator!! And you can customize it!! No ads, no bloat, just a great tool. The social aspect is an added bonus! Don't sleep on this app.

I love this app for tracking my workouts! It's so easy to use and customize. I love that it syncs with Strava, too, since the cardio tracking isn't quite as good as the weightlifting tracking. It's super fun receiving medals for progress! It provides helpful info on correct form and has pretty much every exercise you could want. If you have a lot of different routines, you'd need to upgrade, but I'm perfectly fine with the 4 free ones.

Absolutely love this app. I love that it includes animations about how to do specific workouts, and I love all the growth tracking features. I feel like I've made more progress in the last two months with this app than I have in years without it! There is also a certain "game" element that I enjoy, in that I am constantly trying to beat my own records. It also contains tons of premade exercise routines. I never pay for apps, but this one was worth it.

I would like a larger variety of exercises that don't require equipment as much. I only have a bench and some adjustable weights. So I feel like my overall options are limited much like the space I have to work out. so far for having the app for a week I do find it convenient and easy to use and they don't try to price gouge you every month with barely anything to offer. 🧐👍

The best tracker app for real lifters. It's clean, ad-free design is simple and easy to use. I love the Routines feature, where you can create custom workouts with detailed sets, supersets, rest timers, and everything else. The database contains every exercise you can think of, and the insights after each workout are fun and rewarding. It can also seamlessly sync with Galaxy Watch. I recommend this app for anyone who wants to cut out the fluff in their lifting and wants to see real progress.

This app is great! it has everything you need to track a workout. logging exercises is easy, reviewing a graph of your past weight on each exercise is available to track progress. I use it for two separate routines at different gyms where the available weight machines are slightly different and I have to adjust the weight - Hevy reminds me of the previous weight I lifted on that routine which makes it easy for me. And, all that is on the free version!

I recommend to everyone I meet! This is user friendly and helps you with exercises if you don't know how to do them. I love you can add in your own exercises or equipment that is not in the app already. I barely tried the free trial and loved it, so I signed up for a year, but after that I may upgrade to lifetime! I love all the real life weight comparisons!

Honestly such a great app. I have been working out for literally years and have not gotten the progress I've had since I started using Hevy. It makes tracking so easy and the muscle distribution chart and heat map help me to see what areas I need to work on and what are getting overworked. If you're a gym goer, this is the app for you. Tell your friends and compare progress and exercises!!

I love everything about this app. It gives you data about what you do, never its opinion about whether you've gone too hard or not hard enough. It tracks your lifts over time, and celebrates your PRs. Thoughtful features include rest timers, a thorough list of lifts with written and video instructions for each one, and very minimal social feed. I show this app to all my gym buddies, because it's so useful without any unnecessary bells and whistles.

Very easy to use and has all the exercises I'll need. I use free version. That being said, the reason for 3 stars is that how the app measures some workouts should change. Lunges should include body weight in volume lifted. It would be nice if there was a record for total volume lifted for one exercise in a workout, not just for one single set. Sled push should include weight added, not just distance.

I have always logged my workouts for the sake of keeping track of my progress. The problem is that I use to write everything down - the old fashioned way, pen and paper (I know, I'm old). I decided to give this app a try for the sake of efficiency and convienence. Long story short, this app did not disappoint. It has everything in it I could ask for. I don't have a lot of experience in workout apps, but I know I have no need for a different one. Great job Hevy! - A consistent user

I like that you can copy workouts. Modification is easy. I like being able to take notes and trending. My biggest issue is that something changed in the last month so that when I change a weight it will not let me complete the rep unless I log out and log back in. It makes logging more time consuming in real time. I use the free version so I just suck it up.

I have the payed version, I like the selection of equipment it matches my gym pretty good, the trainer work really good, rotates exercises and equipment so it doesn't get stale and also gi e you replacement options if there's something you don't like or if a piece of equipment isn't available, seeing the past logs and set ratings helps alot, only thing i don't like is it doesn't have some of the past history if I switch routines.

Great App for tracking routines and workouts especially when it comes to progressive overload. My only complaint would be content wise. Lifting the same truck gets boring. Maybe an Oak Tree, 4,000 Large Pumpkins, or 100 Sofas. That way, it's more of a game as to what you unlocked that day. Otherwise, a very solid app. I've lowered my rating by one star because I've experienced a few bugs and there's no way to catalog an entire workout program.

Before I downloaded Hevy I found it so difficult to actually track my progress. This app organizes it so well and makes each is so simple to track your progress in individual exercises, muscle groups, etc. And building your own workouts as a beginner is also incredibly easy as it will filter exercises for your needs to help you get started. I've been using it for about 2 years now and have put on significantly more muscle since starting the app.

Having a plan going into the gym is important for me. This app helps me build that plan, and review past performance, in order to adjust and make improvements. Super easy to use and helps keep me on track and focused. I would love for the free version to allow more than 4 preset routines to be built by users, but with the "open workout" option, you can change it up when you need to from your primary setup. I like seeing the data it provides and sharing functionality.

This app is AMAZING im the type of person that when I go to the gym I want to get in and get out. The fact that you can load your workout and check it off is amazing no more guessing or trying to remember what you did ITS ALL RIGHT THERE and the fact that its free is insane. So far I've lost 25lbs and I know this app had alot to do with it by keeping me on Track😉

Great app that can track a full routine for free. No ads so far, and the subscription is inexpensive if you want to track more then one routine. Adding workouts to the routine is simple and they have have a good selection of routines for everything from beginner to advanced workouts. Each workout also shows a simplified gif and explanation of how to do the workout.

Honestly this was the secret sauce to seeing real progress in the gym as I use the app to record every weight and rep and am able to increase it every or every other week by 2.5-5lbs. On top of it you're also able to organize and create entire workout routines for yourself. Would definitely recommend, even better if you have other gym buddies so that you can compete a bit with each other using its social media features

I used to track all my workouts using an actual physical journal. one of the influencers that I follow on Instagram recommended hevy I made the switch and I'll never go back. the free version allows you to create four routines that you can utilize and if you pay for the subscription you can use even more it's worth every penny but the free version is great for beginners or intermediate lifters.

Really good app for tracking workouts. It's easy to setup routines, find the right exercises, and track your progress, what muscles you've used, etc. Everything is easy to navigate and use. I've been using the free version but might upgrade just to support such a great app.

Love the free, no ad functionality, been using it for a week and im considering buying the premium (option for one time purchase is exceptional). App functionality as well as tracking is intuitive and simple, integration with other health tracking apps and wearable support is straightforward, overall an app built for the user, I wish more companies used this kind of business model, they'd make a lot more money

I've been using this for 4 months. I like that I can set my own routines and it updates with the weight/reps I did in my last session. It makes it easy to keep track of progress. The app also makes it really easy to replace an exercise with something similar when the machine/equipment may not be available.

Been using this app since ~2021 and it has been amazing overall. Likes: Tracks historical data, showing progress and strength growth. Allows for customized workout plans you can craft around what is available to you. Fun little ribbon-achievements every time you hit a new high weight per lift. (It's very motivating!) Lifetime pass is reasonably priced (and I highly recommend it). Dislikes: AI coaching plan that comes with premium could be more customizable. overall highly recommend this app.

whatever happened to being able to change the rest time, completing sets, or anything while you have a routine running is broken. it's so frustrating to try to check off a set but even after 10 taps it doesn't go through. I tried turning the rest time off in case it was some sort of conflict in timers or something but this main thread be going wild here

Absolutely love this app, it works great in helping you track your progress. The only true issue that I have with this app is that I copy my previous workouts for routine and not always do I complete each exercise due to time but keep them in for the next week.. that being said even if those exercises are not checked off per set, the app is still tracking those towards my volume lifted at the end thus not truly accurate in thw end. Would love to see this updated.

This app makes it super easy to keep track of your workouts, gives you a monthly summary, and is overall simple to use, and all using the free version. They also reverted back to manual time entry, which is so much easier than using the scrolling wheel, so that's an easy 5 stars from me.

Easy to use and great for beginners. I liked the preloaded routines to start and create my own routines in the app now that I'm more comfortable in the gym. I like the progress tracking graphs, it motivates me to see that I've gotten stronger. A lot of great features for a free app!

I was looking for a new weightlifting log app that had more than just the basics. I love the simplicity of the interface and the ability to discover new exercises. The user interface has intuitive options to quickly record your sets and add new exercises on the fly.

straight up love this app. the ease of tracking workouts is amazing. I've tried quite a few and nothing comes close. I love the ability to easily see the weights Ive used just recently. I've always had issues I get to the gym and have to start essentially anew. 100% worth the price.

I've been using this app on my phone and watch for a few weeks. its a good app with decent user interface. were it falls short for me is: A) widgets on phone. my homescreen is 5 tiles across and their widget is 4, with no option to adjust length. B) on watch after I've started a workout, to allow you to see what you done the last two sessions on each exercise. c) to add suggested weight increases based on previous workout on each exercise, viewable on the workout itself after you've started.

This is the app I always thought should exist, but I had no idea it would be better than my imaginary app. Truly, easy to use, easy to track my workouts, easy to see my schedule, easy for me to show off to my friends. I still have the free version, but I may upgrade soon. The free version is great, but there are a few more features in the paid version. Definitely recommend.

App is overall great and easy to use. being able to track my workouts and see what muscles are being targeted, helps keep me motivated. My only criticism, is that I wish they included more workout options. For example, ball ball or palates bar kit exercises. This app tends to be geared more towards those would have gym equipment or actually go to a gym. Still, it's an overall good app.

Great app. The free version is good enough for most people, supporting up to 3 workout routine rotations. I decided to upgrade to the paid version to have the flexibility of having more routines. I love the tracking, the timing between sets, ability to do supersets (no breaks between 2 or more workouts) and the tracking of your best overall lifts. This app helped me gain 10 lb of muscle in 8 weeks. Current total gain is 15 lbs of muscle. Goal is 30 lb 💪

after using the free version for a couple of months I moved on to the Pro version and updated my review from 4 to 5 stars. for a few bucks a month, it's a no brainer. the app works very well and syncs seamlessly with my watch during workouts. I track 3 90-minute sessions a week with 32 sets over 8 exercises each session, mainly all with my watch. love the stats and reports as well. haven't found anything major to complain about.

Super simple and straight forward. I just wanted a way to track my workouts like a spreadsheet but needed the spreadsheet to be on steroids, and this is exactly that. I barely found out today that the app also has a way to track not only your weight but measurements of multiple body parts and appendages, like my neck, my back, my... you get the point.. it's super dope. I bought the lifetime membership. 100% worth it cause I can have all of my routines and variations of them stored in there.

The app is really good, makes updating and tracking your progress workout to workout easy. My only complaint is that I wish their was a way to create a routine from your current workout, currently you'd have to remember your weight and rep ranges, plot them all down in the tracker, and save it. would be very convenient to just have an option added to the drop-down menu during your current workout, other than that though Hevy is great.

This app is great. I was using the bodybuilding app and when they updated it last year, it completely ruined it and I was on the hunt for a better app. This app is the best one I've ever used. It tracks your progression, personal bests, best volume, literally anything. It even adds up the total weight lifted and will tell you if you have lifted a truck, dinosaur, yacht, and more. One thing I would love to see in the future is badges or trophies or anything like that.

I switched from Strong to Hevy at the behest of a friend who wanted to see my workouts and it's been a real upgrade! I even upgraded to the paid version when they had an end of year discount and haven't regretted it. I'm a real sucker for data visualization and it's been cool to see myself improving over time in graph form. Have only used it for a few months but so far it's been fantastic!

Really great workout app. A wide selection of exercises even if you don't have that much equipment at home, you can easily find some exercises for every muscle group. I believe the app recently improved supersets workouts. I love the scroll to the next exercise feature, especially useful with supersets,so I don't have to wonder around what is next.

I really like this app. I use the free version for about 30 days before I decided I wanted to upgrade to Pro. I think the free version would be great for someone who only works out at one gym, but I use multiple gems so this allowed me to create different routines for each gym and Unlimited Custom exercises. you have to dig into the settings a little bit to get your rest timer working properly, and just to get things the way you like them to be as far as reading your effort and things like that.

I don't write reviews for anything, except Hevy. It's the only gym tracker I've stuck with. It's easy to use, definitely worth the one time purchase for the additional workouts and custom exercises. My favorite is at the end of a workout seeing exactly how much I've lifted and what that compares to (like a T-Rex!) Thank you for making an app for the gym that isn't bloated or gamified, just gains! ❤️

I'll start by saying I really like this app and recommend it to all my friends and students. But I'm starting to get irked with the lack of integration with Garmin because I can't track my heart rate otherwise. So then I have to have 2 trackers running at once and monitor both. I'm also annoyed that exercises like Kettlebell Swings aren't accounted for in the muscle group breakdown. I feel like that's an easy fix. You give primary and secondary muscles for everything else. Why not these?

Great app. One small problem. I love this app. It makes tracking my workouts super easy and it plays well with my other fitness apps, however, the WearOS version drains my watch so fast. I don't know if it's tracking more than the Fitbit app does, but I can track a whole hour and a half workout with the Fitbit app, and still have enough battery to last the rest of the day, but with this app tracking my workouts, I'll get down to 15% and have to charge again before I go to work.

solid workout tracker. you can use an AI plug in to work out a progression routine. overall just like my old notebook and pen. except much easier to read. for just wanting to track your weights reps and volume can't beat this. I like this app so much I paid for pro Even though the free version is completely functional for all my use cases. keep up the good work!

I absolutely love this app. It's given me a way to keep track of my lifts, build out plans, doesn't constantly ask me for money, and the free version does everything you need it to. Can take notes about my workouts, can add in modifiers, can even make your own excersizes if you know how to import the right files. Wouldn't have the progress I've made so far without it. Easy win dog.

Love it. Setup routines ahead of time. Track reps, weights and sets as you go. Notes are visible right where you want them. The data from the previous workout is right on the screen so you can modify if needed. Rearrange or modify the routine on the fly if a station is not available or a different hit is wanted. Keep changes or discard them after. Very large exercise library as well the ability to add custom exercises. This is the perfect tool to use in the gym and when planning at home.

I've used the free version of this app, for about 4yrs now. It has a large base of exercises and workouts. Options range from Free weights, machine weights, kettlebells, resistance, body weight, and cardio. All options have a "How to" for reference. It allows you to track workout time, Reps, Sets, weight, failure/warmup, supersets, has a section for notes, and you can save/view all prior workouts! I LOVE IT and I would highly recommend!!

I've used a variety of workout tracking apps, and this is the best one I've used. I've been using this app exclusively to track my workouts for over a year now. It's worth paying for the upgrade a you can use to customize and build multiple routines. It keeps track of all of my workouts for every type of exercise and allows me to track the weight, sets, reps, and volume since I began using the app. I really like how customizable and easy it is to adjust my plans.

Had the app for 3 weeks and I'm very impressed with it. It's what I thought I would get with Zepp and the new Fitbit interface, but they failed miserably. Heavy allowed me to build my own routines for each day and then it auto fills your previous weights and reps each exercise so you aren't having to keep a log in a notebook. That allows me to push my limits and get stronger and grow my muscles. Highly recommended!

really love the app and the wide variety of exercises to choose from when creating my workouts. the one thing that would move this to a 5 star would be the ability to have more than three workouts saved. it's not a huge deal but I'd love to be able to see older programs and know what I did so I can modify new programs. aside from that it's a great app

lots of exercises to choose from or create your own. let's you create up to 4 individual workouts for free version. being able to add notes to the exercise, set an automatic timer. and reflect at the end of your workout are great features. If you are new to workouts, use the reflect feature and be honest about your struggles, then come back to them after a few weeks of working out and compare yourself to that former person.

I have been using the Hevy app for over a year to track my progress lifting weights. I find it very helpful with planning routines, selecting equipment and exercises, as well as daily tracking completion. It helps me remember my routines and keeps track of previous accomplishments. The reports and statistic are also useful to evaluate progress.

I'm a week/ 3 workouts into the free version of Hevy. The best part for me so far is the motivation of seeing other people showing up! If they can do it, I can too... it's fairly intuitive, I was able to set up my first weight lifting workout without a problem, and u can log other things like walking, yoga, skiing... I'm very happy so far. editing to add I've made it to 50 workouts and paid for a lifetime subscription! nothing but great things to say about Hevy app

worth trying if you need a free app. will allow up to 4 routines and pro is unlimited. 3 months of data stored for free and unlimited for pro. tbh, simple, intuitive and allows synchronization of health connect and other apps that track health. definitely enjoying the free and most likely will purchase the lifetime option for the pro, but like I said free is pretty awesome too. btw, speaking as a beginner lifter

I really like using this app for my weight focused workouts. It keeps track of all my stats and the little how to videos for each exercise are great for when I need a refresher. The suggestions for similar exercises is also very helpful. I like how the app tells you what your workout volume equates to in a real life object like a helicopter or elephant!

A great tool for the gym. Without Hevy, I was relying on memory to track numbers of sets/reps/weight, and lacking real data for improvements.
