This app is easy to use and highly motivating for me, being able to track new records for volume or weights. Easy to setup workouts and super simple to track progress as I go. Love it!

Honestly so easy to use and intuitive. I feel excited to get back into lifting as this takes a lot of the mental effort out of it lol. Love looking at the little body map to see what muscle groups I should work next for balance. I would love if you could change the sizes of the widgets, finish workouts without entering sets/reps, and if there were pelvic floor/womens health exercises added as well as niche things like foot strength, finger strength. Overall really great!

an amazing app for workouts. provides visual examples of the workouts for proper form, and allows for some different forms of excercise like failure sets as well as notes so I can remember alterations I did in a previous week. it also connects to my watch and keeps track of my heart rate. sharing my workout with a workout group im in also helps clarify what we are working on that day! its also very good for what is offered for free!

I love this app. The free version has plenty of options for me (just starting out) and its been so helpful with keeping me motivated. I recommend this app to everyone I know who is going to the gym. The only issue I've had is my settings not saving for my workouts. It honestly might be user error since I'm still finding my way around the app. I was able to download and use immediately with the built in workouts and that has been instrumental for me.

I really like this app, I started working out with a group of friends and this app lets us all participate and be present for each other in our workouts. It's great for finding exercises based on muscle group as well. I think this app will be a great help as I am starting to workout by myself, would recommend this to anyone in similar situations

Love the Hevy app! It’s super easy to use, tracks your previous weights, and gives you those fun alert medals when you lift your heaviest or hit your longest hike which is super motivating. I also love that I can create workouts for friends and family to follow on their own. Makes training more fun, more social, and way easier to stay consistent. Highly recommend!

First review I've ever written for any app. This app is incredible. Well thought out. Perfect for tracking workout progress. No annoying in-app ads for free version- (which actually makes me more inclined to consider purchasing the "Pro" mode to unlock full features). Glitch-free. Very user friendly. This is exactly the app I was looking for and has become an essential tool in my workout program. Zero cons to this app. Great job to the team at Hevy. Keep up the excellent work.

I use this app to track my workouts on my functional trainer with a barbell. It has access to many different exercises. It's just good to keep track of what you've done, what your personal best is, and just keep track of working out. It's a nice app, very user friendly, and has some videos that show the actual exercise, so that's pretty cool.

I am a beginner weightlifter but knowledgeable of the weights. just haven't weightlifted in a while. I had used a couple of other apps but they were designed more for Sirius bro type weightlifters. when I found Hevy, it provided me with the platform to track my workouts and easily swap out exercises. I'm using the free app and it's truly incredible, but I'm thinking about upgrading in the next month or so. from a technical perspective, no glitches, no issues with the app! Love it

This is the best workout app I've used, and it beats my spreadsheets. I can track my workouts well with it and it gamifies it by giving me a "total weight lifted" for the workout and for the workouts that week. This just makes me feel like a rockstar, and pushes me to work harder. Wrapping up an arm day that lifted 3.5 tons makes me feel like a monster. I'm currently using the free version, and that is working well for me, but will probably go to paid when my next workout program starts.

I've used dozens of fitness apps over the years - and Hevy is far and away the best there is. The interface is very mobile friendly, the access to demo videos is a great feature - but my favorite is the memory that displays your last workout. I cannot imagine even one additional feature they could include to make it better.

Great app for tracking your workouts, don't bother installing the companion app on your watch though because the watch version is straight trash clearly programmed by a 3 year old. Not only that, if you're trying to track your workouts using Samsung Health on your watch the Hevy app forces it to stop tracking randomly because it's (Hevy is) such a piece of junk.

The Hevy app is nice! It is a great app for logging and tracking your workouts. I use the free version and it's good. I would like more options for bodyweight / calisthenics / resistance bands options for tracking. If you have that feeling of needing to report or log workouts to see your progress then definitely give the Hevy app a try.

Really like the app, the tracking and progression monitoring has been game changing. I have recommended it to many ppl. While the exercise library is extensive, its missing some common movements. However, you can add a customs that get added to ur personal library. the cons are minimal but mainly consist around random ui errors, like closing out an edited workout session and only getting half of the option to update or keep appears on screen or a formatting limitations when adding custom img.

Love this app for tracking my workouts. It has everything I need all in one place. Not only can I track my workouts, but I can also send them to others when planning a group workout. The sample workouts are great, as are the animated tutorials for newbies like me. I will definitely recommend this to others.

really great app to track progress and log workouts and measurements. I'm part of a fitness group on FB and already got about 8-10 people to sign up and get the premium subscription too so we can keep track of ourselves and show off our progress to each other. 10/10 would recommend to anyone that goes to the gym.

I love this app. I'm new to the gym and it's really helped me build a consistent routine & track the metrics that matter to me so I can see my progress every time, & adjust sets reps and exercises on the fly. I also value the small animations of each exercise in case I have form questions & the muscle heat map to show what I've worked in each set and over the course of the workout and week. The comparison of my total weight lifted to a heavy object like a truck or a yacht is awesome!! LOVE IT!

this is my go-to app for tracking all of my workouts. It keeps my rest periods consistent and I can implement progressive overload because it always shows how much I lift it the time before. for anyone. just getting started or who is currently a lifter. this is a great app to track your workouts. if only they add a circuit or tabata timer, then we'll be in business.

The app is pretty great, I like how it lets you compare your workouts over time. I wish my optional post workout summary photos would always give me the star chart that measures the muscle groups. 5 stars if you guys could make it a permanent choice. Other than that, the app is pretty great.

Really enjoying Hevy. I’ve completed about 25 workouts and have seen solid strength gains. I like how it suggests weights and reps based on past workouts—it pushes progress without overdoing it. The PR tracking is motivating (volume and max weight). Logging feels smooth, the rest timer keeps me on track, and workout time estimates are accurate. I upgraded to the lifetime version after about two weeks and have been happy with it.

As an advanced lifter doing this solo,meaning no trainer, this app helps tremendously with tracking and organizing my lifts. I love the rest timer aspect to keep me from exceeding my resting periods!!! 10000% recommend if you want to track/log your workouts. bonus : it tells you the total weight you lifted for the day!! fun!!

it sucks that I had an iphone and switched to galaxy and my lifetime membership was lost. Also alot of the things on the iphone version are not available on the android version. It doesn't track total weight lifted, it doesn't have the integration to android fitness, not being able to select/deselect weights and machines, not being able to adjust rest time during a workout. it sucks the iphone version is so much more fluid and android version just seems over all much more basic.

I first used the Apple version of this. It's great. Interface is different but concept is still the same. Great app that tracks your workout and gives you solid workouts as well. I used to piece my workouts together from various magazines but embracing tech (slowly) this offers solid work for solid gains. It was recommended to me and I recommend it to you all.

I like how you can customize your workout instead of following someone else's plan. But there are many exercises not in the app. You can add your own images. But that is not convenient. Also, would be nice checking off the Ex and tracking nr of reps and weight increases. Sometimes someone is on the equipment at the gym. Need to skip the Ex and return later. No way to track was you already did

the iPhone/ Apple version is a million times better. I can't restore my account, and the exercises are COMPLETELY different. it's not very user-friendly either. You can't change exercises mid workout if something isn't working out. Way too many extra steps to make sure everything is set up correctly. made me end my work out early because of it. if the devs can make it the exact same as the one app for the iPhone, then it would be 5 stars, but this is pure torture.

Had the iphone version and it was much much better. It was intuitive and actually had the ability to build me a split like upper/lower. The android version has none of that and would only give me one day of workouts instead of three different ones. I had to build it myself. Pretty disappointed especially for the price. Put as much effort into the android version as you did the apple version.

This is one of the best apps I've found for workouts. It has so many exercises and tracking methods. I recommend them highly for that and I had an issue when switching from iPhone to android but their customer service is great. You have to enroll for premium and get the free trial but they responded via email before the first charge and got my issue fixed.

Do not get this app if you are on Android. Picked up this app because a buddy used it. Didn't realize the Android version would be different. Thought I'd stick with it, and it would eventually update. Almost 2 years later and I couldn't be more wrong. Any time the app is updated, your custom workouts get messed up. Either the exercise is removed or it changed to a different name. Why would you ever remove an exercise or change a dumbbell exercise to a barbell exercise?? Why not keep both??

This is a great app to build workouts and track progress to begin with. Not sure if it was from an update or whatever the case may be but all of my preset workouts and progress has been removed from the app, this is about the third time experiencing this. Please provide some way to back up progress via email or cloud.

After trying out the iPhone version the Android feels incomplete cause there are options missing. Not sure why they made this one different from the Apple version. One thing I don't like and would like to see added is that when you skip days going to the gym, the app continues your workouts where you left off. Also to be able to see the rest of the week's WODs.

The Android version of this app is HORRIBLE. My wife has the iphone version and loves it. She showed me all the features it has - setting the amount of time you have for a workout, specify the equipment available, etc. I was sold, so I immediately bought the lifetime plan for Android. I was incredibly disappointed to find out nearly all those features are missing and the Android version crashes and loses progress constantly. I tried asking for a refund after a day but dev didn't respond.

I need an app that doesn't require a live internet connection, because my gym doesn't have public WiFi. And when I try to open this app anyway, it crashes and says "something went wrong." Probably because I opted not to rate an app that I had only had open for five seconds. Well, now instead of the five star rating the app was getting for, it's getting one star instead.

Awesome workout app for tracking and programming but huge downfall is the support. I purchased yearly membership and then a lifetime membership but if you get a new device/phone, your subscription doesn't transfer. After paying for lifetime membership and then changing phones the app wants me to pay for another annual membership. Sent emails and texts to Fitness22 support but haven't gotten a reply. Do not purchase if you plan to change phones within the membership period. No online support.

Not sure if it's because I switched from Apple to Samsung or if it's because I hadn't used the app in about 6 months but coming back it is nowhere near as good as the previous iteration. The workout plans no longer include 5 or 6 day plans, they're nowhere near as well thought out, and trying to edit them is no longer as convenient. It was the best app available 6 months to a year ago, now I'm not renewing when it expires.

I got results in 3 weeks of consistent workouts. I tried a few apps, this one has great variety, options, and pushes me to try new lifts. I'm an experienced lifter who doesn't want a personal trainer, this app is perfect: pre planned workouts that really work. Improvements: 1. I can't pause a workout, so if I have to finish a workout later in the day, it's gone. 2. Other apps can graph your progress in a clean visual 3. Once you modify a workout u can't see the original suggestions.

Its a great app and has a ton of great workouts, and what you get for the price is a good deal. i just wish they had some workouts that completely focused on part of the body because i like separating my workouts. For example, have an arms workout, a legs one, back, core, chest, etc.

So happy the major update from Workout to Gymverse is finally on Android! Support is amazing- I had the lifetime license from iOS and recently switched to Android. I reached out to support and they were able to provide a code quickly. Thank you! The workouts in this app are great. I've made a lot of progress with this app where other apps have failed me. Give it a shot- it's worth it. Yes you really need premium. It's still cheaper than most other competing apps.

I think its a great app for the most part. Few bugs that need worked out I will occasionally get kicked out of my workout making have to reinput everything back in to catch up. Its not as easy as I would like, to make your own custom work out or add exercises that aren't programmed in the app but otherwise its a great app that helps me stay on track with my goals.

Trial needs credit card, so I couldn't even use it to workout. However, playing around with the User Interface, it doesn't seem like such a bad tracker. The exercises tab shows you every muscle you can target, then tapping on them shows you exercises for those mucles. It seems to have a really good variation in workouts to suit both gym and/or calisthenics. Had I been able to trial the app properly, I think I might have used it a lot for training.

Still using it no further issues I can't say it's perfect but it's good. Had a few months now pretty happy with it until today when it randomly wiped all of my workouts, while I was lifting. frustrating to say the least. Best app I've used. Needs the option to chain more workouts together, additional sets allowed per exercise, the ability to add excersises and Arnold press really needs to be in the database. But other than that its great, no glitches no issues workout timer is awesome.

next to no improvement to the app over the last 2 years I've had it. I'd like to see you be able to edit workouts during a workout, without stopping the workout. Also getting tired of recreating my workouts everytime I get a new phone. There has to be a way to save it to the app. create an account and such. Overall I like the app I paid for lifetime membership, but definitely feel like I should have a flip phone everytime I use this app. UPDATE!!

love the app. I had it on iPhone before and thought it was perfect, I wish it was the same for Android as I noticed that I can't change exercises in my workout after I've pressed "start workout". on iPhone you could swap an exercise even after you've started your workout. Would love it if they could do the same for the android system

I thought it would be like the one on apple. Yet, it ain't on the apple version you can do so much and even choose which version of workouts you can complete home or gym equipment. Add workouts and check the progress of your journey. It was a disappointing purchase I'm going to see if I can get my money back because on this version stinks like poo.

App is definitely... tracking things. It keeps track of weight, what exercises you do, how much, ect. Some excercises don't appear in the look up, and it seems to prefer doing full body days over setting up multiple different days for different areas of the body. I'm very annoyed I spent 60 dollars for an app that many others surpass for free. Will be requesting a refund.

Great experience and worth the price. I was able to change out workouts on this app when I didn't have the equipment. I like that you can change/ record reps and weights. I enjoy the ability to create supersets. You can also choose rest time and the clock automatically starts when you hit done with a set. I also enjoy how there are so many options and guides for all levels. Reminders to increase weight. Only negative is if you get a new phone or delete the app all progress is lost.

I love this app so much, because it gives me a variety of workouts that I would of never though of. Plus it gives me a visual demonstration on how to do the workout. I now don't have to spend $99/month on a trainer (or more) when I can pay a one time fee on this apps and get an unlimited amount of workouts and meal plan options I need. You can do it all yourself here.

we purchased the lifetime membership and loved it! then switched from iOS to Android and everything we read said you can't move your membership over. we were bummed and tried other apps that weren't nearly as good. I decided to reach out to support and they sent me a code to activate on the Android so we didn't lose our membership! excellent customer service and best workout app out there!

New Phone- New everything. I had this app on my iPhone and when I got an android I went straight to purchasing the year subscription. But I slowly started to regret it because it's not as advanced or offer as much as the iPhone version. Quite disappointed about that. I hope they release the same version as iPhone or else I'm just gonna feel like I got ripped off and that's on me.

One of the better fitness apps I have used. The personal trainer keeps your work outs varied and keep you for the redundancy of the same routines. Seems to focus on full body which is great for my individual goals. Helps you keep track of how much weight you do at each exercise and even has an "increase weight" if your last set was too easy so you remeber next time yiur at the gym. I will say I am disappointed that they have put no effort into updating their app to make it better since 2020.

Boy am I glad that there is an app like this again. A few years ago I got in the best shape of my life following an app like this, but one day it disappeared. I'm glad to have found an app that helps me keep on track and tells me what to do in the gym. The workouts are complete and easy to go through in about an hour given the gym traffic. The assisted photo gifs on each workout help me do the exercises correctly if I have any doubts. 5 of 5 stars. JUST don't go dissappearing on me-Active Member

Highly inferior compared to IOS version. Purchased lifetime subscription while on android and life was good. Then switched to IOS. I wasn'table to restore my purchase so, I bought the lifetime subscription for IOS. The IOS version is so much more intuitive and the workouts are considerably better. I'm back on android hoping the app had been updated. Sadly it's still the same app it was when I left two years ago. In those two years, the IOS version had several updates and improvements.

I really like the pre-developed workouts for when I'm not wanting to make up my own, but there's something that drives me crazy with this app. Exercises need to be updated and added. When you search for something like a snatch, you only find barbell snatches, but what about single arm snatches? Search for treadmill, it's only running. Search for the stationary rower, limited options. There are also limited cardio options, and adding more would be nice.

For being so expensive, you would expect a flawless experience with the app. No way to track workouts/progress with graphs. It constantly loses track of my progress during a workout routine even when saving. When going back into the app during workout, can't click on the notification, have to open from the app icon itself or recent apps. Not efficient.. I'll change my rating once see improvements

Love this app, but I would really enjoy seeing a create your own workout feature. Edit: I still love it, but I am disappointed that every once in a while, if I switch to another app, the workout I'm currently doing cancels out, forcing me to restart collect data for the workout. This happens pretty consistently.

Great app, just missing integration capability. It has great exercise routines for every part of your body, plus you can build your own routines. For the yearly membership, this is a great deal, the interphase is well design but it is missing a very important part for me, the ability to connect to other apps like Fit, Samsung Health and so on. If it had that option, this app would be wonderful. Hopefully that feature can be added before my membership ends of not I may have to go.

This is a good app. Two suggestions to make this a 5 star rating. These apply to the personal trainer routine. - Option to switch out exercise for alternative working same muscles, e.g., for when gym does not have equipment for default exercise - Option to register workout completed with one click. After one or two workouts, I have the routine memorized, so prefer not to take phone with me and just register completion of workout afterwards. As it is, need to click for every set and rest.

Grandfathered in lifetime premium before they took it away and found out that it actually was never an active subscription. They were kind enough to refund my purchase though. However, adding in custom exercises is difficult along with customized supersets. Additionally, when using the notification tray to complete sets or to maximize the app, the app will frequently crash or not display any active workout meaning any work done up till that point is lost.

I've reach out to these guys seeling a little more info regarding the workouts. No response also the personal trainer program rested without me doing it . So I had to restart. Also the rest period is not enough time if you are lifting heavy. Sometimes the timer will disappear and all u will hear is the bell . So 3stars for me. But at least it's not a monthly subscription you have the option to pay a one time fee. I hope 🙏

Love the app and flexibility of it. I've been looking for an app that's easy and let's me create my own workout routine with rest timers, etc. The only reason I'm giving 4 stars instead of 5 is because the exercise database is sort of limited. If the exercise isn't in the database, you can't create it from what I've found.

It appears you can only get certain workouts on certain days. For instance, legs only on Thursdays. but if you don't workout on Thursdays, you won't do legs that week. The app didn't used to have that problem. Don't waste your money on this app. After reading reviews, it must be because I have an android. I wish I could get my money back.

It's alright but not great, the push button when you slide down the notification if you're in another app doesn't work have ti come back to the app to select did it. Ended up just building my own workouts in the app and that's alright for weight tracking and rest time tracking but other than that I'm sure there are better apps for the money

So far it's ok, I bought the premium for a year before even using the free trial. To start, you have to figure out your weights which I guess is understandable. If you try to do workouts other than the one they tailor for you, it gets kinda confusing. Alternates between 2 days but there's 6 blocks. No real guidance on the app. Also during my tailored workout today the app glitched 4 different times, forgetting where I was in the process and making me start over. Fairly frustrating.

If I was just rating for the iPhone it would be 5 stars with nothing but positive things to say. But after switching to android I am disappointed, you can tell they put zero effort on the android platform. It's clunky, NO WAY to track progress like weight or inches, harder to swap exercises, different plans all together & they aren't well thought out, no option to have a woman as the video display or video walk thrus. Would happily change it back to 5 stars when they get it together on android

Not bad for the process. If you're looking for something to get you started then this is the perfect app. I like it for simple fact of the tracking purposes prevents you from have to carry a paper log. While many of the exercises are just basic exercises it does give you details on how to conduct the exercise correctly. I have not used it enough to experience to see how in-depth the app actually goes

Doesn't seem to have very many exercises compared to something like Jefit. Though it's nice to be able to put in custom exercises with your own photos. Can't do that in any other apps I've found. Seems decent for a beginner rather than someone a little more advanced.

One Issue There are some exercises I do that are not in the app. Not a problem but there needs to be a way to type in custom exercises that dont exist in the app. For example: plate raises are nowhere in the app. Because there's no way to add an exercise that isnt preloaded into the app I can't add it to my workouts or track my progress.

I really wanted to like this app, but whoever developed it is an idiot. In what world can someone superset 2 timed workouts with no transition time between them? Does this developer have their own private gym, because most of the world doesn't and can't move from one piece of equipment or workout to another with no transition time to account for... and no 45 seconds of rest time to also transition and select weights is not enough. There is so much more, not enough space to write it.

They're getting 1 star because the customer service is trash. My app was somehow deleted. I tried to restore it and the option wasn't available. I emailed them because I have used the app for about 5 years and didn't want to lose the 100 or so workouts i created. They emailed back saying they only provided customer service to premium plan members.

I've used this app for two years now after purchasing a lifetime membership and loved it since. After the first six months doubled the weight lifted in all principle exercises (Bench- press/ squat/deadlift). I had little experience at the gym prior with no attempts at planning. I've also had a couple of times I've required support and the team have been nothing but helpful. Would recommend to anyone.

Has always been my go to app for fitness. The recent change is a huge downgrade. now you can't easily see the weight by clicking on a workout at a glance. You have to start the workout. why remove basic features?? You also can't change the weight of an exercise without starting the workout. Awful update that's made me download and use a different app.

So on Apple I had a whole lot more features but with switch over to an Android device it just doesn't have the same bells and whistles. so today will be my last workout with the app i have been here since day 1 and thought I could get along with out the ability to swap exercises on the fly and adjust as needed but I can't. thanks for all the memories but I'm out

I've been using this app for the past 3 years on my iphone but since switching to Android I noticed some functionalities were missing such as swiching and easily editing the workout as I am working out, I can only do so before I start, also I noticed that my weights weren't increasing automatically for me when I had this function in the iphone version. But overall great app and programs

Gym Verse is a clean, user-friendly fitness app designed for beginners and regular gym-goers alike. It offers structured workout plans, progress tracking, and helpful exercise guides. The interface is simple and motivating, though advanced users may find limited customization. Overall, it’s a solid choice for staying consistent and organized in your fitness journey without feeling overwhelmed.

Update 6.37 seems to have fixed the constant crashes. Thanks. However, the "upgrades" are still downgrades. I only use custom workouts, but instead of it saving weight and reps, I now have to re-enter weight and rep targets for every set on every exercise, EVERY SINGLE TIME! Even with Smart Weights and Reps feature turned off! And even then only once exercise started and not in edit mode! Not only this, the ability to set your own rest period has completely disappeared! WHY?! Please fix Devs!

edit: 1 star now after update. They added ai weight and rep recommendations, which I don't need so I turned off. No problem right? Wrong. When doing my custom workouts now, the rep count is no longer what I did last time, but a range of reps I should shoot for according to the app. Inconvenient and waste of time having to now manually check what I did last time. edit2: and now even with the setting off, my weight and reps are getting changed. Avoid the app if you value reliability.

There are a lot of usability issues on the Android version compared to the Apple version. - harder to customise the number of days out of the week you will be working out to make a weekly plan. - cannot change a workout type during a workout if the equipment is in use. - no suggested workout weight to use on the workout type. - When selecting muscle groups to workout, you can't select twice if you want to super focus on a particular group. I do like the app but I'd like to see the apple feat

been really good to keep me motivated and user friendly! only down fall is it seems some things aren't available on Android that is on IOS... comparing on the Facebook group seems its a bit more interactive. nothing that would stop me from using it though. love to "share" function for workoit stats to try ro inspire friends!

WTH! I can't custom my workouts anymore! I spent ages inputting my new workouts only to find out ALL MY REPS AND WEIGHT disappear after each workout. this app went from 5 stars to 1 star till this gets fixed. I seriously have to purchase another app till it gets fixed. Hurry up please developers, been a loyal supporter 4 years already

removed a bunch of workouts. I'm assuming it's to put them behind a paywall. smh Update: added lots of exercises and you're able to make a workout plan without a pay wall! so rare in today's micro transaction filled world.

I love the simplicity of the app. Description of the exercises, record keeping and no boring sets. Only downside for me would be that I wish the optimal usage videos were available for more or all the exercises. It helps visually to correct the forms. Also, wish I could connect it to Samsung health or any other health system this way it would track burned calories. Right now I am manually keeping track.

paid for a yearly subscription on iPhone but bought an android recently. the yearly plan AND all data were lost. unreal that the paid aubscription for the same app can't be transferred between devices.

Love this. I've seen progress for sure! Update: The app just made a major upgrade, but the app keeps closing down when I'm in a workout. The app doesn't seem to be able to handle the upgrade. It's very frustrating.
The new update stinks. I don't want the AI messing with my workouts. You can turn it off but now the app is slow and clunky. Want the old version back please.
This android version of gymverse sucks, got subscription because it was amazing in iphone. Many of the features are not available in android version.
ruined the app with the suggestions. So confusing, and even when you put your weight it still shows suggested weight. Horrible idea!
previously I wasn't happy with this app as it was far out dated in comparison to the iPhone version. It has since been updated & great to use now. I've switch back over to this & loving it.
Very clean and friendly, to be perfect needs an option to choose different sounds.

I dont know if it's because of an update or if it's because I have this on an android device but I dont have the option anymore on changing the timer during the work out id have to leave my workout entirely or put a timer from my own phone .. also I dont have the option anymore to choose what muscles I'd want extra focus on? it's been a few months since I've used this app and everything has been so inconvenient it's still a good app just little things that change the experience up... :(

This app was awesome with Apple. The Google app is terrible. The Android version doesn't even have half the stuff the Apple version does. The Android version keeps cutting my workout off half way through and I have to enter all the information again. The Android app needs a lot of work.
Good gym app with lots of useful features. The exercise videos are clear and there are plenty of different workout programs to choose from. Easy to use and helps me stay consistent.
i switched from iPhone to Android , and unfortunately the experience here is 3 stars - giving 4 stars as it's 6 stars on iPhone

So this is a very basic app that has all you need. There's a choice between their fitness plans or a personal plan (2/3/4 times a week). Now it still lacks some stuff to be a 5 star app - if you want to edit a personal plan, there could be some options for alternative exercises (freeweights/machine/no weights or smt). You can add them yourself, but you need to know what's what first. Socials - sharing the workout stats is nice, but you can do it only directly after workout, not later.

Awesome app. My pocket personal trainer that I don't need to pay every month. I have been religiously using this app for quite a few months and I have seen gains. Improvements I can suggest are 1. There need to be stretching exercises included to flex the muscles either before or after workout. 2. Theee need to be alternative exercises just in case the machine or equipment is not available or busy in the gym. Overall superb app.

Taking one star, you should incorporate a sign in feature where people like me who have designed their own routine don't loose it once you change phone that would be mega cool and I'd be willing to pay a small fee aswell for it. Other than that this is the best app I've ever used. This app is amazing and i can Do full workout without having loads of different apps.

Long time user - this app is solid! I've been using Gymverse for a few years now; the exercise tracking, workout builder, and general exercise library (with great videos) is unmatched. The new UI is taking a bit of getting used to - but highly recommend to any one looking for a general workout tool. > Request to the devs: Would love the option to add a note to a general routine/a specific exercise that's part of a routine (ie. indicating hand grip, width for bar routes, or anything else)

I love the workout plans amd the interface in general. But you can never run this app on the background. If you ever open another app and come back to this one, it will crash and reset your workout routine, as if you had never started. I was expecting a much better experience for the price I'm being charged. I really hopes this gets fixed before I run out of patience and ask for my refund.

I have been really enjoying using the app so far! Though one thing would really push it over the top for me. I think it would be really nice to have the ability to let yourself know that next time you do an exercise, next week let's say, that you should increase the weight. Either with a little green up arrow that you could check that would show up next week, or being able to add notes to your exercise routine. I find I forget sokmetimes and there's no note function for each exercise.

Great app. Whether you're starting out in a gym (like me) or an experienced athlete, this app caters for all. You can follow the set programmes for all kinds of requirements or create your own from scratch with just a few clicks. It has almost all gym exercises and their variations to draw from - meaning you can create almost any regime. The handy gifs and videos are really helpful if you're new to this as they show you correct form and technique. Would highly recommend, thanks.

Been using this exercise for a few months now and compared to other apps this one does what it says it does. Great for tracking progress with weights and reps. Proposed schedules are too long though and exercise choose seems long, but does miss some of basic exercises on alcertain machines, but you can replace with a similar one and do the one you want.

Poor UI for the core functionality makes this app very frustrating and unusable for me. The rest countdown timer and progress bar is tiny and hard to read. The "Did it" button  is very slim and hard to hit during intensive workout. Just make those 3 vital areas 2x higher and this would be a 5* app. I would probably become a paying customer because exercise selection is broad and the plans look interesting.

Almost the perfect app. Easy to use, simple and lots of features. Would like to be able to edit a workout once complete. Would like to be able to add more than 5 sets to a workout. Would really like to see analytics of which muscle groups are getting worked (eg shoulder 20% arms 10%) so you can monitor lacking areas. I've seen pie charts on other apps that are visually easy to understand. Lastly a cool feature would be a PB marker. Otherwise it's a great app and I've recommended it.

Love this app. I have tried so many fitness apps and this one is the best for me so far. I wish there were more machine workouts available for custom workout routines along with more floor routines but its still good. I also wish that I could save my custom workouts through my account. I have to get my phone fixed and got a temp phone. Temp phone had to be sent back and get another one before getting my original phone back. I had to input my custom workout way too many times. Despite all of this, this app is awesome and keeps me on track and takes away so much confusion on what to do. It also helps keep me motivated.

more and more, I realize this app is not flexible at all and is missing a lot of features that the iPhone version has. Exercises are missing from the list, like the rowing machine. I had to reset my whole exercise plan because I tried to check something in the exercise setting, meaning that I was back to my program from 6 months ago, exactly the same. iPhone version also has a feature where it chooses the weights for you in order to have a load/deload cycle. here you have to figure it out.

Recently been using this app, seems great so far very user friendly but please create an option to delete an unfinished workout! You get 16 workouts before they refresh the exercises for the next 16 but I've found sometimes due to bad Internet connection or if you want to save what you've done so far in a workout and exit the app it will complete it and you can't go back into that same session to finish it. I've done this by mistake a few times which is going to mess up my routine.

Do NOT sign up! I signed up to this app on a 7 day free trial as you're unable to view any of its features without doing so. I cancelled within 10 minutes as it is not what I am looking for and have already been charged, not even 10 minutes into the alleged free trial! Have contacted customer services for a refund but have not received a response.
Great variety of workouts, bubble timer disappears and difficult to adjust for my rest time preferred. some glitch, lost my workouts and progress.
Very easy to use app,excellent workout.

Potentially a very good app but none of workouts are available unless you upgrade to premium. It would be great to have a few of the beginner workout available for free without having to subscribe. It would give newbies an idea of what it can do before unlocking the premium version. I like how you can customize your own work out plan and has videos to show u as well as be able to add your own excercises if it's not in the app.
horrible, lack of support, not user friendly. Bought it by mistake and now even harder to get customer support
It needs to be able to substitute workouts like it has on the iPhone app
I LOVE, I DO LOVE THIS APP ON IPHONE. i switched to android and its a piece of scrap here unfortunately

Can't lift with out it! Keeps me in the zone with the customisable in between set timers, has a huge range of features and keeps track of my progress every single set! Only request is in the progress tab it would be amazing to see a %gain stat for gains on completed work outs, even more motivating would be a %increased from your first exercises. ie, your bench has increased 12% this week and 80% overall

This app needs work. It recommends single limb exercises but doesn't allow you to record for each limb individually. Also once you start a workout you can't swap out an exercise when the one in the plan isn't available. Not very useful.
Awesome app, very useful for those who go to the gym, much cheaper than a regular gym instructor and the explanations are clear

As an Android user, the workout report dashboard feels very poor compared to iOS. There’s no proper calendar view, limited workout summary, and no detailed body part tracking. It makes progress tracking difficult and less motivating. Please bring feature parity with iOS.

the only reason I use to use this app was the ability to freely design my own workouts with a nice UI. All my friends had versions of the old app, now they have gotten rid of that feature so its time for me to go else where.
Just great, everything you need. Been using this app on and off for 6 years and I'm still super happy with everything this app has to offer.
Great app! I just wish it had integration with Health Connect or Google Fit to better track and manage my training sessions.
easy to use beginner and more freidndly

Offers basic tracking, and an exercise list which at least has pictures and sometimes video, but it says nothing about what makes each variation of an exercise different from the others, making it almost useless except for extreme beginners. The app tries very hard to sell their subscription plans, with full page ads pooping up everytime. I don't know about their paid coaching, but in the sea of exercise tracking apps out there, this is one of the worst ones.

Garbage. It deleted by it self all my planned workouts. Edited to two stars. This rest alarm bug is getting more an more annoying. I set the rest alarm for two minutes, I mark a set as complete, the counter clock starts and the alarm will only sound when I return to the app. So what's the alarm for? Sometimes the alarm sounds after 5 ou 6 minutes in rest time, even if I set it two 1 or 2 minutes.
