The voice would say "workout paused" then when I started again it would say "workout resumed", now there's nothing the time just keeps running the option for auto pause is not in the choices under workout setup as it shows on the help screen.

Amazing app. Turn on your location while you run and it will keep track of your total distance ran, average pace per mile, and how long you've been running. It even runs in the background while your music, or other apps are playing WITH YOUR SCREEN OFF. On top of that, you can save every workout, so you can keep track of them and try to beat your PR every run. 10/10

Incredibly accurate GPS tracking. I use it for my late night dog walking to ensure she gets at least 2 miles daily. The option to leave notes on weather, her mood, who we met on our walk, etc. is priceless. Well designed, very thoughtful app. And I don't even use half of the available features. As an aside: this is the first time I've used an app that has been worth leaving a review. I usually hit "remind me later" or "skip". Yet another well done Under Armour undertaking.

Good app that I very much enjoy, but one problem. If burning over 1,000 calories it says 1k in my workout summary. It rounds the number. You could burn 1,022 cal and it would still say 1k or if you burn, say, 1,332 it will only say 1.3k. I saw no option to change this annoying feature. These numbers are important, it would be nice to just have the exact calories burned displayed so I didn't have to do workarounds to figure the number. Very annoying, so -1 star. Otherwise, it's an amazing app!

This app is superb! I've been using it for less than a week & it has already met & surpassed my expectations! I am quite impressed at how accurate the tracking is, even for trails that are not registered on Google Maps! It tracks distance, speed, time, elevation, pace, heart rate (if you have a connected device), calories burned, steps, total distance/steps traveled & total calories burned. It also tracks indoor walking/cardio. You can also connect to other apps such as MyFitness Pal.

This app is great for beginners, like me, that are either in the beginning stages, or those already in an exercise routine. I haven't been actively exercising due to the issues of Covid 19 this past summer, but in doing so, I became lax and complacent. I recently bought a beach cruiser three speed bike to get me going again. I set the app to my specific bike, and the app not only gave me the distance, speed, caloric burn, and route, weather, it also allows you to pause & save the tracking ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I've used this app for several years for free and it was wonderful. Due to an injury, I had not walked in 3 months. When I logged in the other day, I could not get off the MVP screen and was forced into the trial offer for a monthly/yearly subscription. Is this app no longer having a free version????? I don't need bells and whistles, just distance:/ I know...submit a support ticket, blah, blah, blah....

Amazing. Pretty cool. Ez peasy. Good for the lazy (yet still helpful) I downloaded and started app (easy and fastl). I walked an hour. I stopped to rest a few minutes -- and without any prompting on my part --the app suddenly (and surprisingly) vocalized to me, "30 minutes, 2 miles, 15 minute mile." Oh . . . not to have to dig out my phone and hunt for the on button, the app, the statistics! I liked not doing "that hunt." It automatically told me the low down of my workout.

"I'm continually impressed by the accuracy of this app. It's incredibly convenient - I can simply open it and go. Whether I'm listening to music, taking a walk, or jogging, it's the voice alerts at every mile that really motivate me to push my limits. It also offers a detailed visual map of my route. And the best part, It's all for free. I can't imagine my workout routine without it!" 🏃🎶🗺️

Typically, I have no issues with the app. Recently, had logged a 2-mile walk, (wanting to include it for one of the challenges), and after having paused the workout for a few minutes, I was unable to resume. Was stuck in the workout page and had to uninstall and reinstall. Working better now, will look into the "pause" option to avoid workout losses like that in the future. Other than that, I've enjoyed the app and the many walk-type options and settings it offers. Still learning!

Have been using it for several months, and find it accurate and easy-to-use. By far the best of the apps out there to record lunch, daily walk and activity. Several months later my opinion is only become stronger This is by far the easiest to use and also by far the most accurate of all of the apps that successfully record walking using GPS and time. Highly recommended. Now in July, by far the best of a crowded field. Now it is October and I continue to love it! Now it's December! Same story!

This is the only walking app I have used, so l have nothing to compare it to. I love it as it offers all the possible stats for a phone app, as distance, total duration, current pace, avg pace, number of steps, and terrain elevation, which uses to calculate calories used. It allows you to pause the walk, which is great for me, since I walk in my neighborhood and run into people I know from time to time. At the end of the walk it provides a map that shows the path, along with all the stats above.

Pretty good walking app. I appreciate how easy it is to turn on and just walk. Listening to the ladies' voice telling me how slow I am makes me push for better times. My fellow walkers and I listen for her to report our mile times. They seem accurate to me. I run it with my fitness watch and thiscapp is way more accurate. It feels like we're being scolded for not walking faster. I've been using this app for at least 2 years before Covid-19. For a free app, it's been excellent.

I've only used a couple of other exercise tracking programs, so don't have a significant basis for comparison. But I can say that I'm thoroughly enjoying learning about and using this one (free version). As a fitness walker, it is a pleasure to be able to track my course in real time and to use the real time stats to determine any course changes I might want to make mid-session. I like that flexibility.

I use it for walking. Tracks distance, speed, and calories burned. It also shows your route and it is saved in the app to be used again if you want to use the route again. I love it for this. HOWEVER I can't use it for strength training. It's difficult trying to enter each exercise. You have to make some guesses to get it entered. On days I do strength training I don't enter anything.

This app works great for me. I use it to log my twice daily walks and it automatically imports it to MyFitnessPal. I find the GPS tracking to be very accurate and I also really like that I can go back and choose an old workout (sometimes I forget to end my workout then go for a drive or something and it messes up the distance/time etc.). That makes for a quick fix.

I have only used it twice in 2 days but it seems to be very accurate as far as distance and path walked. I believe the calculated calories burned is accurate as well. It's a great app so far and I'll continue to use and update as necessary. Update: I have added bike riding to my regime and I am really liking this app. It does a great job of keeping track of distance, time as well as average speed and calories burned.

It doesn't track distance properly, at all. Sometimes it won't let you end a workout, no matter how much you try to hold the end workout button it just doesn't register. We're forced to use this app for my fitness class, she told us how many miles an amount of laps around the track is, ann even if you do that amount of laps for like 3 miles, it only counts .7 miles to maybe 1 mile.

This is an awesome app. I love how easy it is to set up and use it. It tells me my mile marker and the rate that I'm walking. For some reason, the app is now hard to close n out. When I try to pause and save, it keeps popping up the save workout, but it doesn't save it. I have to force close to go back in to save my workout. Please fix this glitch in the app.. It's a great motivator to pick up your pace when you want to slow down.

I have been using Map my walk since July of 2013, and I am very pleased. I am only interested in tracking distance and time, I don't use any of the other features, but I do note that the calories burned seem to calculate consistently based on time, distance, and elevation. I find it is very dependable, and accurate. Update 5-17-2025. Still using the app. Still find it accurate. I do mainly use it for distance, but find the calorie and time feature useful for comparison to previous workouts

So Encouraging! Knowing how much you're doing in real time - and with the app providing feedback at milestones - is so encouraging that I just don't want to stop! Update - 1 month later: I wish that I had the option of requesting when to be notified - I would prefer to be notified at the half mile instead of just at the mile completed. 🤷🏽‍♀️

I'm almost 70 and i need to get back to standing and walking rather than sitting and reading. This app and it's incredible monitoring of time and distance... and GPS keeps me engaged. This free app is the best I've used and it keeps me engaged by actually letting me see the effort i put in. My next walk, I'll want to do at least that again, if not beat it. i highly recommend this for anyone who needs incentive to engage in walking as exercise and stress reduction. 👍

This app has always worked perfectly for me, using at least seven different phones. It works great! It gives a map of your location and shows a live track of where you are hiking, handy in an unfamiliar location so you can retrace your steps. And because all the businesses are labeled, you can find a bathroom or drinks, etc. You can save and share the route or keep it for yourself for later use.

Won't save work outs (hikes) if not connected to a network. It appeared to have saved, but not viewable later. Otherwise, a decent app to track workouts. It's really designed for someone wishing to "physical activity," ie calories burned, speed, etc. i use it strictly to let me know how far I've gone & route. There's probably a better app for that but this allows just the basics, and thats what I want. don't need or want all the other stuff.

This app is a godsend when it comes to tracking how much, how fast and how long you've walked, it even counts calories lost. That is not all, it also shows you your pace. Oh, and this app does not use a lot of battery and it is a lot more accurate than my GPS watch. It even tracks inside the stores. One of the coolest things about it is that it also maps your workout on a map. There's two flaws though: Walks, Runs, Cycling etc... are all counted together and it's buggy on older devices.

Overall, it's a great app, track's my steps, distance time, and calories burned. However, I feel if it's able to track speed walking and distance, it should be able to know when I've come to a stop and automatically pause and restart when I start moving until I've hit done and save. When I'm in a fast pace walk and I stop for just a minute I don't think to pull my phone out hit pause and restart. But besides that it's a great app. 👍

It does a great job doing everything I want it to! Update: The app gets stuck. The "save my workout" button doesn't register when I click it. After many save attempts, I (very frustratedly) try to just delete the workout. Then, the delete button causes another delete button, and when clicked, it quickly brings me back to the save page to start all over again! I can't save any workout! 😭

I can only speak for my first day using this but so far so great. It uses the GPS to track where you are walking which can be good for exploring the world around you as your working out. It tracks your distance, steps, duration of your walks, your average pace, calories, and even elevation and then when your done you can save your progress including the route you took to look at for another time.

I gave it another chance on my android phone. It's still full of ads and froo-fraw you have to opt out of, and after my walk it won't say how far I have walked - just the time and number of calories. No miles. When I try to move it to a previous page to see the miles, it asks if I "really want to delete this walk" (?!) So for me it's worthless.

Very easy to use. Simple. Download and go. Shows me what I want: distance, speed, exact map, how many steps and calories burned. That's all I need. I'm sure there's more complex features in the app but I'm very happy with this. Update: I've been using the app for 6 weeks now and still love it. Very easy, very reliable. I like that it tells me when I've walked a mile, then when I hit two miles, etc and the speed (in miles per hour) that I'm doing. I'm so glad I found it!

This app gives you all the information data you need about your walk. Location, distance, calories, elevation changes is all recorded. Over all a great app to keep you motivated on your workouts. I have used the app for about a year. I have found I was able to set up fitness goals and reach them. I'm thankful to the developers of this app . Thanks to it I was able to surpass my personal goal of walking one thousand miles. Thanks guys, much appreciated.

I have used the free version for several years for my goal of walking 10 miles per week. The app does an excellent job of tracking my walks. Unfortunately after using it for several months it slows saving my walks eventually locks up. I then have to delete and reload the app. Fortunately after logging in all my previous walks and data is still there. I wonder if I paid for the MPV version this problem wouldn't occur. Overall I'm satisfied and have recommended the app to many fellow walkers.

Used to work great, but now it constantly crashes during my walk. It will either just close completely or freeze until I reopen it. Problem is when I open the app again, it will still have recorded the time I've been walking, but not the distance. For example, it crashed during this walk at 9 minutes and 0.6 miles recorded. I opened the app a while later and it has me at 16 minutes, but still at 0.6 miles

Today, this app worked great, on my phone!! I have been trying to use it off and on for a year or more, and with mixed results!! Seems it is just too easy to stop itself, reset itself, not save walks, etc. I am guessing this is due to operator error! But, no matter, it worked today. And, it worked in the background while I was watching YouTube and making phone calls, etc. I walk on a trail, for my health, not adventure, so I generally pass the time playing on my phone! I am happy to report t

I really enjoy this app. I use it to track all of my walking workouts. it tracks the distance and gives me my average per mile. I like that I can look back week to week and see how many miles I've walked each week and compare. I've been using this app for probably almost 2 years now come on I would recommend it. You can also add friends who also have the app and cheer on and support one another in workouts. You can make comments and like other's workouts.

I have been using this for a couple of weeks now to hold me more accountable on my weight loss journey and I love it. It always gives me accurate miles and duration. It is super easy to use. And the fact that I can save my data just means it holds me more accountable since I can physically go into the app to see how many miles I did, how many days I walked, when I biked verses walked.

I really liked this app. Did what it said it was going to do. All without bombarding me with ads. I liked the verbal cues of telling me of Every mile I walked and how fast I was walking/running. It was a fun little challenge to beat my logged walk of the previous day. Lost 30 lbs. It was really nice to enjoy the free features the app provided. All without a constant trying to sell me something. Letting me get into the groove of my workout. I plan on buy a full version if there is one.

The app works great. However, it now prompts me to turn off power save mode, if it's on. I don't always see the prompt, as I click Start and go, so it doesn't start tracking my activity until I stop and deal with it. It would be nice if the app would still count the activity in the background. It seems to work just fine when I leave power saving mode on for my phone.

Works fairly well for the most part. Doesn't follow the streets as closely as I thought it should. Seems to cut right through peoples' yards when you go around the corners. At least that's what the graph shows. 2023, Things seemed to be working much better these last couple of years. I really enjoy using this App. Now, it being 2024, the App seems to be working much better. We really enjoy using this walking App !!

I am physically,progressively, disabled with peripheral neuropathy from agent orange exposure. I use it to try to maintain endurance walking with a rolator walker. It gives distance to 0.01 miles, time to the second, and an estimated number of steps. Walks can be saved for comparison and to analyze progress or lack thereof. It has other features that I don't use. A great app for my purposes.

Am I to understand that I am being forced to upgrade this app to a paid version even though I've been using the free version for years simply because the app was recently purchased by the Outside company? Upon further review of this issue when it appears that I am stuck on the try upgrade for 14 day screen, simply heading the back button will not get you out of that screen. However, those icons on the bottom do get you out of it. Issued resolved. Thanks for letting us keep free version!

Long time user and was great but something changed recently. The integration with GPS isn't working like it did causing parts of my walks getting lost, also my pace being recorded as both inaccurate and widely ranging a great deal even though I was steady throughout. I sent a message to support and I see multiple other users with new reviews showing this problem. maybe they'll correct things. Working great now. Thanks

the actual routes I walk isnt being counted. The map only shows the furthest points one time and if rewalk the exact area it chops the path off like I didnt walk the area. The furthest points are connected to give a generalization of the area but does not really show path walked. False lower milage is obtained which then shows took longer to do fewer miles.....ridiculous. Complete waste. When had app year ago, it showed every place walked on a path, no matter how many times walked it.

Great app to keep track of the miles you run/walk. Plantform is great and easy to use. Bonuses are challenges you can enter into and win stuff so it says but they are great motivation. The only thing I don't like about this app is you can't use other apps while this app is going otherwise it doesn't count the miles you did while running other apps, that's why I give it a 4* instead of 5*, would be great to use Pandora for music while I count my miles.

Was a great app until last update. Went from accurate tracking to worthless tracking. I walk the same paths daily and am now getting under reported results. The mapping doesn't even display the rout accurately. Voice prompts are no longer on the mile intervals and the split times are really goofy. Calories burned are no longer accurate. About all it's good for now is tracking overall time and a date. Very dissapointing. Update: Switched to backup phone and unreliable results about the same.

I've tried almost every walking app out there. This is my favorite by a large margin, and the one I actually use daily. It's accurate, the UI is squeaky clean and simple, and it does exactly what I want and no more. The free version might not be enough for a serious athlete, but for a fitness walker/runner it's perfect. 5/5 Enthusiastically recommended! (ps, no, I'm not a bot. I'm a cranky old man who rarely finds anything to say "yes" to. Map My Walk is a full-throated YASS!) Best in category!

Done one walk with it so far, so can't speak to its tracking over time yet. Easy to set up and use. The one removed star is for the pace and elevation graphs, which have static y axes that don't adjust to your actual trip. I was curious to see what my 36-foot elevation change around my neighborhood looked like, but because the app expects you to be climbing mountains, it's basically a flat line.

When I start the app it tries imediately to save the workout, even before I can start recording my walk. And, no matter how many times I save or cancel the save, I never get to the screen to start recording my workout. It previously worked correctly. I must have had a stealth update installed without my consent. So disappointing. NOT RECOMMENDED!!!

While I find this app to be a ballpark estimate of what I have accomplished, it often seems WAY off. For example, when doing my uphill hikes to prepare for backpacking excursions it will tell me that I have covered a mile in 15 minutes. What?! I'm a 65 year old man carrying 35 lbs on my back while gaining 300' in altitude per mile! I'd be very happy if this aspect of the app could be improved.

I appreciate that this app respects my privacy, having moved here from one that didn't. The features work well for me and the app is easy to use. I especially like being able to record which type of workout I did, and add photos and notes! I wish I could track all my steps each day instead of just workouts, though -- and could track steps as well as miles ...Then it would have all the features I need.

Easy to use and accurate! I am using the free version and it has been awesome! I walk dogs and walk for exercise, so I love being able to label the workouts. I love seeing how many miles I walked over week and I especially love seeing elevation gains. I have not had any tech issues with this app at all! I am using it on an Android device. It has a great pause and resume feature, which is great on the dog walks. Great app! I recommend giving it a try!

Easy to use, and I like how it automatically adds the walk to My Fitness Pal diary. I use another that I prefer because it has years of my walking history that syncs and can be viewed online, and gives verbal updates more frequently. (I needed to check settings - Looks like this can too so I made some setting changes. I will test next time then update this review.) However, the last 2 walks it couldn't find GPS though it was on and this app found it without delay. So I'll stick with it.

I used to use Map My Run a while ago and decided to download this (it's what came up when I searched Map My Run, though I now see there are a few similar apps for the different activities). I mainly wanted to get an idea of changes in elevation on some of my routes, but it did not track elevation. There is a place for that stat, but it doesn't seem to function. I probably won't use it if I can't get elevation since most of the other stats are tracked by my Fitbit.

I was disappointed that the app does not recognize my LETSCOM ID115 Plus HR tracker's HR monitor, and that this app is not available as a third-party app in my tracker's native app (for sharing data). Otherwise, it was nice using the app again for the first time after many years! The route mapping seems more reliable than in my tracker's native app, too. I was also very pleased to see that my old workouts from 2012 and prior were still saved, and available to look at.

I enjoy using this app to keep my pace up and measure my distance when walking. I find it accurate and helpful. My issue with the app is being asked to rate the app at the end of a workout before it is saved. I lost great workout recording just now. once when I refused the request to rate the app I lost another of my best workouts. I'm in an outside the app challenge this month and wanted to share. ASK AFTER WORKOUT IS SAVED!

I am a low income senior, so appreciate this free App. I walked 985 miles last year (2018...epic for me), & Good thing I keep track as well, because Map my walk malfunctioned & stopped syncing. Very disappointing! Surprisingly, the App started back up, as normal, Jan. 2019 & so did the pressure to purchase. So I wonder? Technical syncing issue or, because I didn't (couldn't afford) upgrade? I like the app, it's free, simple & is good for my tracking activity.

I use the"free" version. In app ads a bit annoying. I have found that using the dog walk doesn't give as accurate results as just using"walk"setting. Dog walk has tendency to automatically pause but has to be manually restarted. Wish there were more choices for logging a gym equipment workout. Overall very happy with free version. Update : step cadence apparently only works when running. They used to send a monthly report of how many miles you've walked. That feature has disappeared which is sad

This is a really cool app. Does alot more than I expected, very pleased! Charted everything from average pace walked to the elevation (higher and lower) as I walked. Very accurate and simple to use with easy to read charts, graphs, and even keeps track of calories burned. Very satisfied with my choice of application will be recommending to others.

I turned the app on when you started our walk and put my phone in my pocket. I took it out once to check and make sure it was still running. It calculated our distance through a neighborhood and our speed. I love the log feature. I was able to say how I felt anecdotally about the walk. I want to see an improvement in not only my distance and speed, but how I feel during each one. It's perfect for that

What has happen to the app? The last 3 runs I've done have not recorded correctly. I always do 5+miles and I'm showing between 1-2 miles each time. I have to manually go in and adjust the miles. Disappointing, as this had always been a great app until now. I don't want to give low stars as it's always been great but as it now it's useless. If the problem gets fixed I'll come back and adjust.

This has been a great "free" app with an option to upgrade to a Premium version. When I went to use it today, it is forcing me to sign up for a free trial of the Premium version and doesn't even give me the option of staying with the free version. As much as I like and use this app, I do not need or want more than the offered on the freebie. I'm really bummed and will uninstall Map My Walk and look for something else to help me with my fitness tracking.

I love that it tracks not only my distance down to the hundredth of a mile, but my current speed and my average speed and the elevation changes. It does appear that sometimes it takes a moment for it to register a change in my speed, from walking to running back to walking, for example. But overall quite accurate and super easy to use.

This app is great! The free version does have lots of advertisements, but I can live with that. I like that it shows my path; counts my steps, calories, pacing; and also measures the altitudes of where I walk. ❤ 🤹‍♀️ Since the last update even more 🤔 ads, but it still provides the info I want.🤹‍♀️ Update: Still love the app. It's changed a bit, but still does what I need it to do. I use it multiple times each day! ❤

This would be 5 stars if it was dependable. Every other walk, it stops logging distance at .04 miles. I like that it syncs with my Fitbit. I like the social aspect so I can have healthy competition with friends. I like the live updates along my walk for distance and pace. I detest that it fails to log my walk 50% of the time. I've uninstalled it, but the only thing that helps is clearing cache and data for the app. What a pain.

I love it. It's very easy to use and it has different selections for the type of workout you're doing (walk, run, dog walk, treadmill, bike, etc). I also love the fact that it has a detailed map of where I am and it also saves a copy of the map and allows you to write details about your workout experience along with providing you your distance, time and calories burned. It also announces when you hit mile markers. It's not overcomplicated and it's easy to use. I definitely recommend it.

Overall it's been a wonderful app. Not thrilled with a subscription just because most people can only afford so many subscriptions a month and everything's a subscription now. But I use this one enough that I pay for it. I love the ability to add photos. All I use is the walking timer and the photo app and I keep everything private. I know it has a lot of other features but that's all I need and it does it great. :-)

The distances and actual tracking of where you have been is very accurate. The audio voice reminder of each mile is helpful. To see the route taken and the time it took to travel is great information. Having it sync up with Fitbit app on the phone is very useful. Enjoy using this app. Recommend this app to use even if not using Fitbit. The Road Cycling setting includes the speed in the data. IF used to track traveling in a car with this setting, disregard the calories.

This app is a fantastic way to track my walks. It allows me to load maps I created from their website, or those shared by others. I love the mix of stats and the flexibility of when I hear updates on distance, total times, and split pace stats (or even turning it off). Being able to add photos, and share the workout easily is a big bonus.

When the app works, it's great. However, a few time each day it wants me to turn off my power saving option. If I don't, it doesn't save all information from my walk. If it doesn't ask me to turn it off, everything works fine. It's irritating because I walk the dog several times a day and it makes me stop to turn off the battery saving. Deleting!

It's great for tracking your walks, just don't forget to end your workout afterwards - otherwise your drive home will mess up all of your stats.. plus there is no way to edit the end time from the app itself. EDIT: The editing option mentioned in their reply seems to allow manually changing the stats, as in typing in new numbers - but how do I know what to change it to? I don't know how long it took to drive home or the exact distance it is.

Works good. I have no other similar app to compare this with because it has done what I want from the start. I expect to stick with this one. UPDATE: Have used this for over five years now and still like it. Use it walking, hiking, biking, and kayaking too (but it doesn't have a selection for kayaking, so I usually choose hiking). I'm a basic user, but since it has a lot more features than I use, it's probably good for more advanced users too.

I really want to like and use this app. I have a couple issues preventing this. first, sometimes it does not count steps. Two walking workouts in a day and steps for both were ignored. but a walk in the store s couple hours later was. This has happened on more than one occasion. Second, you're cannot edit duration of a workout. Sometimes I forget to end a workout and it totally messes up my tracking. I will be sticking with samsung health for now.

Wish there was a way for the step counter widget to update in real time. Especially at the end of the day when I'm trying to reach my target goal. And most of the time when I open the app it still takes 30 seconds or more for it to update the count. And sometime it won't update at all. Frustratingly, sometimes the app will decrease the amount of steps or heart points that you have earned. If you are trying to maintain a streak of days where you've made your target this can be very demotivating.

It doesn't sync to anything anymore. Google Fit is now blocking apps I want to connect to and have previously connected to. Data is inaccurate because of the lack of syncing, even when shown as connectedness. If you try disconnecting and reconnecting the applications, Google Fit will deny the connection, and there is no way to override. Google Fit is Junk!

I seriously loved this app. It was a great catch-all for me because I hike, cycle, and horseback ride. But now that they're discontinuing it, the whole thing is going downhill. I have no idea why they decided to kill this thing; it was reliable, easy to use, and would sync nicely will other apps. Super disappointed because all of the other fitness apps look like buggy, expensive messes. PLEASE Google I BEG YOU to keep this app alive.

It's a good app. I use a Fossil Gen 6 and while I use both apps Fit seems to work better. I like tracking my vitals along with my activity for the day. THAT SAID... I have a backlog of data and don't like weight entry set up like an "odometer" and the calendar is also tedious. Just let me type in the data please. Even better, you folks seem hard core on us talking to our phones, why not just me tell Fit, without typing/scrolling at all? And bring back desktop support like all your other apps!

I just want to say, so far, outstanding. I have seen Fitbit charge a crazy price for their "premium" services when it's mostly the same as this app! I can manage my Fitbit data through here with the fit to fit app, though that app still needs help. This app only needs the ability to sync data between third party apps possible! Add that, and possibly a mental health section as well, since you have meditation and whatnot, why don't we add an actual journal? Or a health journal? Just an idea.

I specifically bought my Fitbit for sleep tracking purposes, as I use a CPAP and I'm also a light sleeper and have dogs that tend to wake me up various times during the night. The big update seems to have completely neutered its effectiveness for that purpose, as it no longer reports hourly oxygen levels and doesn't seem to see short wake up periods anymore. It was never perfect, but now I'm probably just going to get rid of it because it's unable to help me ID when I need to make adjustments.

DOES NOT ACCURATELYTRACKSTEPS, NOCUSTOMERSERVICE. In a team challenge at work for 1 month, have let them down! At the end of the day my galaxy fit 3 says over 10,000 steps but the app states 2,000. I have updated, reinstalled, reached out to the community twice, the AI assit couldn't help, found an email address that was no longer active. i only bought the watch fir this challenge, wish I had enough money to buy a fitbit instead.

Unable to track lap swimming. The old Fitbit app was accurate and helpful. Google's "updated" app is useless. Tracks less than half of laps I swim. Wish I could get rid of this and get the old app back. Sleep tracking is also off. Doesn't recognize times awake during the night. Google broke what was working fine. Will be looking for a better tracker from another company next time. Very unhappy. Thought Google was better; if this is an example of their work, I won't be using their products.

This app used to work well, but over the last month seem to not want to quit tracking my walks even after I stop the tracking, close the app, close the app, by swiping up on the home screen to make it disappear from the list of running apps, and sometimes even after doing that and restarting the phone. it's very frustrating to have my normal walking added into my fitness walking and not being able to track my actual exercise.

I miss the user friendliness of the Fitbit app. My biggest disappointment is that in the Health app is that once you find where you can find the place to change the hours for your 250 steps per hour when you set it it doesn't save it. The day Google forced me to switch I had it set for an unusual day and now I can't switch it back. Grrrr!

Once again, Google buys a company, changes the app, and provides less functionality. I use the FITBIT APP to log my calories as part weight loss strategy. Not everything is in the database, so sometimes I just have to manually enter the calories. But that is no longer an option. I can't be the only person who uses this and it couldn't be that hard to code in, so the only reason I can think of that it was not included was the people working on the app don't use the product.

This is the very worst version of anything Google have done since buying Fitbit. I have gone through the trouble shooting process multiple times, uninstalled, reinstalled, checked permissions, etc. Consistently received "something went wrong" messages. You have now made my Fitbit fitness tracker into an expensive paperweight. Your responses to the issues raised by customers are inadequate. I am convinced that I will need to leave it behind and completely switch to another company.

Useful for me, but... Fit does a great job of tracking my physical activity, but its caclulations of calories expended are so far beyond realistic. I could never burn the calories it claims I burned because it is physically impossible. The calorie counting part of this app needs a lot more work to make it realistic. I would also like to option to clear out the accumulated daily data when I go hiking and climbing on rocks.

Thanks for continuing to offer this app. I track my menstrual cycle with it and thought I lost my data after my last phone crashed. This data is really important now, since I'm nearing menopause, last cycle being months ago. Fit did not come pre-installed on my new phone so I was relieved to see it still available in the Play Store. Thanks.

Double counting steps because I can't select what data sources to include. There's two google apps that you need. Google Fit and Google Health Connect. They don't really explain any of this and its a hassle every time I upgrade phones because the read/write permissions reset. I only use this app to sync my garmin steps to other apps unsupported by garmin.

Negatives: 1 - as many people have pointed out, the app is kind of hit-and-miss with regards to accurately counting steps, and allocating heart points appropriately. 2 - There really should be a cumulative total distance walked available for each day, rather than having to add up each "walk" for a total. 3 - On MANY occasions, the cumulative number of miles DOES NOT ADD UP TO what the app told my on the particular day AS THE DAY WAS PROGRESSING! Positives: easy to read/navigate.

Almost every time I track a workout and try to stop it, it shows it as already completed, but when I open it again the next day it shows that it's still tracking yesterdays workout, and even drained my battery doing it. It can't properly track workouts, so I can't justify giving it any higher than a 1.

I would give this zero stars if I could. The new Google Fit/Fitbit app update removed features I actually used every day. The interface I relied on is gone, the layout is worse, and basic food tracking is now much harder. I can no longer add custom foods, which makes the app almost useless for accurate tracking. The food database is incomplete, and I cannot reliably verify my macros. For someone who depends on this app for nutrition and activity tracking.

the forced conversion from the Fitbit app to this reduced functionality app would have been bad enough, but the conversion process also seems to have cost me the history I had in the Fitbit app as well. Zero stars for yet another Google move hostile to the people using the product. I'm sure it was mildly helpful for their bottom line though.

I lost ALL of my data. I am very unhappy with the fitbit app changing into what it is now. after the change I still had all my data but after logging on today to make some updates I have a completely blank profile. I will cancel my premium if Im told the only way to recover it is to call a 1.800 number or send an email to support. this is incredibly frustrating as my doctor's appointment on Tuesday largely depends on everything I have been trying to track

so far I'm enjoying the app, it's easy to use, tracks everything perfectly, and for those curious it works well with fitdoc. However one thing I wish it could do was tell you the amount of calories you're about to consume if you take a photo of the food you're about to eat by using the camera in the phone a nice alternative for those who don't have a smartwatch.

I would give this app a Zero. It doesn't count distance accurately compared to my wife's fitbit. The journal hasn't updated in 5 days. The map of my route looks like an abstract drawing. I checked my steps and distance halfway, when I finished my steps increased but the distance was less. Go figure The measurement of my pace is way off. I wish I could find an app that works without intrusive advertising.

This app used to be really good, but it stopped syncing with other apps & the step counter is no longer accurate. Even when it counts steps, it will not total them correctly for the day. The email address listed under app support, is no longer active, so there is no way to contact anyone about this issue. Google really dropped the ball on this one.

Where are all these extra steps coming from? I have checked Garmin Connect and Health Connect, and they both reflect my steps correctly. Google Fit does not. I have turned off Activity Tracking and more as well, yet somehow, I have over 2000 MORE steps on Google Fit. Fix this once and for all; this app is not reliable at all. Health Points also constantly lower randomly.

Not happy about update. Want my active minutes back! It is not the same as steps. Steps is useful but Not as useful as active minutes! Got worse today, now it won't open. Keeps crashing. Reboot, uninstall/ reinstall, not working at all. Looks like they fixed it, putting back to 3 stars. I was wrong, still broke. Updates from fitbit app are sporadic at best. Have to go to both apps MANY times to finally get data to sync

I'm in a group mission and unfortunately it has a hard time connecting to post a distance or corresponding with the other app to record the activity. The other horrible issue is that it doesn't read your miles and steps accurately. I'm always shorted by a quarter of a mile, sometimes more.

So tell me something.. why can my maps app track every activity.. walking.. kayaking.. etc.. to the minute.. but this useless fitness app can't track automatically when I'm walking or doing any other activity? Baffles me! Before you say anything.. yes, all settings are enabled just fine! Also, please bring back the ability to connect my HRM instead of using my watch!

Terrible I'm switching watches now. If I could give negative stars I would. I've had the fitbit for a long time and its been my favorite watch and tracker. I do everything on it. track my calories, steps, workout, my cycle...everything. this newest update completely changed the interface. I can't even make it similar to what I had before. not only that all of my history is gone....are you kidding me???? also it keeps popping up these tips and suggestions you can't delete. you ruined the app

This app does not let the user include their own recipes nor will it add foods not in the database via scanning like the previous Fitbit app. I am unaware how to request foods to be added. I am unable to track my food intake as easily and I should not have to purchase another app to do so! I already pay for premium and this new version has fewer features than the old one

The forced app "upgrade" from fitbit is awful. it is clunky and cartoonish. I'm an adult that likes to review information presented as if I'm over the age of 10. Other specific complaints - I can't scroll backward by days. Plus I cannot get fine details by the hour anymore. Is there anything I can do to get the old app format back? This is bad enough to make me abandon Fitbit (or Google fitness tech), even though I've been a loyal user for a decade.

It was just okay for the first several years, but I had to uninstall it recently because it was using so much mobile data for no reason. Around 50gb in 2 weeks! I'm at home most of the time and when I do go out, it's to walk my dog. It made no sense. The step count is terrible, but I used this app and another one to sync with other health apps. Sorry, but I had to let it go.

Yesterday, it corrupted all of my miles walked by reducing the distances by about ten times. I tried everything but can not get my correct stats back. It used to work great, but the last year or so, every update makes it worse. Now, if I walk 1.5 miles it will initially record correctly, but later it will change to something like 0.15 miles since about a week ago. Now, I see why so many people give up on Android and change to iPhone. Google is irresponsible.

This is a downgrade from the Fitbit app and I am now looking for a new tracker. In the previous app I could easily track zone minute calories that were automatically captured outside of officially logged workouts. I have an active job, where I am walking almost all of the time for short bursts, and the previous Fitbit app captured and displayed these calories in Zone Minutes. Now the Zone Minutes only display time moving, not calories. This makes it nearly impossible to accurately track.

There are so many bugs, it's completely unusable. First, I ran around a large block 3 times: Fit tracked it as a half a lap and then me teleporting through a building to get to my final location. I checked my phone just before the final lap and it said 1.25 miles, but when I ended the workout it said 0.8 miles?? Next, the weight tracking uses the absolute worst moving average curve I've ever seen. If you enter your weight 8 times in a day, the curve makes a sharp turn. That's just terrible math.

It used to track weekly miles walked. That panel has now disappeared and replaced with "Energy Expended" (calories I guess) which I don't care about. In fact I don't see any way of editing my goals anymore. There used to be a way of doing this. Maybe it's right in front of my face and I'm missing it. If I figure it out I'll edit this review.

It's a pretty good app, I like tracking my steps and my workouts and weight all in one place and I like that it connects to other apps. However, two issues: when tracking a workout, I wish it were easier to log what workout you're doing. It gives you a drop-down menu and you have to scroll through the whhhoooolllleee thing every single time. I wish you could search your workout and save a minute between sets. Also, I wish it were easier/faster/more efficient to track water directly on the app.

I've been using this app for about a week now, maybe 2, for tracking walking. When I stop the tracker, it tells me the distance, time, and pacing/mile is one thing. However, when I go to check the journal, all the numbers have changed and cut about a third of a mile off, resulting in time changes as well. Why is the summary one thing, but the journal entry is completely different?!

No longer pulls in data from other apps. This used to be my go-to for having all of my separate fitness data synced into one place. Over the past few months it has been unable to actually pull in any data from any fitness app. Which is really unfortunate because it was great at that. I get that the focus is on Fitbit now, but it'd be nice if this app could at least work. I have actually used Fitbit to see if it could receive data from other apps, and it does. So it's definitely an issue with Fit

App used to work great but just like everyone else it stopped working recently. The app will not count any of my steps or activities. It also no longer will allow me to sign in on my regular account and I had to log in with a different account. I've uninstalled and reinstalled and that did not work, switching accounts didn't either. I'm just trying to get an accurate pokemon go step count and without adventure sync it doesn't work, so I beg of you, please resolve this issue.

When I use this app a few years ago it worked fairly well. I've stayed on top of the updates and it just doesn't seem to track my location properly at all. I'll walk four or five miles in this app will only track a third of a mile or so. It's really frustrating, my polar beat has no issues with tracking mileage & distance, but this app seems to no longer cut it at all. Location and physical activity have been allowed for all of time, so there shouldn't be any issues there. Not pinging correctly.

I've sent feedback at least 2 times in my app, under profile as you've suggested to the numerous people who have the same problem I have. I'd love some type of response. Since I have had no acknowledgment there, I'm writing my issues here. Fit is not tracking, unless I manually turn on/off tracking. This used to work automatically until December. Please address.

Overall, it does what it's supposed to. I debate the accuracy, but my one real complaint at this point is that if I am looking through the data for previous days, there is no way to see the total distance traveled that day. You can add the distance for each recorded activity set, but that's an irritating waste of my time. I would like to be able to calculate average distance per day more easily.

This app used to be better but lately it seems to be getting worse. It no longer wants to track the minutes I spend exercising. I just went on at least a 20 minute walk which even my old outdated pedometer was able to pick up . Yet Google Fit failed. Update July 8 2023 It has gotten even worse since last year. As others have said it no longer tracks activity. I walked back and forth in the house for at least 10 mins and it picked up nothing. Also as someone else said Fit is very buggy.

I admit that I liked it's tracking, but it's such a battery killer that it's unkeepable. I went from my battery never dropping below 60% in a typical day (~16 hours) to it never being above 10% when Fit is allowed to run freely in the background like it wants. Turn only that one thing off and battery usage levels return to near-none. However, then it's unfunctionable and can no longer track. There are far too many competing apps that don't have this problem this severely. Must uninstall.

It would help if I could add distance to my workouts. I run on my elliptical for a set distance, not time. I appreciate being able to track my steps and heart rate. I should be allowed to edit the distance achieved. The same for stationary bike or treadmill. Allow us to edit the stats to add distance. Otherwise, I have to write down my stats, delete the workout, and add the entire thing all over again. And lose heart points in the process.

You have officially lost a user. The last update has made a huge error in keeping track of the distance accurately. It jumps all over the place, too far in distance in a short period of time then jumps back to starting your distance when you've been at it for 20 mins. This is no longer a reliable app. Too bad you don't pay attention to your app. You can see comments explaining this app is having trouble and not running accurately, and you're not doing anything to rectify the problem.

I like that this app is so closely integrated into the Android OS and that it works well with wearables such as Wear OS. It also covers all the basics that you would expect from a fitness app (steps, active minutes, calories, heart rate, etc...) and connects well with 3rd party health apps. What it doesn't have is workout detection and sleep tracking that many of the top fitness ecosystems have.

It's wasn't the most accurate by itself, so it's only real use was to act as a go-between to synchronize data from my fitness watch to my diet tracking app. Well, it didn't do that consistently either, and now that they've done some sort of update or migration with Health Connect, it doesn't even serve that purpose anymore. What a waste of phone space - do not bother with this app, it's completely worthless now, and I have to seek out another app to somehow get my data to my diet tracker.

They dumbed the app down and now it sucks. Now it relies on heart points which are based solely on time. Do if I walk farther but faster I will accumulate fewer heart points than if I walk a shorter distance but walk slower and take a longer time to do so. They've also taken away stats for distance. So if I want to know the total distance I've walked in a day, week, or month it's simply not there. All in all just a terrible app now and I'm looking for another one.

They changed the app from tracking minutes exercised to tracking steps, a change I disagree with. Steps work for tracking some things, like walking and running, but they do not work for other forms of exercise, such as bicycling and things where you shouldn't be carrying a phone or other electronic equipment. Please make it possible to choose whether we want to use steps or minutes.

Could be better... Everything seems a little complicated to me. Some connections don't seem to update. The nutrition tracker should be more easy to use. Like QR codes on food packages or downloading recipes. There's so many easier apps. And yet why use this if others are easier. Exercise tracker included. It's more work than necessary, looking up EVERY activity and the calories it burns. Need to add more activity options.

The GPS and distance tracking is some of the worst I've ever seen. I watched my distance tonight jump from 1.5 miles to 1.2 miles, while the number of steps increased. The same walk earlier this week was tracked as .4 miles total, and 3 miles total. This makes the app virtually useless, which is a shame, because the app has some truly great features.

Really doesn't give much information about anything. I don't really care how many minutes I've moved as much as I want to know intensity, how long, if stairs were involved etc. Paired with the overestimation of distance people have mentioned doesn't help. I use it because it pairs with my meal tracking app, but according to that, Google fit overestimates calories burned as well.

I used to really like this app! Easy to use and clearly tracked data. This update it terrible though. Every time I add something, there is no update to my overall goal for that activity for the week. I don't get it. I hope the next update isn't as terrible, or I'm back to finding something that's actually useful. And, I don't care about "heart points". Just let me track my workouts for the week!

Looks nice but that's about it. It tracks while you drive. Can't enter seconds in manual workouts. Had to go back and make this 1 star. Would make it zero if I could. Can't edit your workouts in a meaningful manner. Treadmill workout just runs and tries to track even if you don't have it on your body. Problem is you can't go back and edit the distance. Who designed and tested this?

I'm continually in the search for an app that can track my cycling workouts. I've tried most of them and this app is just the worst. Generally, I get to about 3 miles and it shuts down or doesn't track my miles. Before you say it's my phone, I've used the same app on at least two other Android's and had the same issue. For a company with so many really awesome products that I use daily, this one is just...plain...awful.

I usually love this app, but in the last few days when I'm tracking a walk and trying to end it, it no longer shows as something that's active (not in my notifications or even in the app itself). It'll show it as an event that just completed. Hours later, I'll discover that it's still tracking, and my 45-minute walk has been going on for 16 hours.

Impossible to see my total workout for a day, I can only see the last exercise for that day. Keeps tracking my short drives as walks (how does it determine that I can walk 2 miles in 10 minutes or less). Track-Workout no longer has the option to modify a workout which is super inconvenient when I forget to conclude a workout and GoogleFit tracked a 2hr cardio/weight session, I then have to delete the workout and log it manually. Logging workouts manually is a pain, Google seems to know better how much time I worked out when it constantly changes the time I logged for a workout by adding more minutes to my workout, and trying to edit the workout doesn't seem to work as it continues to add more time to my workouts. Very disappointed and will be uninstalling

With this latest update to center around step tracking, Google shot the knees out of the only thing that made this app truly unique. Finally, there was a solution out there that worked for people with long strides, people who bike more than they hike, and people who value anaerobic exercise. Instead, they went with what "users were more familiar with". Way to act like every other tracking app out there. The API will still be useful for moving health data between apps, but that's about it.

This app doesn't even work as a pedometer. Connect to GPS or not, steps randomly fluctuate throughout the day. When walking with friends with other devices, I'm sometimes 1,000+ steps less than what their devices record. I've gone to bed with 20k steps displaying, just for it to jump down to 15k overnight. There is no rhyme or reason and no consistency. The GPS also never maps my full workout route. Using a stationary machine does not pick up half the time either.

Overall, I like the app. I've been using it to track my HIIT workouts for about a month and a half now, but I've got some suggestions: I don't like the focus on "Heart Points." I'd like to see a bigger focus on calories burned during a workout. That's what I'm really interested in since I count Macros/Calories each day. I'd also like to see features added to log body fat %, as well as body measurements.

I'm a new user! So far it's been nice, I just have one request/suggestion. Google already has text reading capabilities. I think it would be neat if there was an option to read labels and auto-fill nutrition info in the "calories consumed" panel. Would love it more if this were possible. Thank you 😁 From a slightly lazy user 😅 I also would like a way to look at my overall nutrition intake for the day without selecting the individual items and adding things up manually.

Google Fit was my favorite fitness app because it was straightforward to use without all the extra stuff most other apps have. The new version is awful. How are you supposed to count the steps when you swim? Or even bike? Or do yoga? All of those things used to be worth something to your daily goals, as they should because they are exercise! If I wanted to count my steps I'd get a pedometer! It's okay if they want to have this feature for those who want it, but give us a choice at least.

Useless. I got this app for the wellness program for my employer to get a reduction on my insurance. At first I just kept the phone in my pocket. Some days it registers the activity, most days it doesn't. Bought an arm band for the phone. The app registered nothing and I've got sweat running down my face. After 10 actual workouts the app registered 2. Absolutely useless.

Lame AF app. I set my sex to male and for some reason they thought that I wanted to track my menstrual cycle??? Good one Google 🤣 The app doesn't actually include anything that is useful for weightlifting besides recording time spent on that activity, but doesn't even allow you to delete activities recorded from other apps. The only good thing it has for people that are serious is a way to track calories and nutrient intake for meals, unless you wanna track "heart points", whatever that is 🤷‍♂

Unable to manually enter multiple fitness activities on the same day. I entered my weightlifting workout, and later entered my cardio workout. When I saved the second workout, the app combined the time,calories etc with the weightlifting entry. So when I look back at my activities, the cardio workout won't be listed. That makes this a pretty pointless app.

What the hell is going on with resting calorie burn? The only reason this app used to stand out above others was because of the projected calorie burn and the calorie goal progress widget that used this projected measure. No other fitness app does a projection of calories burned by the end of the day so that one can get a sense of how on track one is to reach one's goal. But they went and ruined it by taking away the widget, and now as of the past week, by messing up calorie burn estimates completely. It looks like resting calorie burned is not taken into account at all, so that it's impossible for me to reach 2000 calories even at the same or greater activity level as before. I've submitted many, many feedback comments within the app and have never heard from them. Is anyone even listening? Additionally it's not possible to edit workouts imported from Strava or Motifit or any other fitness tracker. Ughh. Please fix this! Move Points are unrelatable Nd useless!

Among the more useless fitness trackers. Sure, it tracks vitals ... But you have to manually enter the data. It's the year 2023 and Google still doesn't know there are fitness trackers out there that they can use to automatically track things like heartrate. Using this is like opening up Notepad and typing in your heartrate. What happened to you, Google?

I use it to track steps. However, there are many issues with it! It won't sync to my Galaxy Fit watch, my step counter resets to zero numerous times a day, it is supposed to link to other apps through Health Connect and it doesn't. The list of problems just keeps going. Don't try to figure out how to contact support because, as far as I have found, there isn't one.

For Strength Training, your WearOS counterpart has more functionality than your actual phone app? I cant manually tell it what reps I did in the Phone App? Also, it keeps track of my reps, but I cant really do anything with that data. I cant look up easily what I did last time for a specific lift. I cannot do a 1 rep max calculation. It really isnt very helpful to have information with no ability to do anything with it, or search it.  I would like the ability to have a list of all my exercises, what I did last time, what my 1 rep max would be, and other information like 80%, 90%, etc. If you are going to add weight lifting tracking, you need to add helpful weight lifting statistics. Maybe a drop down for if it was too easy, too hard, just right, etc.

For whatever reason, this app only tracks my heart rate from noon to 4. Nothing before and nothing after. I've been through every setting and there is nothing to adjust. I wish Samsung Health gave us the ability to track other parameters on things like rowing machines instead of giving us the choice to track a calorie goal or a set distance. Let me row for an hour and add my own details like the Garmin App. If it did, I'd drop Google Fit in a hot second.

Tracks activity pretty well when left alone, but almost never accurately tracks steps per minute for heart points. So today I decided to start an activity (to tell it I was actually going for a walk) to see if it would be more accurate. I walked a mile and it said I took ZERO steps and got zero move minutes. This is really irritating because, otherwise, it's a good app. I know Google doesn't REALLY care about their users (only advertisers), but I do wish they would fix this app.

I used this app for over a year because it was the best for tracking my strength training sessions (reps, timing, it recognized the exercise forms by your watch movements, etc.). I spent the money for a smart watch that specifically would let me use google products because I liked this app so much. Now there's nothing about this app I need. They got rid of the coolest features that made them better and different from other fitness apps.

I loved this app until Google did the update. The heart points don't make sense and no other information is really provided. I can see how the simplicity may appeal to some users, but I personally am not a fan. I liked the app better when I could see caloric expenditure and distance walked. Hoping they change it back soon or else I'll have to find a new fitness app to use.

it doesn't count your steps when the app isn't open/phone screen is off like it's supposed to, and there is no option to track health while pregnant for women. I keep getting told I need to lose weight, but I can't really do that while growing a human. Like normally I would agree, I'm not skinny by any means, but right this second my main goal is eating healthy so baby grows well. Fix these two things and I'll give 5 stars.

Stops counting steps randomly throughout the day, won't even count them when I manually start a workout. I've gone through all the steps to try and fix it, all my settings are fine, everything that should be on/off is, tried deleting all data/uninstalling and reinstalling multiple times, restarting phone etc. I have this to track steps for pokemon go since being a stay at home mom means I'm very active around the house but can't get out as much. I was feeling extra motivated to up my activity levels but with all the tracking problems it's led to nothing but frustration and disappointment. Google Fit is a garbage app and Niantic should be kicking them selves for using it as one of their only 2 step counters.

It's amazing to me that Google can't seem to make an app that syncs properly with the phone and a wear watch. Constantly having to manually edit the number of steps. If the battery dies on the watch it will not always sync correctly when you restart it. You have to go in and estimate your fitness data and enter it manually because it syncs typically a much lower with step tracking. If you walk away from the phone with your watch on when you come back, sync is way off and it's getting worse.

I would have given this app 5 stars, but Google made a change and now it's 3 stars. Home shows you steps, calories, distance, heart points, and move minutes for the current day. Journal displays steps, heart points, and workouts. It no longer shows total move minutes for prior days. I was using this app to track total minutes moved per day, so now I have to use a different app. Journal does not display calories burned. Otherwise it works well. Easy to log workouts. Easy to synch w other apps.

It's not very informative at getting people into an active lifestyle, but is VERY good at being a "Fitness tracker" app. It seems to accurately count my steps when compared to a pedometer, can track heart rate and respiratory rate in-app as well. Takes a bit to figure out what various connectivity functions do. I just dropped it to 3 stars. No way to delete a specific entry, can only delete all data for a fitness category, or ALL data completely; any inaccurate reading cannot be overridden.

This app is questionable. I have my smart watch synced with it, but even when I'm not wearing the watch, the app is showing I'm burning 45 calories every hour. And the calorie count is way off when I do wear the watch. I understand this is just an "estimate", but this apps "estimate" is off by at least 500 kcal compared to more accurate "estimates" from other apps.

Just as others describe, the map/distance tracking is abysmal. If I walk a 4-block square route, it shows most of a triangular route, which doesn't correlate with my route at all. I've sent feedback on this before, and it's just gotten worse. I'm surprised that a company that does mapping and geolocation and can sense how many mph I'm traveling doesn't use the GPS sensors more precisely to see the route I'm walking.

Would like to see more integration of apps. I use a cpap machine. My air by res-med. Although I use a watch app by veryfit. Would alsoike to see more of a back and forth sharing between apps where that is allowed. This way health and patterns that can aid Doctors, nutritionist and specialists. Can gain an idea of what info is available for insight. Also, what maybe close to the target range of what is happening. If this can be done. I'll give it 5 stars

Would really like to rate this app better. The app will sync after being checked earlier in the day, move minutes will be added but distance removed. The app has also decided that movement on the gym equipment doesn't count for distance traveled, even though it can track your number of steps in that time and give you move minutes. Unfair, unsatisfactory, and inappropriate when you're pregnant and just trying to keep up your weekly distance when there's a heat advisory and unsafe air alert.

Most apps get better with time, but this app has only degraded. It stopped tracking activities. Then even manually starting activity didn't work and kept giving error. So I uninstalled the app and reinstalled and now it won't even login to my account. It keeps saying " Can't verify account. Check your internet connection". Clearly I have internet connection as I'm writing this review. They don't seem to have testing at all.

Doesn't sync up heart rate data other than daily avg from my connected device, but the devices app records it every 2 min and tracks/calculates data. App keeps resetting my entire day's move and heart points when I'm half way through a workout!! My weight is automatically changed back after I update it. So many bugs. No widgets. Wow!

I loved this app, but ever since the latest update that changed interface, it has also been dumbed down. I cannot set my own goals anymore, nor edit calories spent in activity, nor see history properly,, and to boot, it doesn't recognise activities well anymore! Outright useless! My first reaction was "why the heck would someone dumb down their own app?!", which my partner (software dev) explained - to get more revenue in percentage cut from other apps that are sold/installed. Thank you, Google, now I have to lose two or more years of data and start searching for a new app 🤬

All of my data from last week disappeared. Not sure what happened, but other reviewers seem to have experienced something similar. 0 stars for that blunder, Google. You've failed me on hardware (Nexus 6p)... Ughh. If you're going to start doing that with software, it's time to explore other options.

The wide selection of workouts that can be tracked is very helpful, and it accurately tracks my steps wherever I go The battery drain isn't noticable when doing just walking, however when tracking a workout like skating or biking it can sometimes pull a little more. All in all it's a good estimate of calories burned, steps taken, and a healthy amount of activity

I'm using Samsung watch with Samsung Health for tracking steps, that is linked through Health Connect with Google Fit. But for some absolutely ridiculous reason it only syncs data at 23:59 every day. What's the point... To sync another app and not to see the data here. The biggest problem is, in the settings I can clearly see the data gets accessed every time you open the app, so Fit has the data all the time. It just doesn't show them!!? This also breaks integration with other apps. Stupid

I really love tracking my dog's walk but the location tracking is not very accurate. Our 2.12 mile route is the same every time but the map results are always different, sometimes by .2 of a mile. Sometimes it shows us on the sidewalk and other days we walk through a building at the exact same place. I really wish it was more accurate so we could clearly track our stats without doing the math. (I would also love to be able to sync a dog pedometer.)

Used this app to track my steps. It was working fine until 8/18/19 then it just stopped. I clicked on the steps to see if that would help but it just seemed to keep loading. I uninstalled and reinstalled. Then I couldn't log in. Just kept me loading. I finally shutdown and restarted my phone. I was able to log in and view everything again, that I thought was not being recorded. This seems to be the same problem with other users.

This app has turned into complete garbage. It used to be great, and it still tracks your steps. However it now does not track workouts correctly. When you start a workout to track it often just disappers after 5 or 10 minutes. You can then add it manually, however when you add a 2nd workout activity for the day the first one disappers. Virtually worthless for tracking actual workouts. Seems now their focus is just more accurate location based and step data to tie togwther for maps and ads. Sad.

I carried my phone with me at a dance event in hopes of getting a measure of just how much movement there was. Afterwards, the app showed a grand total of 39 steps for that entire period. From looking at several days of history, it seems like the app might only record steps when you have recently been using your phone and goes to sleep when the screen has been off for a while.

Doesn't track accurately and then doesn't allow you you to edit anything to improve it's accuracy.  Response to comment below. "I've tried that multiple times as instructed by the help section. However when I follow those steps the only sections it lets you edit are the activity title and notes section. It shows no selection box for changing distance, time, etc..."

When I first installed in September, 2019 it was great. I have my MiFit app attached to this. For some reason, it no longer keeps my move minutes and heart points. Minutes and points just disappear. I got notified that I had exceeded 150 heart points, opened the app and it shows only 64. All points from previous days were reduced or gone. I don't have my phone on me all day, so I depend on the data from MiFit.

Dear Fit Developers, I love your app I use it everyday and keeps me motivated to exercise daily! I have a complaint and a suggestion to make, however. - I think that the app could be better designed in terms of what are buttons that one can use to access more information (like there are no affordances to tell you that you can touch the distance/calories/steps numbers in the homepage). - I would love it if one could change the calorie count showed to Net calories burned instead of gross calories.

It's unfortunate, but this does very little to track the fitness level and goals of someone who is tracking with a watch. I don't take my phone on long runs, I leave it on the bench when I play sports, and its too much hassle to manually input everything. If it would connect with Garmin or Strava and add those steps, that would be outstanding! In the mean time, it does not really help. Good thought though!

This app has been simplified to the point that it's barely even functional anymore. I can't disable the automatic tracking that thinks I'm walking when I get stuck in traffic (the article says it should be in the settings, but it isn't), I can't edit a journal entry to change the intensity, I can't set long term goals. Looking for something else now.

I loved the old version of Google fit, but the new one is so limited in its features. You can't access half the stats you used to be able to, step count is no longer a forefront feature, and it sends you judgy notifications when you miss a week because you're busy. No, I don't want to adjust my goals! Bring back the old version, it was so much better!

Used to work great, but after the recent update a couple weeks ago the app is no longer working. Keeps saying "an error has been corrected" when trying to record trips. The GPS, distance, speed, and routes are all inaccurate. Wish they wouldn't have tried to fix something that wasn't broken. It's also annoying that they're always trying to push the paid subscription on users, even though it offers only slightly more usable features vs the free app did before it became full of errors.

Solid app, but most features have been slowly moved into the subscription so that as a free user, you are essentially just giving the company your data. Other basic fitness apps have feature parity with the free version now, so unless you're ready to pay I would explore alternatives. To add insult to injury, in 2025 they have sued Garmin with some patents that never should have been granted. The second any issue with Garmin linking occurs I will delete this and never use it again.

Pixel 7. Latest update. Every single time I watch a video, the audio from that video will continue to play on repeat until I force quit the app. Even after I go to another video, even after I go to another app, even after I turn off the screen! The audio from that video won't stop looping! It's crazy-making!

The tracking consistently adds about 2-3% extra distance which doesn't sound like a lot but for a 20 mile run is about half a mile. The app also puts all this AI garbage in your face. Nobody wants a coach that only says how great you are at everything. The app is also pretty buggy and wouldn't let me finish the recording for my first marathon ever so I don't get to look at all the inaccurate stats it recorded.

This app motivates me to push through my fitness goals, post about them and, share bike routes with others. It's been very fun, and I can't wait to discover more bike routes around the country and globe. I must add, the consistency of the GPS and share functions help my loved ones keep peace of mind while I'm hit the paths with a group or solo.

I've been using Strava infrequently, sharing my activities with a few friends. But when I recently tried to use it to start recording activity, it interrupted me by displaying a message reading "Upgraded feature" and "Recording just got simpler." In my hurry and the bright outdoor sun, I couldn't find how to make the message go away so the app would start recording. So, Strava really failed here. After similar problems later, I deleted my account and uninstalled the app.

love the app. I'm probably doing something wrong but my heart rate doesn't read from my fit bit. And I wish when hitting pause on a work out, it didn't keep time during the pause time and add it to the workout time. that is why I picked a 4 star. otherwise, the app is great! I like all the features. too many to list. I run, walk, lift, and do other workouts. I like the how it keeps MPH and distance.

Strava's decent. When I first began using Strava many years ago, I had access to great features under the free version. Little by little my favorite features transferred from the free version to the paid only version which I don't subscribe to because every app does the same thing and my income has changed. There are a lot of glitches with my Strava tracked distance. It very often shows a track I didn't take and says I walked or jogged several miles more than I did.

Strava will not stop spamming me with requests to upgrade to the paid subscription. However, I would never even consider upgrading when there are so many bugs and errors on the app. Please stop asking for money until you have an app that works. The only reason I am still here is because I love the third party website Strava feeds its data to. Once I figure out how to go around, I'm out. Currently, Strava is telling me my last walk was my longest ever. It's not my longest walk in distance or time

This app consistently stops recording halfway through my run and I lose my entire other half. so all of my numbers and records and distances and progress are wrong, and it's incredibly frustrating. I've reported this problem several times and haven't gotten a single reply. It has not been reliable for me. I'm really disappointed :(

my free version of this app has worked well for a year . Now i can't use it, it keeps saying it has a problem when i try. it also loses data connectivity when before it was just fine. I can't turn off the screen anymore or it quits recording. Completely unuseable now. any suggestions? I've tried updating and reinstalling, no improvement.

I hate everything about this app. I just want a place to record what I do. No social media. No trends. No workouts. No competitions. just a place to record my activities, neutrally.
