But this app just forces everything down your throat. it's like going to the gym on my tablet. a truly *awful* experience. But if you like those things, it works well.

Useless. "Strava recovered from a problem" and loses half of my route. EVERY. SINGLE. TIME. And yes, it had al permissions, battery saving disabled, yada yada. Unreliable, avoid like the plague. Even more garbage than Sports Tracker. 90%+ of my recent dozens of workouts randomly stop wrong in the middle, and I losing tracking. And I have all the battery saving settings disabled, all permissions given, yada yada yada.

The free version of the app is great. It connects to many brands of devices and gives me a way to interact with others who live farther away. Lately, the app has been offering a free 30 day trial. Like constantly, with a countdown timer. I'd be interested, except I can't actually sign up - the app just gives me an error message. So maybe the developers should make sure the free trial feature works before making a gazillion splash screens that don't work.

Horrible app. It seems to only work if you take out a paid subscription. Despite it supposedly being able to import information from other apps it would not do so from my Garmin app. However the final deal breaker was when it wouldn't let me log in with my Google account went through the whole process of resetting the account and then it wouldn't let me in without presenting me with captcha visuals. I'm not stupid but after trying about 10 times I could never get past it.

Your app is making it difficult to delete my account. I only download for a specific reason but realized I didn't need too i owned a device that does this already. So every time I hit the delete account it keeps taking me out to your web page to enter my email and keeps telling me I have to log out first when I'm not logged in. And when I'm about to log in after I receive a code it does it all over again. can you delete my account on your end? This is getting irritating.

This app and the service in general is absolute garbage. I'm giving it one extra star as it has some basic niche utility. but it is highly unreliable. Workouts randomly disappear from the feed or, worse yet, will not appear at all even if they are able to appear on your profile page. people cheat by pausing for long periods and then resuming their workouts which can lead to faster paces than a normal experience. Features randomly go away. Customer service is AI slop. Don't reccomend.

Buggy & expensive. Currently in free trial period. The (huge) cost of a subscription would only be good value if the app was amazing, you use it loads & the fancy features useful. It's not, I don't, & they're not (for me). App constantly nags to get me to do things I don't want to do. In use (cycling), the map randomly resets from North-up to orientation-up, with no input from me, & is often 90deg out! I end up having to interact with little on-screen buttons when riding, which is dangerous.

It was great, but recent changes have ruined the app. 1/ Zooming out of a map results in a white screen. 2/ Constantly having to put heatmaps back on, as the map keeps unselecting them. 3/ AI run reports are actually so bad. 4/ App never loads half the time. Come on, Strava, pull your finger out!

Simple and effective community running app that is sorely missing certain effective tools and features. One of my biggest complaints has to do with the fact that when I do a run I want to see my "total time", but strava prioritizes "moving time". Another issue is just recently I had issues with GPS and my distance was stated to be 0.28 KM while my watch was indicating I went 1.80KM. I want to adjust the distance however I am unable to edit my activity. This is honestly frustrating.

Strava used to be the King of all running apps, but since they put most all their previously free features behind a monthly paywall, I don't rate it high at all & only maintain an account for the sake of keeping in touch with distant friends I used to run with. Strava is certainly not my go to running app anymore. I'd give it 1 & a half stars but it wouldn't let me so I gave it 2. Actually,No; I give it 1 star for screwing us skint runners over.

Strava has become aggressively paywalled, bombarding free users with constant trial prompts. I'm here to support others, not for features—I use Garmin for that. Pushing deceptive trial tactics (like auto-charging users) is exploitative. This strategy risks alienating the very community that gives Strava its value. It's short-sighted and erodes trust. I'll leave if this continues.

great for tracking your activities. also not great at tracking your activities. It Has issues with auto resuming from auto pause, sometimes just draws a straight line and doesnt even count the distance. most of the time it just stops the whole recording on your first stop. this doesnt happen with ever other app. I am speaking from more than a decade of usage.

Pretty disappointed with the whole experience. GPS on for activities like pádel or tennis doesn't make much sense. No dedicated boxing activity (I know, originally it was for running, cycling and swimming, but it has evolved since then), not possible to reorder activities on the watch. Also, it won't import/read activities from any other source. And the UI is far from being clean. I guess it's not for me.

Not worth the money for a paid subscription. The app mainly duplicates data already visible in Samsung Health, and although there are some differences it doesn't justify the cost. I also find it inflexible when trying to just record runs, instead it gives me everything with GPS data. I don't need to know my stats for a walk to the shop. Final reason I stopped using it was because it stopped capturing data from Samsung, and I'm not paying for an unreliable app

After switching to Strava last year, I was quite happy with it. The last three months I have been unable to log into the app. Login to the website works fine, the app just doesn't accept the mailed code. I've been waiting for support for three weeks now, and finally they admitted: 'Unfortunately, our team is limited in our ability to investigate these sorts of issues directly.' Lots of people with the same issues. Not important of course. App is fine when it works, but support is just terrible.

Used to really like the app and it got even better once I got a Pixel Watch 3 but not anymore. This past month or so it has stopped tracking from the actual start though it seems to have the correct distance, and at other times it shows the correct route but has started shaving off half a mile occasionally. Very bizarre. Have tried updating the phone and watch, as well as checking calibrations.

I am very disappointed with the Strava app. It does not work reliably. Many times, the app fails to record activities properly. Even when I start a run, it only begins recording after 200–300 meters, which is not accurate. Recently, my last three activities were not saved at all, which is extremely frustrating after putting in the effort. An app meant for tracking fitness should be accurate and dependable, but this one has become very inconsistent. I hope the developers fix these issues .

Another perfect example of enshittification. Over time, more and more essential features are being locked behind a paywall. Basic challenges such as "Walk 50km" are gone, so you'd pay to set your own challenges; Records and medals just changed to only track your times of the current year instead of all time; Yearly recap is now only for paying subscribers; etc. Oh and of course you're bombarded with begging to pay for premium features.

It was working fine then a few weeks ago it started automatically recording everything as a bike ride instead of a run. slightly annoying but the one star is because it no longer works. I start to record a run, it works fine, then when I finish the run it just sits there thinking about it and I can never hit save. I've restarted my phone and reinstalled it but the same thing keeps happening. The app is now useless.

Strava is the absolute gold standard for tracking runs, rides, and outdoor adventures. The GPS tracking is incredibly accurate, the interface is clean, and the data breakdowns give you the perfect amount of insight without being overwhelming. If it's not on Strava, it didn't happen!

In the 2 years since I first installed Strava it's been easy to use and lots of helpful data on the free user version. It's very inspiring for a novice runner. Since Saturday though there seems to be a bug. I cannot save runs, it's hanging. I've tried multiple options on Reddit thread on the problem to reset but no luck. Seems like it's since the 20th April upgrade. I can no longer use the app to record and monitor my runs. Disappointing

Strava used to be perfect. Never a problem. In the last few months though, it's barely worked at all. Just stops recording, and misses half of my run. Makes it look like I've run a direct line from point to point, through houses and rivers. It's unreliable with my speed, once saying I was going less than 1km/hr and 35km/hr in the same run (can confirm I wasn't). A bit frustrated at this point!

Horrendous app. Logging in doesn't work half the time. Recording auto cancel some runs. Tried to cancel a run, only to find a week later it is still running, cannot cancel as you must wait for all the "run" data to load. Only solution I've found on the web is to log out and log back in to effectively cancel the run. Currently logged out of the app because the login in is bugged once again.

Leading athletics app until they put every single feature that was previously free behind a paywall. its essentially a notebook using google map without the subscription. My second week using it I couldnt share any maps as they were all gltiched and broken platform side, which was confirmed by support. I get wanting to make money but now the app the same as or worse than every other fitness app.

Update 18 Jun 2026: still can't pace for non-run events. Now the app hides my challenges within challenges that I have no desire in ever doing. Time to mark down when this subscription ends. A great app for tracking your events. However, you only get feedback during the activity when you are running. I don't run, I cycle and walk, and would like to know what my pace is during the event without having to turn the app on and causing my phone to use more battery life.

This is the only fraud app which automatically goes to the subscription page when your phone is in your pocket. It even enters the card details itself (although incorrect). And it is not the first time. I faced this issue multiple times. Uninstalling it and putting it out there to check if anyone else is facing the same issue.

Honestly the best running and biking app available. Tracks location and pace wonderfully, and offers a lot of good and interesting stats. The free trial subscription was so good that I'm considering signing up, but it is not necessary to get a lot out of the app.

New update, The continuing of cheating of e-bikes on segments, still a problem!! Not being able to see times while going up hill or down is cumbersome. I understand the safety aspect of it, but show us something towards the last couple hundred feet or so, so that we know when the segments ends. No notification of KOM's being lost, another annoying reason from Strava. It's not fun anymore ☹️ price hike. Everytime I click the app it goes to app info and won't go into the app.

After updating app now glitches, flickers and won't open at all, all within a fraction of a second. Phone still has a modern enough operating system to run the app. No clue what causes it. Tried everything to fix. All other apps in my phone still work. Given up.

I have thoroughly enjoyed the app. I like the activity summaries and the social aspect of it. Strava needs to expand the options available to set as "default". In particular default maps (i.e. choosing not to use the live route playback) and default body model for weight training. Also, I like to share my Workout Analysis panel, but every time I screenshot, I get a pop up saying "use our share function". I'm not using it because the options don't show what I'm trying to share!!

worst app incorrect distance tracking people think they are running fast but it is their myth actually the app tracking the wrong distance if they are running 3km it shows them 3.5 and some time 4km by which we are running on a good pace but it is not correct i tried it for the same route 5 times and each time I got different distance measurement and not minor 10 or 20 meter difference,the difference is about 200 and 500 meter and some time above 500 is normal .

Dear Strava Support, I hope you are doing well. I am following up on my support request, as I have not yet received a response since providing the additional information you requested. It has now been 6 business days, and I am becoming increasingly concerned. I am still unable to access my Strava account, which means I cannot upload any of my activities. As someone who uses Strava regularly and has years of running history on my account, this has been extremely frustrating.

Dear Strava Team, I am writing to request that you add a setting to disable the share prompt that appears whenever a screenshot is taken of an activity. While I understand the desire to encourage social sharing, this popup interferes with my device's scroll capture function. The overlay prevents me from capturing the full details I need for my records, which is quite frustrating. Please consider making this feature optional in a future update. Thank you for your continued work on the app!

I've used the free version for years without issue. Recently the app would close during usage and upon completion of a ride would state it had recovered from an issue, with nothing recorded. Tried to fix the issue by unistalling and reinstalling the app. Now strava wont even recognize the code they send to log in. So totally locked out. A whole new low for strava.

Fix Your Distance Issues!!! I'm uninstalling this because the distance never syncs up with my Galaxy Watch 7/Samsung Health and it always says I ran a shorter distance. Maybe if this synced properly I would be willing to pay for it... but certainly not interested in even using it when it makes it seem like I'm doing worse I actually am.

Better than nothing certainly, but they've lost focus on the user and the fact they are likely exhausted when using the app. You need big areas to click on to reverse an accidental touch, rather than two accidental touches of the main screen area landing you trapped in a full screen map with no option than to click the back arrow, leaving the activity. The number of times I have to stop my run, sort it out and get going again

Easy to Use and navigation. Very good Distance accuracy and Time accuracy. We can see the records of STRAVA app users in the World and can send appreciation comments and can give Kudos to them . while using this app low Battery consumption. Every one's Personal Sports information Record . no charge ,Cost for to hold normal records. App have an appealing design . by using this App you can improve your sports technique by seeing your segments record . Very nice Best and Beautiful STRAVA App .

I tried everything to make this app work. I locked it so it doesn't auto-close, gave it every needed permission, even made sure I had enough battery so my phone doesn't go in battery saver mode (because the app won't work properly in battery saver mode as per the in-app message). After all this the app still fails to record my activity properly. I had simple expectations but the app doesn't meet them, and needs significant improvements. If only it had worked, I would've bought the subscription..

Hi, I’d like to follow up on my refund request submitted yesterday regarding an automatic deduction from my cash account. The funds were intended for my school expenses. I understand that I may have activated a free trial, but I did not intend to subscribe to the one-year plan. I was also not clearly notified or asked for confirmation before the payment was charged to my account. I find it frustrating that the payment was automatically deducted without explicit confirmation. Please.

There was a recent update that added more animations everytime I complete an activity, there doesn't seem to be an option to disable these and they are laggy and annoying. App also frequently crashes and does not track my activity accurately. Initiating a recording of an activity sometimes causes my playing media to pause.

I wa.t to subscribe but so far I am unable to connect strava to my whoop. In whoop it shows that strava is connected and it reads data fekm strava. However in strava when I tried to connect it showed me that an error occurred. I can't see the connection in strava. I emailed support many days ago and so far no reply. If I dknt get a reply I simply won't subscribe.

😠 App going downhill again. Too much junk, not enough control. Wish unwanted features could be switched off or moved to secondary pages. Progress is irrelevant nonsense, I don't use devices during tennis, gym, running squash etc. I'd hide challenges and workouts too, especially distractions in my feed (which is now 2 interactions sideways). Overall not a patch on Ride With GPS at creating routes, following routes live, or looking at performance in detail.

​"Strava is the absolute gold standard for anyone who loves running, cycling, or outdoor activities. The GPS tracking is incredibly accurate, and the 'Segments' feature adds an amazing, healthy layer of competition against your own past times and the local leaderboard. What really sets it apart is the community aspect—it feels like a positive, unfiltered social media platform where everyone actively cheers each other on. It’s highly motivating and easily a 5-star app!"

Good app when it works, but it keeps failing at the worst possible moment. I did a long distance cycling session for hours, and Strava suddenly failed to save my progress. All that distance, effort, and data just gone. This has happened more than once. It’s frustrating because tracking rides is literally the main purpose of the app. Imagine pushing through a long ride only to get hit with "activity not saved". Needs serious fixing for stability and sync reliability. Thankyou

Its not the first time I have a issues with Strava as other companies that trick users offering free trials and create tricky pages with small letters to take money out of users without authorisation. I absolutely despise it, its such a scam. we don't have a team online to help. hate it.

Much improved reliability since my 2 star review of 6 years ago. That focused on the poor Galaxy watch implementation. It's a lot better on the Pixel Watch 3 and I'll assume other watches are similarly improved. Areas for improvement would be in the auto hiding of start/end. By default you only want that when you're near your home.

Accidentally purchased yearly subscription over monthly and support is impossible to contact. Would have loved to sign up for a monthly subscription, but now reconsidering as they have not responded to me in over two weeks. Will gladly update this review if helped.

Everything is a beg to buy. Every screen you change to has a big ad click to buy, subscribe for this, do this. EVERY screen. You spend half your time just getting rid of the subscribe now screens. Other tools do much more and don't annoy you to death. Used to be good, now just a huge begging game where your activity is assaulted with begging.

April 2026, did not work on GrapheneOS. I was disappointed I couldn't use Strava as it was automatically blocked through the Play Integrity API despite its proven increased security. May 2026, elated to share that it's been resolved quickly and can return to recommending Strava. Share and strengthen you and your friend's fitness goals, journeys, and wins through the one app. I hope the subscription becomes affordable but open to different tiers to support.

I used to use this app all the time for tracking runs, bikes, etc and loved it. I deleted the app a little while ago. Now I'm in a new area and wanted to see what trails are around so I download Strava again only to be hugely disappointed. I don't see how free users utilize this app- you can't do anything! If you're happy with paying a subscription for this app then go for it but I can't bring myself to pay for something that I used to get for free. Strava is nice to have- not a necessity.

Auto pause feature does not work as intended, not even close. Everybody using this app pauses themselves. And the entire app and most of the communities are built around unhealthy obsessions with sports and number games. Strava would make you run to your death to make money.
freezes a lot. Also, doesn't let you add if you forget to start the app, you can only crop if you forget to stop. A bit clunky overall. I would never pay for such an unreliable tracker.

They recently updated this app and it is now pure garbage. I use it on Wear OS 4 and used to track every single exercise. It is so incredibly buggy now. I have to leave the app in the foreground or it stops tracking. Never used to happen. Sometimes it goes to the background automatically, but won't pause. But sometimes it does pause. I'm absolutely disgusted with the devs. 40 minutes into an exercise to find I only got 5 minutes of heartrate tracking is insanely enraging. I *hate* this app.

it's barely usable these past few weeks. hangs constantly. won't load any of my other activities while it spends hours uploading the latest one. doesn't stop recording when I press stop. the latest Android version is a mess. if you haven't upgraded yet, don't. it's a total mess!! hopefully the developers release a new version soon to fix all the bugs.

Glitchy mess. It wouldn't save two exercises, for the 1st one I was able to delete it since it was stuck in the save mode, for the second one I was unable to delete and had to uninstall and reinstall. I was thinking of paying for the premium version, but I can't if this app is so unreliable. What a joke. Don't waste your money, use the free version.

Hi Strava team, I understand the need for age limits, but I think there should be a safe solo mode for younger users. Some kids just want to track their cycling, distance, and progress for fun without using social features. A restricted account with private activities, no followers, and no public sharing would make Strava more accessible while keeping safety and privacy in mind. Thanks for considering it.

used to be better when the app didn't force you to search the exercise to get the model that shows what body part you worked. having to go to the list instead of just highlighting the body part is a total drag

the wear OS app is pretty bad, sometimes it stops running, sometimes doesn't continue running in the background, it doesn't allow you to pin your favourite sports or to show most recently used sports, also doesn't seem to sync up with the mobile app during workout, only after completed. Android app also constantly bugs out and I lose my activities, disgrace of an app, and that's using a premium subscription, won't even use it for free as it's too unreliable

It's clunky. Those in charge don't realize you may be ready to run or bike and have to press a button to get it started, so you get the app all ready and press the record button and the app fumbles like it has no idea what you're trying to do. I'm biking and have traffic around me. I want a button to start and no extra BS. I've had rides I thought recorded but didn't because of this. And rides I've done in the past are now too short, just trying to time a hill segment and Strava won't work.

It just keeps getting worse. I left a review recently about how the map no longer zooms in & focuses on me when GPS lock is achieved. It also seems like when I first open the app the first timeline screen never really loads, just black screen. Now this morning I finished my 4mi walk, opened Strava to end the activity, noticed the app was slow to open back up, & when it did it said I was at 2.7 miles. The app gave up somewhere along my walk. Same route as hundreds of other walks. Changing apps.

I am unable to follow the people and this error is showing on strava "the strava server has encountered an error. please contact support if the problem persists." Iam not able to follow people from last 1-2 days. please fix this!😡😡😡😡

Keeps listing false 'achievements' eg. A recent ride showed '9 achievements'. When I checked all results, none of my times for any segments were in my best 3. Always a major disappointment! Limited functions on free version eg you can only see 5 or 6 of your previous efforts for segments and these are greyed out, so barely visible.

great sports app for athletes both professional and amateurs alike. almost every sport is covered. however, quite some glitches, if you are a fast cyclist for example, Strava thinks that you're cheating and says that you must use an e-bike even when you 1. only have a normal road bike and 2. cannot even afford an expensive e-bike.

I love Starva as a stress free way ti track my workouts. They're fun and motivating challenges to join that help me keep going. I've currently logged 30 activities in the past 2 weeks because of Strava, where before I had barely any movement. I always look forward to logging my workouts on Strava, but I never feel pressured. Looking forward to seeing to my mood and health improve even more.

The login screen is completely broken for users running GrapheneOS which is far more secure than default android or apple. ​When I enter my password or one-time code, the submission button fails silently and does absolutely nothing. This is a known issue with Strava’s rigid backend, which hangs indefinitely when it can't run tracking or Play Integrity checks, rather than failing gracefully and letting the user log in. ​Please fix and I will change to 5 stars.

The app does not track distance properly!!! I switched to Strava from Adidas Running this spring when a known software glitch in Running caused it to stop syncing activities from my watch. Strava worked great for a while. Now it's counting 30 mile bike rides as 16 miles and 16 mile bike rides as 9. The loop I ride is 8 miles. 2 laps can't be 9 miles!!!!

my first run i ran 6 miles, it didn't measure properly and showed 4.76. I know I ran 6 because I live in the country and I ran 3 blocksout and then back in. edit: I gave it a second try. turns out if you're not actively on the tab when its recording it doesn't track distance, just time. so I ran 8mi, tracked 2.8. I'm wholeheartedly disappointed.
It's a great app to keep you accountable while storing all your historical activities. The app provides feedback and analytics. Plus, you can join groups and interact with other users.

Won't connect to Samsung Health. Can't use the website on mobile without it transferring you to the app which doesn't work well. Overall app is only useful if you don't already having a tracking app that you want to use. Was excited to try this app, but now I'm just disappointed.

As with all apps, the more features added, the more it breaks existing stuff. I keep seeing cycling segment PR's on the ride headline but clicking through they aren't there (and they aren't PR's anyway!). Year headings on personal segment times on Android are missing now. Starting Strava sometimes fails with just a spinning ring in the app. Luckily I use Garmin to record my rides and just transfer to Strava so I don't know what else might be broken.

Too many network errors while other apps have no issues. Contacted me in a very ICE manner that I had breached their rules, their rationale was based on an erroneous machine translation of the name of a road. Route mapping has become route napping. Just did 62, it logged 50. Average speed and performance on segments all void. Liaising with support is the same three times over: here is the support page that will explain the steps you need to take, but none worked. Why pay?

Strava is an excellent app for tracking running, cycling, and other fitness activities. The GPS tracking, performance stats, and activity sharing features make workouts more motivating and engaging. The app works smoothly and is especially useful for athletes and fitness enthusiasts. However, some advanced features and detailed insights require a subscription. Overall, it’s one of the best fitness tracking apps available.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Great data tracking, even for free users!As a basic user, I find this app incredibly useful. It syncs perfectly with my Amazfit watch via the Zepp app, meaning all my rides and activities upload automatically. I love that I can manually add other types of workouts too. It is a fantastic tool to keep all my fitness data in one place.

Liked the app at first, but doesnt seem to track treadmill runs at all. Then if I track them manually on my watch, I need to log into a browser to add them, as the "add manual" option doesnt appear to show anywhere in the app? Appreciate "serious" runners might always run outdoors, but im still running the same distance on a treadmill so id still like to see it recorded. Also the homepage widget on android never updates and needs to be deleted and readded after every run to show weekly progres

The watch app is utterly useless. When I'm tracking my workout, I want the app to be constantly open so that I can see my pace and other parameters. But after a while the app goes into the background and I cannot configure it to stay open. And the ads coercing you to buy are super frequent and annoying.

It's a decent app but it doesn't track the GPS all the time and randomly flicks the route to somewhere I haven't been, and then back again. So I really have no idea how much I have walked. If it zips my location half a mile somewhere and back, that's a mile that I didn't walk. Maybe good in cities with good GPS but where I live it's not reliable as a walk/run tracker. The elevation may not be correct if it zips my location to the top of a hill I just walked down then back to the bottom. Shame.

What happened to this app? I haven't used it in about 3 years but a friend sent me a link on a hike he wants to take me on. The link gave me errors on the browser, I installed the app, still got errors. So then I decided I'm just going to search and see if I can find it. No I have to pay to search! just for a search? You've got to be kidding me. And a search I'm having to perform because the link he copied directly from your app doesn't work. That's suspicious. I hope all greedy developers fail.

Strava is honestly one of the most motivating fitness apps I’ve used. It makes tracking workouts feel social, fun, and competitive in a healthy way. I love the community energy, the ‘kudos’ feature, and how easy it is to see my progress over time. It keeps me consistent and accountable especially on days I don’t feel like training. Highly recommend for anyone serious about fitness or just trying to stay active.

This app is nice and all, but there's an insane annoying amount of ads and for some reason the track recorder thingy doesn't record my track whenever my phone is off but the app is still running. I tested it out, and noticed the trail was inaccurate. Please fix this. Please also fix this annoying bug where I move the map a bit but then it moves back automatically. It's extremely annoying and would be amazing for it to be fixed.

I just joined a few weeks ago and have only been able to log in twice. Then they started emailing me a security code and when I enter it, it brings me straight to an email automatically addressed to Strava with the subject line of "account suspended". I have sent several emails and it's been 10 days and still can't get in

For last 2 weeks the App doesn't work. Everytime after a activity, when I try to submit or finish , it doesn't let me complete. Though my internet/ wifi is fine. It keeps loading throughout. I have uninstall and install everytime to work it back normally. By doing that, I will not able to save that activity which i tracked and it gets discarded automatically. what is happening with strava now a days ???

Pathetic app. Using the paid version on a flagship Android phone, but the running readings are always higher than Nike Run Club, Mi Fitness watch, and my phone’s health app. Worse, many runs are not even getting saved after completion. I tried everything all permissions granted, battery optimization off, restart, uninstall/reinstall still not working. Very disappointing. Kindly refund my money.

This used to be amazing. I bought the subscription to see if the app was better with it & ever since I keep getting the following error messages - "Strava has encountered a problem. You activity has been restarted". I have uninstalled twice. It works ok for a couple of days & bang it happens again. DO NOT PAY FOR THE SUBSCRIPTION. It's just not worth it

The story of corporate greed. It started as functioning running app where you and friends can hang. Now they would force feed you "pro" ads every other time you use it, sign you up for corporate challenges, a third of functions on the page don't work unless you pay for the app, and you can not even see your manual splits without a paid account.

I'm not sure if it depends only on Openstreetmap, but since today when opening the map of an activity there is way too much visual clutter, with too many small paths that (sometimes) barely exist, and should not appear unless someone zooms in a lot, in my opinion. App has always been great, but I find this very annoying.

It's one of the best apps in its field with smooth and interactive UI andmany advanced features but I only have one concern that is after the latest update many free features have been transferred to the Subscription plans. Bro why are you guys doing this you it was as good as it is before that with sufficient features now I have no choice but to use other apps. And it's not like I haven't thought about taking the Subscription but that's already out of budget for me atleast.

what is strava even doing??? the app has been absolutely bugged for several days atp and they still haven't fixed anything, I've already done and recorded several activities in the app and every single time, it would get stuck loading whenever I try to upload the activity and it even lost the data of two of them.

I've used Strava for years with a paid subscription. The subscription is very expensive but I always thought it was worth it due to Segment analysis. But recently Strava started to add a bunch of unwanted features like workouts. it's so annoying I decided to cancel my subscription. Now the app is even less usable because every time you try to do anything a big popup shows up trying to get you to pay a subscription. This app is going to the trash bin soon.

At first, I was happy about this app but then after a few days it somehow availed the free trial by itself and the app became so hard to use due to its constant loading at the home page, I cant even save my own progress. Even if i switch to wifi from mobile data, the loading doesnt seem to be fixed. Will change review upon fix, or uninstall if it doesnt get fixed.

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker by Driversnote
playStoreApps.description: Automatically track, manage and categorize mileage for an IRS-compliant logbook.

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker by MileageWise
playStoreApps.description: Featured by Forbes for Compliance-focused Tracking - Maximize Your Tax Savings

playStoreApps.name: Everlance: Mileage Tracker
playStoreApps.description: Automatic Mileage + Expense Tracking. Every 1,000 miles = a 725 tax write off

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker App by TripLog
playStoreApps.description: Automatically track your miles and expenses for tax savings and reimbursements!

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker & Vehicle Log
playStoreApps.description: Track miles automatically, classify trips & export tax and reimbursement reports

playStoreApps.name: Stride: Mileage & Tax Tracker
playStoreApps.description: Expense, income, and gas & miles tracker for self-employed workers and drivers

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker & Log - MileIQ
playStoreApps.description: Automatic Mileage Tracking. Never Forget Another Drive!

playStoreApps.name: Mileage Tracker by MyCarTracks
playStoreApps.description: Track mileage automatically for reports, reimbursements, and live vehicle GPS.

playStoreApps.name: Vehicle Trip Logbook Tracker
playStoreApps.description: GPS mileage tracker for personal, business, tax deduction purposes.

playStoreReviews.text: This app is shady! There is NO indication that you start on a limited plan: it does NOT tell you upfront you must pay to access full reports for filing taxes, NO subscribe banner anywhere, and NO suggestion to upgrade your account under Settings > Your Account. Nothing. Complete silence!! I only discovered the subscription requirement when I looked into the iBeacon. About that subscription: other mileage tracking apps cost MUCH less while also letting you track other business expenses. Pathetic!

After over a year of use, which cost bout $130, it used to do exactly what I needed and nothing else. However in recent weeks it's been buggy. It'll track the mileage for 2 hours but then never stop tracking and crash or just fail to load up any time you try to open the app to turn it off. Sometimes it'll open days later sometimes it'll just start tracking again days later and mess up everything.

I don't know what the problem is but I went 400 miles and this app shows I went over 1200... it's hard to want to purchase something that is off that bad... I figured it would at least be as accurate as life360 because they are spot on every time... I have everything set correctly and on automatic but this is hit or miss on if it will work when I start driving automatically.... has good reviews so I figured it would be an awesome tool to use....

way too expensive. $10 per month subscription for this type of app is absurd. There are cheaper alternatives that work. I am using TripLog. For mileage tracking it's free. for $5 per month you get the premium subscription which includes expenses tracking and other features. Frankly I don't like the idea of my data going to "the cloud". I'd rather buy a piece of hardware that keeps that data local.

It keeps track of my miles along with my IRS deductions everyday. And then I can generate a report at the end of the month. it's the easist app I have used so far. I drive for lyft and it simplifies everything I need in a matter of minutes. I start the app when I start driving and stop it when I'm done for the day and there it is. All nice and neat. I would not recommend turning on the auto tracker though. If you do it tracks everytime you get in the car. Even if you're just going to the store.

I have used the app for about 2yrs now. The auto tracker keeps turning itself on even when I turn the toggle off. It drains my phone battery like crazy. I have the ibeacon, but I use manual trip recording now. I like that you can add a missed trip, but it's limited to available route directions the app offers. If you took a different route, it won't be calculated. I wish there was a way to trace/draw on the map to retrace your trip. Other than that, it's a pretty easy app to use.

Best milage tracker app!! I've used this for a couple years now, it makes everything so incredibly easy. I started with the free version then quickly switched to the subscription, it's absolutely worth it! It saves me so much time during tax season. Also any issues are quickly resolved by the amazing customer service team. Highly recommend!!!

I don't know what they did with this app, but it's been wonky ever since the last update. I track manually using both gps and their iBeacon. Beginning the track works fine, but when I reopen the app to stop tracking, it black screens. Then it crashes. In order to get it going again I have to "Force Stop" and "Clear Cache" and hope my trip wasn't lost in the process. Please fix this.

the app/service is convenient. as someone that is self employed and very forgetful about tracking milage this is so handy. be prepared for this to eat your battery though. I expected it - but not this much. the other reviews are not exaggerating like I thought. I paid for the year but I won't be using it again next year.

5/5/25: Tracking mileage for business/personal trips is much easier with the Beacon. I give it a >95% accuracy on the actual route taken in relation to the actual odometer miles logged. I seriously doubt you will find a better tracking system for the cost. 3/21/26: Can't pass up this update! After a year of using the iBeacon, the mileage actually is rating for me at 99%. Definitely worth it for me! Highly recommended!

I went through and reviewed/ tested many simular apps. this one has been best so far, and the added beacon helps alot to keep the tracking to the vehicle needed. only downside was how fast the free testing ended. didn't make it 2 weeks at 2 trips a day. but it still does what I need and accurately, and its subscription cost isn't bad and it works which theres some out there you can't say either of those for

Tracks phenomenally... also drains my battery just as well. Galaxy S25 Ultra, latest update... using ibeacon and still used my GPS on my phone for over 15 hours. was running in the background for 19 hours. System wake ups above 250 times and it has used twice as much battery in the background throughout the day than the two hour teams call I had with my screen and camera on... Battery usage needs to be resolved or we'll be forced to use something else. But tracking trips is great!

Paid 50$ for an Ibeacon that doesn't work, but customer service says they don't do refunds. Customer service was also a headache because they only answer your inquiries through email, and almost every email was a different representative. I told them that I completed all the steps that were listed, I got the beacon linked up, I moved it to a different part of the car even, all the settings were right, yet every email said the exact same thing about checking that my settings were correct.

I've been using this app for a while to track my mileage. I have for about a couple years now and although it's easy to use, I'm disappointed to learn that there is a limit for a number of trips to take each month. This never happened to me in the past and last night I received the message about the limit. I'm not paying for a subscription as both options on a mobile app are ridiculous. Not everything needs to be subscription based. I expected better from this app. Might never use again.

I had used quicken to track my mileage & recently it had stopped working. I put in a request for help & 3 weeks later, still waiting for a response. I tried this app out & had a question about it & received within a day a very comprehensive reply that is not typical of today's customer service. App works great. Suggestion, would be nice to have a checklist of notes that can be repeated without retyping each time. For example, I note which client a trip is for and have to retype each time.

Works really well, but it is NOT free after a certain number of trips. I was bamboozled by the top reviews saying it was a free app. If you can afford the monthly payment, this app will be totally worth it! It is very easy to use :) This app was a huge waste of time for me because now i need to reinput everything into a different tracker that is /actually/ free. I don't see anything about a monthly subscription in description

MileIQ used to be $60 per year, then they increased to $90, which why I went looking to replace it. This app appeared to be free, with no obvious indication it had limits until I got a notification that I had exceeded the allowed drives and had to pay to record more. Even more than MileIQ, so I stopped using it. Underhanded bait and switch. No way it costs that much to run an app with so little need for data. Greedy. Edit: No one looks on an app website. Intentional deception.

Easy to use and accurate. With the recent update the UI for manually entering trips is more difficult. I miss the calendar and clock face. It made entering a week of trips smoother as I know where I was Wednesday but I can't always remember what exact date it was. My biggest complaint is the subscription model. For what they offer, its too expensive. Were they to offer a cheaper option only for the app and not the tracker I'd be on it.

Close to perfection.. I prefer manual entry w/ odometer reading and/or simply entering miles driven. Being forced to choose a route isn't good when my route isn't listed = inaccurate mileage. I don't want physical addresses shown in my reports (it's bloated), just the to/from locations. There needs to be a setting to drop the decimals and use whole numbers. Lastly, I'd really like to see a round-trip feature implemented, otherwise it makes double the work to log trip entries! Could be 5⭐️!

If you're comfortable doing this manually, continue to do so. It's buggy as hell sometimes, and doesn't log trips very well which leads to this being a trip micromanaging app. I have to force it to stop often so that I can do basic operations in the app because of UI errors/bugs. You can't split or modify current/recent trips, and sometimes doesn't offer enough possible routes on manual entries. You have to pay like 12USD/month for the extended use of the reports feature. It's "meh" at best.

The app needs work. "all trips" list needs to start from the current date. Nobody has time to scroll from the first to the 28th of the month to update or add a trip manually. If it's two days old, nobody remembers to update it. If using more than one iBeacon or car, list the trips of both cars on one list in the app. You do this on your website. I don't know why it isnt this way on the app. I've added trips not remembering what car I was in. Waste of time.

It's a good app overall, but won't notify you if trips are not tracked for at least a week or more. Lately, I find trips of 49 miles which is actually many small trips where it did not stop amd record, but kept recording for many, many days on end as one big trip. It's been asked that the app be upgraded to text or email when trips are not recorded for an amount of time a user sets on the app, but obviously this is not looked at as important enough of a feature. Overall a good app.

I really want to like this app, but it has too many issues in its current state. I can't even open it right now as it gets stuck on the loading screen, I've left it for over an hour, and it just won't open. So I have 7 months of miles I can't access. When it was working, it was very slow, I'd press a button and it would take 3-5 seconds to respond. The auto tracking was wholly unreliable, at least twice a week,it wouldn't start tracking until I was already hundreds of miles into my trip. Plz fix

I have been using the free version of this app and I like that I don't have to think about turning it on every time I start because I'm bad at remembering. I wish there was a way to edit individual completed trips. I was looking into the paid subscription, but feel the $12 is more than I want to pay as I don't want or need the iBeacon. I would indeed pay for a cheaper app only version, Please look into this. Thank You.

This is a simple program that is relatively easy to operate. It's not always accurate, but it's better than Google maps as far as accuracy. It's an okay option for individual business use, but it is not useful for family mileage. To get anything useful beyond basic mileage and cost, you need the premium version, which I don't think is worth paying for. It doesn't provide enough value to make the premium version worthwhile.

Managed to use it twice. On the third try, it would not let me log in, stating there's a problem with their servers. Also, there does not seem to be a way to easily switch between personal, commuter, and business miles while driving. It looks like you have to physically stop your trip and then start a new trip which is ridiculously time-consuming. This app did nothing to make tracking my business miles easier. I'll go back to pen and paper.

I have been using this app for about 3 months now. I have it set to automatically track me everyday between certain hours. I like how easy it is to use and how easy it is to review the trips and delete the ones that are not work related. I just noticed today it is not actually tracking me all the time though and I have lost work miles. So disappointing. It literally has one job.

It's a decent app for tracking mileage on more than one vehicle, it tends to turn itself off if you have have stayed in one place too long. It needs to be updated so that manual trips are started and finished MANUALLY with out auto stopping. more than 15 trips requires a monthly subscription, might consider a 1 time payment of $20-30 for the convenience of automation, but not gonna pay $132 a year for somthing I can do manually with excel on my ipad for free.

The app used to decent. Starting paying for a subscription and it just hasn't been the same. I do feel $12 is high. Since getting the subscription the app always tells me there are two issues. Which are related to battery optimization and physical activity. Not really issues, I'm just not allowing it to run in the background. For the last two weeks, the map hasn't been able to track the roads. It looks like I have a flying truck. More than likely going to cancel at the end of the year.

Love it in concept, but even after purchasing their "iBeacon" to make sure that no trips would somehow be missed, there are still huge periods of time with no trips logged even though I have not touched any settings and I drive multiple trips almost 365 days/year. If you decide to use this app, you must check daily to make sure it's working properly. :-(

This app finally started to work when everything was set to allow which is silly. I do not think it was made to work well with Android phones or Samsung. The stop trip button was located at the top of the screen behind other setting displays and camera lense which made it impossible to stop the tracking. Trying another app. Good luck. Maybe it will work with your phone

Driver's note is a pretty nice option for mileage tracking. If you let them know there are features that would be beneficial, or that there is a problem they have done a good job of updating the app. it can be slow to show some trips though. I really like the manual trip feature so if I am switching vehicles in a short window of time I can delete a trip and separate the milage for the two vehicles, usually I forget to stop the trip in one vehicle and start a new trip. I luv the monthly reports.

UPDATE: FAILED AGAIN. paid for yearly, have the tracking beacon. all settings as they are supposed to be in phone/app. i still find this app fails constantly, and I need to try to recreate numerous hours of driving. app often hangs in white screen when I open to check, need to reinstall, and I find it missed a huge swath of time. frustrating and unreliable, and it's cost hours to add miles I drove, many I'll never get manually. I want to sleep, but now need to do this yet again before I forget.

I love the app when it works, but I drive for a delivery service so I leave the tracking on for 8-10 hours at a time and when I try to close the trip it usually is frozen on a white screen. It doesn't lose my miles, it logs them when the app choses to close the trip itself after no activity for so long, but I cannot close the trip myself, which is problematic when I'm still driving but no longer for work purposes. I was also disappointed that the free version only gives 15 trips/months.

Absolutely the worst mileage tracking app ever. App uses well over 10% of my battery everyday you have to have physical activity turned on so they're tracking you when you walk through your own home not just when you're driving your vehicle for work purposes. the price for the app is considerably higher than other apps that do the same thing. customer service is practically non-existent and the app specifically says you can expect a reply in 24 hours that sure helpful, not.

There is a limit of FIFTEEN, yes you read that right 15 drives or trips in the non premium, non subscribed users. You will need to pay 12 dollars PER month to get that unlimited drive. God , if I only knew I would stick with another app that does not limited the non subscribed and free tracking. 12dollars/monthly? That's almost 150 per year. Lol. This app is a joke. Stay away.

Excellent. Nicely remembers common destinations, which is particularly helpful if you make the same runs over and over again. One can easily go back and manually add these by selecting a date, clicking the start location and stop location. done. I downloaded several different mileage trackers and wanted one that was simple allow me to accurately track business trips. There are a few very minor features that I would like to see added, but this was the best app I tried without subscription!

This COULD be a great app, one I would gladly pay for, but it's infuriating. I want to track trips manually (and after the trouble I've had with trying to get to be ACCURATE with a manual trip, there's no way I'd trust it to track automatically). I've found workarounds, and honestly it's better than almost all the other mileage trackers, but still infuriating. Support...is polite and tries very much to be helpful...tries.

After repeated communications with the tech department they could not resolve the issue of missing trips. I would go days without any trips logged. The conclusion for my issue was that I parked my car to close to my home which meant the iBeacon did not disconnect and reconnect from the app. Even parking on the street rather then our driveway did not solve the problem.

Too many times the app is not tracking. I keep it updated and rely it to function properly. I tried to send a message in the app and it won't even send. so I am doing it through a review. I went to look at my reports and see my mileage for a quarter and notice weeks missing of tracking. This is supposed to be reliable and I pay plenty of money for this to work daily and not wonder if it is tracking. For over $100 this app needs to be more reliable.

The fact that it shows up as a free mileage on the Play Store and it has no mention of a hidden subscription fee is really deceitful and pitiful. I started using this app because stride made a cruddy update that would no longer track my mileages accurately. I'm trying to save up my money for my taxes, not waste more through another subscription! I paid enough subscription services as it is, I don't need to pay anymore because $12 is an expensive service for just one vehicle!

if you use the free app option it limits how many trips you put in. which is fine, but when you accidentally go over the allowed amount it blanks then out and won't give you a total. and you can't delete the blanked out ones inorder to get the total for the rest. so the app becomes useless cause you can't get any of the numbers

it worked really well for about 3 years. then suddenly I was hit my 15 trips for the month, but I hadn't. Then waited, thinking at the end of the month, a week away, I would be able to start again. It never reset that 15 free trips a month. flash forward a month, after uninstalling and reinstalling just in case, it still doesn't work. So I'm missing a whole month of data. I only take two trips each week, so I don't know why it errored out like this for me.

Very accurate. App is so easy to use. Gives you an excel download. I'm pretty impressed and very happy with this app, sometimes I forget to log trips and this app is a lifesaver!! And if you don't have to travel much within the month (like me..i have travel less than 10 times for business) it's free. You can track business only purposes and set times to track. I love this app!! I would highly recommend!

I deliver for DoorDash and UberEats, and this app has helped me track my miles so much more easily as well as keep track of my driving patterns for the day. There aren't any ads that pop up, and I can easily start tracking since it's got a very straightforward interface. Occasionally, it may stop tracking but I think that may be an issue with connectivity. Otherwise I love this app, it has helped me track my miles & save so much on taxes

Using auto tracking with the Beaco installed iny car. All stops auto tracking at every app update, phone restart, etc. Days go by before I remember to check it and find out the system has not been tracking. Restarting auto-track manually does the trick, bit what's the point then? It should be set-and-forget it. Too unreliable for regular business use, too bad because, when it works and auto tracks, it is an excellent app with lots of useful features for business expensing.

Update: Lately this app has been crashing so much. I check for app updates every day but it isn't fixed yet. It is very easy to use and has served me well in tracking mileage for rideshare driving. I have a cheap Android phone so the app runs pretty slow. I really think that is mostly due to my phone's lack of processing power. I used to have an iPhone 11 Pro Max and it ran a lot quicker. I highly recommend paying for the very affordable monthly subscription so you can track unlimited trips.

Looks like a pretty good app, but apparently oriented for higher-end users - $12/mo is a lot for this small business owner. It seems to have a lot of features I don't need, so I'll go looking for something a little economical. P.S - You should tell folks up front that there's a trip limit for free users, rather than waiting until they install it and get it set up.

This app is still trash regardless of the updates they've done, still slow, still have to wait 30 plus seconds for screens to load, GPS is terribly inaccurate and choppy causing the driver to have to eat miles, because it marks them as miscellaneous, it does not follow streets accurately, I can't believe I paid for this garbage. Even with the point a to point b having to push the stop button at every in and out of the vehicle this app is still trash and does not record distances accurately!!!!!

The app excels at tracking routes and offers a great selection when entering trips manually. Kudos to the devs for their fantastic work! However, I rate it three stars due to the five trips per month limit. The $12 subscription fee is too high; $4 a month would be more reasonable and make me willing to subscribe.

stayed using this app with the auto tracking via the phone. that was a waste it would continue tracking for up to 45 minuets after I had stopped driving and this regularly caused it to pick up another drive 30 minutes later as the same drive. then I dropped the $50 out what ever for the upgraded version and the free Ibeacon. now the ibeacon picks up even less. and the bacon is placed on my dash 6 inches behind the phone. I've deleted and added the beacon at least 4 or 5 times. complete waste.

Decent enough app but it drives me mad with the constant asks to track automatically and allow permissions etc... I don't want full tracking or anything automatic (to save battery and also because I use various vehicles and I don't want it getting confused etc) So I just want to log everything manually, it would be great if it just had a manual mode but instead it really wants me to use the GPS features. Nice enough but I'll look for something else.

App is working okay but it drives me crazy that it needs to show a notification at the top that you can't remove, just saying it's 'ready to start tracking'. I don't need something in my otherwise empty notification area saying that an app is ready to do what it does. I don't want to turn notifications off completely because I want the pop up when it has recorded a drive, but I'm not seeing a way to limit the 'ready to start tracking' notification.

this app was suggested to me from a trainer. Have used this for a while now has been great I started with the free version to see how it went the swiched to the paid and easy to use and helpful at tax time. my only suggestion would be to have a reminder sent for the end of the financial year odometer reading and one at the start.

High background battery usage. I bought this to the attention of their customer service dept just after i purchased the subscription, stating that they were doing an update which should fix this issue, Yes it made it slightly better but my Samsung 24Fe was still chewing through its battery when auto tracking was turned on. After a number of conversation & request for a refund of their expensive subscription no solution was given & no refund. Gofar 1000% better, does more and is much cheaper.

It's good, but unfortunately absolutely destroys battery, even with an iBeacon. It pings your location every few minutes regardless of whether you're tracking or not. I'd hope that it would be smart enough to only track your location if connected to the iBeacon. Clearly not!

I like it thus far. I reserve the right to adjust my review as warranted. But so far so good 👍. Mind you I don't use it to save time. I simply enjoy tracking the miles I Drive and the routes I take. Just spits and giggles. I'm a former truck driver and I would still recommend this app because it's thorough as far as I can tell. stay tuned...

Easy to use, love it has automatic start tracking. My favorite part is how easy it is to separate personal from business. I am having trouble with it recording trips. locations are close when it tracks start and stop points

I've had a need for a tracking app and so far I'm very happy with Driversnote. The app is working great and I'm logging my trips with no issues. I subscribed and got the iBeacon. They were very helpful even when the first iBeacon got lost in mail, they sent me a second one. Very happy customer, I would definitely recommend them. Thank you!

To PaddyPoddy, u talk smack about the app requiring users 2 receive access 2 ANY type of reports regarding milage/expenses. The value of that info isn't $0 so why should they just give it away? To show the app's abilities? That's what the demo is for. Find out how this app calculates the info & U can exploit that & take income from this company!

I've been using driversnote with the iBeacon for a couple years now. This has been the best way to track my mileage for work.. it's pretty accurate, easy to separate work and personal. Come tax season I can do a full year print out with breakdowns of personal/business. My biggest negative is the battery drain. I'm seeing an avg 20% or higher battery usage just for driversnote in my battery usage breakdown. I'm assuming this is cause it's always looking for the iBeacon. Good product otherwise.

was working great, even paid for it as I do a lot of driving for work, but the last 2 days the app wont even open... I looked for updates, I even uninstalled it and re downloaded it, and still isnt working. *edit got it all fixed and sorted via emails with the team at driversnote and its all working great again!

Certainly easier than using a logbook. With the ability to keep notes and refer back to the recorded trips at day's end it's a useful tool. I've used Driversnote for several years & it would be hard to do without it now.

I've been using the driversnote app for many years now and am very happy about it. I can track my mileage and no need to keep another logbook. I can print the report every month and attach it to my claim form. Highly recommended for business.

invested in the ibeacon and all but it have it on track when ibeacon is around I get tracked at home and drains my battery. my schedule changes and it's very inconvenient to have to chnage it. sometimes it just doesn't track so even though im paying for a sub for tax purposes it probably going to be all wrong.

The app started doing something stupid all of a sudden. When I drive, it's working in the background with the iBeacon. When I check the app to see if it's tracking, instead of tracking my drives around city streets, it draws a straight line through the map from point A to point B. It's not doing what a tracking app should. I am now using another app. Don't waste your money on this garbage. Customer support is terrible.

Unfortunately, after 16 h of tracking, I was unable to finish the trip. The application was showing as still working, but not accessible. I need to be able to start and end the trip manually. So automatically, options are not for me.

This app is very good and the auto track feature is very useful. There is more than one style in which to track your trips and which way to track becomes evident through use...very straightforward design architecture. Report function is also very useful for taxation. I did note that sometimes the info on screen did not match what I thought the trip was in actuality but when I stopped the trip the information on screen "healed".

This app makes every drive for business purposes so easy to keep track of. If you're forgetful like me just put it on auto detect & it records your drives making it stress free. I'd be lost without it.

Clever app but needs a few more features. Instead of ibeacon initiating tracking it should just trigger when the phone is detected as connected to the car bluetooth. A shortcut exists to start tracking but none exists to close tracking. This is needed for use of Modes/Routines automation in Android. This app causes the phone to get very hot, the battery is being drained. Really need a way to automate shutdown instead of the app tracking phone travel in the background nonstop.
This app is super simple to use. Has everything needed to track KM's, personal and business. Can set a schedule to track automatically for work days. Highly recommend 👌
Pure garbage, it takes about 30-40 attempts to login. Than it crashes constantly. Sometimes it takes to 15-20mins to load. Hidden fee, after a handful of logged trip, it requires payment.
Impressed with the ease of installation and the degree and accuracy of the route plotting. i am not using an ibeacon yet but the app has worked flawlessly for me on Samsung android up to this time.

tracking works fine but now I need to understand why your app drains too much battery. it save buy the ibeacon and save battery. (it doesn't work ). I have the beacon and still requires location 24/7 even though I have said tracking through ibeacon. if I'm doing something wrong let me know because I have tried three different phones after the ibeacon and all the phones died without me using the phone. if I'm doing something wrong let me know cos it's getting annoying
Great app and the tracker is very accurate well worth the outlay. if you want to save a fortune on your tax bill this is the answer. clear tracking and accounting records.
very good app automatically starts end and logs trips no ads. easy to compile reports and email them to accountant.
This is amazing. Easy to use. create multiple tags for different businesses I work for, and personal driving! Fantastic app thank you!

Driversnote app is used for milage tracking for a business and for the IRS. The electronic trackers in our cars as well as the apps both work well on both IOS and Android. The staff at Driversnote has been great to work with when any questions arose. I recommend this company and their products.
free version only tracks a limited amount, but paid version is only about $19/month including GST, so not bad for how much it improves the quality of life if you do a lot of driving in the day

coming from. iOS to Android this app has a lot left to be desired. upon install it's automatically set to a schedule. which has now caused an inconsistency in trip recording. You are actually unable to set a schedule if you want to. Say from 6am to 8pm it all saves as AM and when you select 6am it saves as 7am. some trips aren't tracked from time to time. I could leave my car for 30 minutes and visit a client and this thing thinks you're driving around still.

Easy to set up and use. I have auto tracking turned off and just start a trip when it's business related. It's working great and I love the prompts to run a report each month, it reminds me to do my claim. The reports are easy to run and understand, I save the files as my record for tax. Highly recommend 👌

I've always recorded my milage in a little book. Giving this a go at the moment. So far it hasn't let me down. update. I've just subscribed. I didn't get the beacon as was thinking maybe it's free.its a bit pricey so maybe in the future. Will keep just the subscription for now.
I love this app and would absolutely rate 5 stars but the most recent update keeps crashing on my Android phone 😅 I can't access my logs for the year for taxes... help!

started using it, and then I get a ransome warning. no more than 15 car movements and you need to pay 11 euros per month. it is not mentioned anywhere in the description. ended up paying it. the app is actually easier to use than the other ones. upate: this app is very good. 1 downside is battery drain.

so far so good, separation between bussiness an personal able to have multiple people on the app at once as they drive different vehicles within the business we can track each of their kms. highly recommend if your needing to track vehicle kms for bussiness purposes.
Been using it for years, but also my beacon finally expired. Probably dead battery. I talked to customer service and they said they would send me a replacement beacon. Great service! Thanks again.
I definitely would recommend this app attracts your mileage it constantly is tracking your mileages when your emotion it's free you can save certain trips if you need to I mean the app is awesome
The app and tracker beacon works really well and is ideal for my millage for tax. customer service were great and had my problem sorted in short order 👍👍
Completely crashed at the end of a long drive and won't load up at all now. Pretty useless if I have to reinstall it and lose everything
Support is worse than AI. They straight up don't listen and give non-related answers. Expensive for paid version as well.
I'm enjoying the ease of use , this is very practical and great for my reporting , I definitely will be looking at the premium options in the near future 😉
This is by far the worst place that I've ever have to deal with. They're not reliable and very inconsistent. I would give them a 0 if that was available.
I've been using this a few days now. Simple to use and exceptionally accurate. Will be getting other members of staff to download to make it easier submitting monthly mileage ..
this seems a great app but it runs continually. As such it is an enormous drain on my battery, accounting for nearly 20% of battery use. Hence the low rating
I like it but it doesn't always connect automatically even withe beacon and I have to manually enter the trip
After a trip it stays blank.White only screen.Does not work.I have a Samsung A54.The app freeze.Even if I turn off my phone and on and open the app it stay in a white freeze screen.
I'm about to purchase the subscription for work related travels, installed lite version for trial and noted extreme battery drain while idle on S24 Ultra, what could be a problem?
Easy to use and accurate. So accurate that it helps me track my hours. No regrets in downloading this app.

When I tried this out initially (free version) it seemed to work well, and I had high hopes. Unfortunately it also tracks you on a train (there must surely be a way to detect train movement using GPS, unless it thinks I'm driving my car on the tracks!). And after I deleted several train journeys, it stopped detecting my car journeys. And when I manually entered details, it showed a different route that didn't start where I told it to start. I may as well estimate journeys using Google maps!
Outstanding app. Never had any issues and it's convenience is unparalleled. Keep it up!

As a DoorDasher, I rely on mileage trackers for tax reasons. I've used MileageWise for a month now, and I'm impressed. First off, it has a lifetime payment plan, which in an age of pouring money into monthly subscriptions, is unheard of! And it's the most popular option, so I'm not the only person on Earth who believes in only paying for things once! I do wish it was quicker on the draw for automatic logs, because I get a lot of anxious customers who don't give me the time I need to log trips!

Was able to upload one month worth of information unless I subscribed to a plan. I wanted to get a feel for the rest of the features before I paid for it while on the 14 day trial. Information that transferred had addresses that I didn't visit and didn't show on my timeline so to start this wasn't going to be a quick upload and done. The app itself, would not let me past choose a plan so since I couldn't even get a feel for the app, I uninstalled.

Very disappointed now. More accurate than other apps. Now I have no logs from June 2nd -30th (the notification bar, showed the app tracking). Support was responsive in trying to find settings that would have caused the issue, and helpful in trying to import Google timeline data, but for some reason that was unavailable. Now they say the development team will further investigate and "the issue will be addressed in an upcoming release" -they suggest using their AI wizard to recreate what's lost.

Actually it does what it does very well compared to other apps. But it needs to be more clear about which app does what MileageWise vs MileageLog. I thought it would do tracking for free (or there was a free tier). Not just a free trial. That said, it is one of the cleanest tracking apps out there. It "just works" and i'm glad to have found it.

I’ve been using MileageWise Mileage Tracker for some time now, and it has really simplified the way I record my trips. The app is precise, easy to navigate, and keeps everything neatly arranged for tax documentation and reimbursements. I especially like how it automatically records drives and creates detailed reports. It saves me a lot of time and gives me confidence that everything is tracked properly. Great app for eCommerce mileage tracking. You can choose how to track your trips, automatically or manually, or enter them yourself. Nice looking app, easy to navigate, tons of features. After some research, this seemed like the best option for a mileage tracker, andas it turned out, it is a really well designed app, and was easy and intuitive to set up. So, it makes you pay. Naturally, I had some questions. They got back to me with answers within 5-10 minutes. I was pleased with their response, so decided to go ahead and pay the monthly. Don't get the app, save your money and go with a different platform.

I originally gave this a five but now a two. this is my third year using it, i have nothing good to say for this year. will not track all of my drives in some cases I'm missing hundreds of miles.
