The app on phone works well...I wish it would auto detect start and ends of bike rides after the 1st start (like strava) bcs I sometimes forget to turn it off, or back on, as I shop on eŕands. What sucks, is the website later for editing, as there is no option to delete all points BETWEEN 2 points, as I always need to delete every up n down of grocery aisles manually, since as mentioned I forget to turn it off and it does not autodetect the difference between riding and walking speeds.

After a total of one ride, I'm happy with what I see so far. Update: I have been tracking my walking and riding for over a month now. This app is reliable and shows me everything I need to know. Update: I have been using this app for years now. The only thing that confounds me is how my Fitbit watch and the app disagree about distance and calories. The Fitnit has no GPS, so presumably they both use my phone's GPS. Anyway, no biggie. Map My Ride is still great in my book.

Keeps track of your workouts, mileage, and your GPS route. I just lowered the rating because, just as I was starting a ride... It took way too long to start. While others are waiting for me to get the app to start and not get in the way of the ride, it decides that it needs to verify my email address. Something it could have done at any time over the past few years. Of course, my phone had spotty coverage, so I realized that wouldn't happen any time soon, and skipped the app.

MapMyRide is one of the best bike computers you can use. If they have problems they solve them pretty fast. There's a coach that can give you various stats on your workouts out loud while you're riding. I really love that feature. It's totally adjustable as is everything else. You can add a photo to your workout which I like. I've been using them for 5 years and I can look back on any past ride I want. I use it to track and it's the best. I ride a Norco Rideau hybrid bike.

Update 3/25/21:App does retain a lot of local data as, over time, it I increasingly uses more storage. Why not cloud storage older data? Also, my distances are quite different than other devices. Something in this app just takes over my phone whenever I try to start it or stop it to save a workout. Can't even get to home as no buttons respond. Just have to wait until it decides it's finished with whatever it's doing. Otherwise, the app has great functionality and detail for my cycling needs.

The stats for my ride on 10/14/21 did not register correctly. I briefly glanced at the screen when I finished my ride and noted the distance. However, after I saved the ride, distance showed as "0" miles. I edited the stats with the number I initially saw. Now the first four splits are showing the same speed, and it's much higher than it actually was. I see that they did an update on 10/12. I believe that may be the reason the stats are off. I hope the bugs are worked out soon.

The maps, distances, and average speed seem to be relatively on point. No complaints there. But you can't edit, or cut off points in the workout. That would be nice for someone like me who forgets to stop the timer as I'm dying for water at the end of my workouts. You can't go back ten minutes later and fix the 'cooldown' time. So you have to always be thinking of hitting the clock as you cross the finish... I get that this is probably there to stop people from gaming the numbers, but still.

The app no longer tracks distance. I don't know if it's because of the most recent update, but the timing seems to coincide. I've checked all the settings like GPS, automatic update of Time and Time Zone Etc. It has become completely and utterly useless to me. How can I go back to the prior version? I went through the T-shooting ideas as recommended, but all the settings are already where they need to be. Your link to enter a trouble ticket only allows me to enter a question which gives me the same automated replay. ARGHHH!

5/10/19: Still not fixed. 4/15/19: What is going on with this app??? I have been using Map My Ride for four years. Within the last two months, the ability of the app to accurately track any of my workouts is WAY off - a half mile or more short each time. The routes it's tracking are a series of jagged lines that aren't even close to the actual route. I install all updates.

I started using Map My Ride years ago because that's what my friends were using. In those days the app didn't pause when you stopped riding, which was a pain. So I tried a couple of other apps, but I never really liked any of them. They finally added the auto-pause feature in the free version, and I've been using it ever since. i like the calorie counter, which the other apps I tried don't have. The free version is good enough. Have to admit that the ads and constant nags to upgrade are annoying!

I was "forced" to use this one as a replacement for Endomondo, but so far I've been pretty disappointed. First, the GPS accuracy of the app is terrible. It records around 30 to 40 meters less every KM. That is huge, especially if you do long rides. And no. It is not the phone. I´ve tried other apps and the accuracy is good. Secondly, and this is not related to the app itself but the whole platform; it lacks a lot compared to Endomondo. Especially on the analytics and statistics when analyzing rides, the capabilities that Endomondo had were far superior. If you could bring back the stats functionality from Endomondo it would be great. So far, not impressed and actively looking for a replacement.

Nice app. I use it often. So far mostly for the map it generates. Seems like it would be fun to use some other options like adding photos and notes. Stats are cool too like speed and elevation also duration and calories. One problem is the "start workout/pause" button is too low on the screen and I have inadvertently paused the workout when I hit the home screen or back button on my phone. Again nice app!

If you want exact split times and distances for your rides then don't rely on this app. It's a good general app for gauging calories and distances. When I compare it to my bike computer it's pretty inaccurate. However I have only been using the free version and have not yet explored all of the features. Still, pretty good app for free.

Good free workout app. I haven't tried (and don't plan to try) the extra-cost features, since the free version gets the job done. I notice that the app shows 1 to 2% shorter distances than my bike computer, probably just not logging quite as frequently. The only really bad shortfall came when my phone ran low on battery halfway through the ride. In battery-saving mode it seems to have woken up every 5 or 10 minutes, and the saved route showed a LOT fewer turns than reality. Otherwise works fine.

I have the paid MVP version and the mileage tracking keeps stopping, so something is going on with this app. I saw 2 other reviews that had the problem I had in my last two rides in the last 4 days. The app tracks my distance and speed up to a point, then the distance tracker stops--first at 8.19, then at 8,82. The timer continues, but the speed and distance tracker stop. When I touch the screen, the mileage drops back to zero, so I have no record of my ride distance. This needs to be fixed or I will have to replace the app

Just used once but they insist I rate. Ok. But you lose stars for impatience. ... Years later: I used this app for several years, but, sadly, finally stopped, after logging 10s of thousands of miles, because the app is a DATA hog. It cloggs up many gigs of storage. That wouldn't be a problem because I have a massive SD card, but when I assigned data storage there the app stops working correctly. So my choice was either allow this app to dominate my phone's storage, or give up on it.

This version does not consistently provide tracking results. Version from a couple of years ago worked and provided accurate tracking results. I have my phone setup to allow MapMyRide to have everything on. I have turned off the auto pause feature due to the fact it is unreliable. Still if I make a stop it does not continue tracking, sometimes it works and mostly do not work. Time to uninstall after many years of use. This app went downhill quickly after Under Armour got involved.

It doesn't work as expected. There's a lot of straight lines extending from points when I checked my progress. There's a giant loop that's missing from my path. My phone is over half dead after going out for 2 hours and that's with the screen off and battery saver on part of the way. It seems that it randomly decides to track you when your screen is off then decided to go idle and stop tracking the other times.

Have used this app since my Junior year of high school, and as a senior in college, I can say it's a great go to app for runs and bike rides. Occasional glitches occur when using spotify or other applications in tandem with Map my Ride, and the ads can be annoying at times (hence the 4 star rating) , but other than that, no complaints!

App used to work great. Now when you first start it seems to read the correct current speed but once it pauses the current speed displayed is far from correct. The total time distance and average speed seem ok. The split speed for the last mile seems ok and isn't even close to the displayed current speed. I had originally rated this 5 star but after sending an email to support and getting no response I am downgrading it to 2. Disappointed and frustrated. please fix it Update on 7/16 didn't fix

Delighted New User. On 3rd day of trying MapMyRide. Don't be put off by the name. It has settings all types of exercise. Mine (so far) has been used on the Dog Walk setting. I didn't realize we were covering miles. Wish MapMyRide had been around when I was young and super active. So much cooler than a step counter (which it does as well) with a map that shows the path you took once finished. I'll definitely sign up when the trial is over.

I've been using this app for ~6 years. Love it! I've use it on 3 different Samsung phones with no issues. Paid the $30 yearly fee for the "MVP" version. Not much to pay for the hundreds of hours of use every year. Mounted the phone on my bike handlebar so I can monitor my ride - time, distance, speed, ave speed, calories. It records much more, including route, altitude, weather. This is a great cycling app. Highly recommended!

Was my go-to app for tracking workouts. A few updates ago, they set it up so that after you were done with the workout, it can take almost an hour before you can actually save the workout. If you forget to actually come back to the app an hour later and hit save, then you cannot start a new workout until you wait the hour before you can hit save, or you have to discard your workout and lose everything. Very frustrating that you can't easily save a workout without keeping the app open for hours!

Excellent app to track my walks, biking and hiking. I even use it cruising the lake on the boat. It has all the good "useful" stats of your journey while in play, and even more stats to summarize the workout when done! Hands down - worth downloading and is my go to app for activities! Tracks distance, speed, pace, avg pace, elevation, calories, time and much more. Excellent mapping /GPS during your route.

I use this daily to track my commuting miles and speed. It is near perfect for this. I would give it 5 stars if it was feasible (or much easier) to mod bookmarked rides to friendlier names. Better compatibility with smart watches and other sensors would also be nice. 10 months later, still using regularly; still wish I could get it to read my Fitbit.

I use the free app and it works great for me. I don't care about sharing my rides on social media. App talks to me during ride; tells me total distance and average mph so far. I save my ride and app will sum total distance for the week, the month, and total ever. A lot of stats for free! I have only noticed an ad one time in six weeks of using app.

Great app to keep track of your mileage. I've been using this app for mountain biking and road cycling. You can change the activity and the app will adjust. What I also enjoy about this app, it keeps track your your route; top speed, average speed, elevation change and calories burned. App works great. I have not had any problems with it so far.

I have not tried to use yet . But first off map my ride .is misleading . I wanted to quickly and easily see the routes I have been driving . This app is a work out app with GPS as a small part .the name of this app sounds like it would be for a salesman or to canvas flyers maybe elections. It's creepy you can find friends .or "accidentally"run into some person you've been stalking . No thanks I will be deleting

When it logs your workout, it is extremely accurate. Would be five stars, except about 10% of the time it seems to get stuck on a premium nagscreen before the workout starts (I use a 30s delay), leading to the workout never getting logged. For the price it's hard to complain, but I'd love to see that detail fixed.

Since the app was revised to "more accurately" reflect mileage, it consistently records significantly less than our group's odometers. While they are not as accurate as measuring with a rolling tape measure, this method was not done throughout the US. I will try other apps and if they are consistent with this one, I may consider returning.

Love using this app. It keeps track of my rides all season, I can compare my progress from season start to season end all on a free app. The ads are minimal, they do seem to be geared toward ladies products which I'm not sure if they are judging by the strength of my workouts? The data on the split times is also very useful. Highly recomend.

The app is great. The features allow you to pool multiple exercises in one place to track your progress. However, sometimes the GPS is a little bit off. I have ridden side by side with another person using the app and our results have been different by as much as a half a mile on a 6 mile or higher bike ride. The same for running with the app. Or I can do the same ride several days in a row and get different distances on each ride.

I don't have an Apple watch, and this app is amazing for tracking distances and paths. I highly recommend it. The best part is If you're walking around and unfamiliar location, you can retrace your steps to find where you came from. It's easy to lose your bearings walking in unfamiliar areas, I've used the map to find my way back numerous times

It works well for a free app. It seems like it used to autostart, which wasn't great because it wouldn't always autostart. At a stop sign for instance. Now I just forget to stop it when I take a break which messes up the data. I love the data and readouts and it makes a nice little route map. Elevation gain is very helpful.

I've been using Map My Ride for 4 years now. I find it reliable, intuitive, and very easy to use. The right amount of data for me. I am an avid but no longer competitive rider of 60+ years. I keep my saved rides to track my status and to share routes for fun or training specifics. No issues with connectivity other than issues with cell service.

Enjoying the app very much. Thank you. Edit: I just lost two workouts in 1 week. Arriving home and trying to save the workout it freezes. When I try to resume since it is the only option available the data is corrupted. I'm sad because this was a great app but obviously if it is unreliable in saving workouts it is useless.

Good app but I'd like to see a couple of additional features . 1) automatically detect a ride like Fitbit app does. I constantly forget to start the app and would love this feature! A short term option would be to add "Start new ride" option from long pressing on the home screen icon. 2) I would also love to have an option for the app to pause audio another app (podcast app, music app) when announcing split times. If these features are added I will update to 5 stats for sure!

Accurate; nice to have a summary of the workout. GPS works well. It continues to be an asset in both planning and having a good ride/workout. Anyone with a cellphone should use this app. I also use the app when using my stationary bike; it (obviously) doesn't track distance, but it does track the time and log the workout - helps me track my activity.

Can't cancel online, cancel page was not loading. I sent an email requesting to cancel auto renewal and my service was canceled completely ( I would have been ok with one more year because it was already charged) the app itself is not bad, it does what it's supposed to do. I'd just rather buy a device like I had before that I don't have to pay for every year.

Everything about this app is user-friendly. My only gripe, and it is miniscule. if you stop for a moment, you have to pull the phone and check it before getting back underway as it will stay paused. Very annoying when you find yourself 2/3 through your activity and it's not being recorded by the app. However, being a free app, who could possibly complain? Just keep aware.

I love being able to keep track of my ride distance and time. I've tried all the other GPS riding apps, and MapMyRide works very well with none of the problems the others have. Edit : GPS no longer works properly. Have set app to non-optimsed battery, but even on a new phone, it will not record a full ride. Switched to Urban Biker which tracks me flawlessly (set to non-optimsed).

I love the stats of the workout and the map it provides however, I don't like having the upgrade form come up when you first open the app. It's very difficult to get out of the form. You waste a lot of time trying to find a way out of it. If you want to upgrade you should be able to say yes to upgrading and if you Don't want to you should be able to continue without jumping through hoops to do it.

One of my most useful apps! The calories burned option doesn't always match my heart rate monitor (app usually says I burned more), but other than that very minor gripe it works like a charm. GPS tracks my ride routes, breaks it up in to split speeds by the mile, gives an overall average speed for the trip, and tracks elevation - not too many free apps out there with that many bells and whistles! Happy riding!

Used to work great, but after the recent update a couple weeks ago the app is no longer working. Keeps saying "an error has been corrected" when trying to record trips. The GPS, distance, speed, and routes are all inaccurate. Wish they wouldn't have tried to fix something that wasn't broken. It's also annoying that they're always trying to push the paid subscription on users, even though it offers only slightly more usable features vs the free app did before it became full of errors.

Solid app, but most features have been slowly moved into the subscription so that as a free user, you are essentially just giving the company your data. Other basic fitness apps have feature parity with the free version now, so unless you're ready to pay I would explore alternatives. To add insult to injury, in 2025 they have sued Garmin with some patents that never should have been granted. The second any issue with Garmin linking occurs I will delete this and never use it again.

Pixel 7. Latest update. Every single time I watch a video, the audio from that video will continue to play on repeat until I force quit the app. Even after I go to another video, even after I go to another app, even after I turn off the screen! The audio from that video won't stop looping! It's crazy-making!

The tracking consistently adds about 2-3% extra distance which doesn't sound like a lot but for a 20 mile run is about half a mile. The app also puts all this AI garbage in your face. Nobody wants a coach that only says how great you are at everything. The app is also pretty buggy and wouldn't let me finish the recording for my first marathon ever so I don't get to look at all the inaccurate stats it recorded.

This app motivates me to push through my fitness goals, post about them and, share bike routes with others. It's been very fun, and I can't wait to discover more bike routes around the country and globe. I must add, the consistency of the GPS and share functions help my loved ones keep peace of mind while I'm hit the paths with a group or solo.

I've been using Strava infrequently, sharing my activities with a few friends. But when I recently tried to use it to start recording activity, it interrupted me by displaying a message reading "Upgraded feature" and "Recording just got simpler." In my hurry and the bright outdoor sun, I couldn't find how to make the message go away so the app would start recording. So, Strava really failed here. After similar problems later, I deleted my account and uninstalled the app.

love the app. I'm probably doing something wrong but my heart rate doesn't read from my fit bit. And I wish when hitting pause on a work out, it didn't keep time during the pause time and add it to the workout time. that is why I picked a 4 star. otherwise, the app is great! I like all the features. too many to list. I run, walk, lift, and do other workouts. I like the how it keeps MPH and distance.

Strava's decent. When I first began using Strava many years ago, I had access to great features under the free version. Little by little my favorite features transferred from the free version to the paid only version which I don't subscribe to because every app does the same thing and my income has changed. There are a lot of glitches with my Strava tracked distance. It very often shows a track I didn't take and says I walked or jogged several miles more than I did.

Strava will not stop spamming me with requests to upgrade to the paid subscription. However, I would never even consider upgrading when there are so many bugs and errors on the app. Please stop asking for money until you have an app that works. The only reason I am still here is because I love the third party website Strava feeds its data to. Once I figure out how to go around, I'm out. Currently, Strava is telling me my last walk was my longest ever. It's not my longest walk in distance or time

This app consistently stops recording halfway through my run and I lose my entire other half. so all of my numbers and records and distances and progress are wrong, and it's incredibly frustrating. I've reported this problem several times and haven't gotten a single reply. It has not been reliable for me. I'm really disappointed :(

my free version of this app has worked well for a year . Now i can't use it, it keeps saying it has a problem when i try. it also loses data connectivity when before it was just fine. I can't turn off the screen anymore or it quits recording. Completely unuseable now. any suggestions? I've tried updating and reinstalling, no improvement.

I hate everything about this app. I just want a place to record what I do. No social media. No trends. No workouts. No competitions. just a place to record my activities, neutrally. But this app just forces everything down your throat. it's like going to the gym on my tablet. a truly *awful* experience. But if you like those things, it works well.

Useless. "Strava recovered from a problem" and loses half of my route. EVERY. SINGLE. TIME. And yes, it had al permissions, battery saving disabled, yada yada. Unreliable, avoid like the plague. Even more garbage than Sports Tracker. 90%+ of my recent dozens of workouts randomly stop wrong in the middle, and I losing tracking. And I have all the battery saving settings disabled, all permissions given, yada yada yada.

The free version of the app is great. It connects to many brands of devices and gives me a way to interact with others who live farther away. Lately, the app has been offering a free 30 day trial. Like constantly, with a countdown timer. I'd be interested, except I can't actually sign up - the app just gives me an error message. So maybe the developers should make sure the free trial feature works before making a gazillion splash screens that don't work.

Horrible app. It seems to only work if you take out a paid subscription. Despite it supposedly being able to import information from other apps it would not do so from my Garmin app. However the final deal breaker was when it wouldn't let me log in with my Google account went through the whole process of resetting the account and then it wouldn't let me in without presenting me with captcha visuals. I'm not stupid but after trying about 10 times I could never get past it.

Your app is making it difficult to delete my account. I only download for a specific reason but realized I didn't need too i owned a device that does this already. So every time I hit the delete account it keeps taking me out to your web page to enter my email and keeps telling me I have to log out first when I'm not logged in. And when I'm about to log in after I receive a code it does it all over again. can you delete my account on your end? This is getting irritating.

This app and the service in general is absolute garbage. I'm giving it one extra star as it has some basic niche utility. but it is highly unreliable. Workouts randomly disappear from the feed or, worse yet, will not appear at all even if they are able to appear on your profile page. people cheat by pausing for long periods and then resuming their workouts which can lead to faster paces than a normal experience. Features randomly go away. Customer service is AI slop. Don't reccomend.

Buggy & expensive. Currently in free trial period. The (huge) cost of a subscription would only be good value if the app was amazing, you use it loads & the fancy features useful. It's not, I don't, & they're not (for me). App constantly nags to get me to do things I don't want to do. In use (cycling), the map randomly resets from North-up to orientation-up, with no input from me, & is often 90deg out! I end up having to interact with little on-screen buttons when riding, which is dangerous.

It was great, but recent changes have ruined the app. 1/ Zooming out of a map results in a white screen. 2/ Constantly having to put heatmaps back on, as the map keeps unselecting them. 3/ AI run reports are actually so bad. 4/ App never loads half the time. Come on, Strava, pull your finger out!

Simple and effective community running app that is sorely missing certain effective tools and features. One of my biggest complaints has to do with the fact that when I do a run I want to see my "total time", but strava prioritizes "moving time". Another issue is just recently I had issues with GPS and my distance was stated to be 0.28 KM while my watch was indicating I went 1.80KM. I want to adjust the distance however I am unable to edit my activity. This is honestly frustrating.

Strava used to be the King of all running apps, but since they put most all their previously free features behind a monthly paywall, I don't rate it high at all & only maintain an account for the sake of keeping in touch with distant friends I used to run with. Strava is certainly not my go to running app anymore. I'd give it 1 & a half stars but it wouldn't let me so I gave it 2. Actually,No; I give it 1 star for screwing us skint runners over.

Strava has become aggressively paywalled, bombarding free users with constant trial prompts. I'm here to support others, not for features—I use Garmin for that. Pushing deceptive trial tactics (like auto-charging users) is exploitative. This strategy risks alienating the very community that gives Strava its value. It's short-sighted and erodes trust. I'll leave if this continues.

great for tracking your activities. also not great at tracking your activities. It Has issues with auto resuming from auto pause, sometimes just draws a straight line and doesnt even count the distance. most of the time it just stops the whole recording on your first stop. this doesnt happen with ever other app. I am speaking from more than a decade of usage.

Pretty disappointed with the whole experience. GPS on for activities like pádel or tennis doesn't make much sense. No dedicated boxing activity (I know, originally it was for running, cycling and swimming, but it has evolved since then), not possible to reorder activities on the watch. Also, it won't import/read activities from any other source. And the UI is far from being clean. I guess it's not for me.

Not worth the money for a paid subscription. The app mainly duplicates data already visible in Samsung Health, and although there are some differences it doesn't justify the cost. I also find it inflexible when trying to just record runs, instead it gives me everything with GPS data. I don't need to know my stats for a walk to the shop. Final reason I stopped using it was because it stopped capturing data from Samsung, and I'm not paying for an unreliable app

After switching to Strava last year, I was quite happy with it. The last three months I have been unable to log into the app. Login to the website works fine, the app just doesn't accept the mailed code. I've been waiting for support for three weeks now, and finally they admitted: 'Unfortunately, our team is limited in our ability to investigate these sorts of issues directly.' Lots of people with the same issues. Not important of course. App is fine when it works, but support is just terrible.

Used to really like the app and it got even better once I got a Pixel Watch 3 but not anymore. This past month or so it has stopped tracking from the actual start though it seems to have the correct distance, and at other times it shows the correct route but has started shaving off half a mile occasionally. Very bizarre. Have tried updating the phone and watch, as well as checking calibrations.

I am very disappointed with the Strava app. It does not work reliably. Many times, the app fails to record activities properly. Even when I start a run, it only begins recording after 200–300 meters, which is not accurate. Recently, my last three activities were not saved at all, which is extremely frustrating after putting in the effort. An app meant for tracking fitness should be accurate and dependable, but this one has become very inconsistent. I hope the developers fix these issues .

Another perfect example of enshittification. Over time, more and more essential features are being locked behind a paywall. Basic challenges such as "Walk 50km" are gone, so you'd pay to set your own challenges; Records and medals just changed to only track your times of the current year instead of all time; Yearly recap is now only for paying subscribers; etc. Oh and of course you're bombarded with begging to pay for premium features.

It was working fine then a few weeks ago it started automatically recording everything as a bike ride instead of a run. slightly annoying but the one star is because it no longer works. I start to record a run, it works fine, then when I finish the run it just sits there thinking about it and I can never hit save. I've restarted my phone and reinstalled it but the same thing keeps happening. The app is now useless.

Strava is the absolute gold standard for tracking runs, rides, and outdoor adventures. The GPS tracking is incredibly accurate, the interface is clean, and the data breakdowns give you the perfect amount of insight without being overwhelming. If it's not on Strava, it didn't happen!

In the 2 years since I first installed Strava it's been easy to use and lots of helpful data on the free user version. It's very inspiring for a novice runner. Since Saturday though there seems to be a bug. I cannot save runs, it's hanging. I've tried multiple options on Reddit thread on the problem to reset but no luck. Seems like it's since the 20th April upgrade. I can no longer use the app to record and monitor my runs. Disappointing

Strava used to be perfect. Never a problem. In the last few months though, it's barely worked at all. Just stops recording, and misses half of my run. Makes it look like I've run a direct line from point to point, through houses and rivers. It's unreliable with my speed, once saying I was going less than 1km/hr and 35km/hr in the same run (can confirm I wasn't). A bit frustrated at this point!

Horrendous app. Logging in doesn't work half the time. Recording auto cancel some runs. Tried to cancel a run, only to find a week later it is still running, cannot cancel as you must wait for all the "run" data to load. Only solution I've found on the web is to log out and log back in to effectively cancel the run. Currently logged out of the app because the login in is bugged once again.

Leading athletics app until they put every single feature that was previously free behind a paywall. its essentially a notebook using google map without the subscription. My second week using it I couldnt share any maps as they were all gltiched and broken platform side, which was confirmed by support. I get wanting to make money but now the app the same as or worse than every other fitness app.

Update 18 Jun 2026: still can't pace for non-run events. Now the app hides my challenges within challenges that I have no desire in ever doing. Time to mark down when this subscription ends. A great app for tracking your events. However, you only get feedback during the activity when you are running. I don't run, I cycle and walk, and would like to know what my pace is during the event without having to turn the app on and causing my phone to use more battery life.

This is the only fraud app which automatically goes to the subscription page when your phone is in your pocket. It even enters the card details itself (although incorrect). And it is not the first time. I faced this issue multiple times. Uninstalling it and putting it out there to check if anyone else is facing the same issue.

Honestly the best running and biking app available. Tracks location and pace wonderfully, and offers a lot of good and interesting stats. The free trial subscription was so good that I'm considering signing up, but it is not necessary to get a lot out of the app.

New update, The continuing of cheating of e-bikes on segments, still a problem!! Not being able to see times while going up hill or down is cumbersome. I understand the safety aspect of it, but show us something towards the last couple hundred feet or so, so that we know when the segments ends. No notification of KOM's being lost, another annoying reason from Strava. It's not fun anymore ☹️ price hike. Everytime I click the app it goes to app info and won't go into the app.

After updating app now glitches, flickers and won't open at all, all within a fraction of a second. Phone still has a modern enough operating system to run the app. No clue what causes it. Tried everything to fix. All other apps in my phone still work. Given up.

I have thoroughly enjoyed the app. I like the activity summaries and the social aspect of it. Strava needs to expand the options available to set as "default". In particular default maps (i.e. choosing not to use the live route playback) and default body model for weight training. Also, I like to share my Workout Analysis panel, but every time I screenshot, I get a pop up saying "use our share function". I'm not using it because the options don't show what I'm trying to share!!

worst app incorrect distance tracking people think they are running fast but it is their myth actually the app tracking the wrong distance if they are running 3km it shows them 3.5 and some time 4km by which we are running on a good pace but it is not correct i tried it for the same route 5 times and each time I got different distance measurement and not minor 10 or 20 meter difference,the difference is about 200 and 500 meter and some time above 500 is normal .

Dear Strava Support, I hope you are doing well. I am following up on my support request, as I have not yet received a response since providing the additional information you requested. It has now been 6 business days, and I am becoming increasingly concerned. I am still unable to access my Strava account, which means I cannot upload any of my activities. As someone who uses Strava regularly and has years of running history on my account, this has been extremely frustrating.

Dear Strava Team, I am writing to request that you add a setting to disable the share prompt that appears whenever a screenshot is taken of an activity. While I understand the desire to encourage social sharing, this popup interferes with my device's scroll capture function. The overlay prevents me from capturing the full details I need for my records, which is quite frustrating. Please consider making this feature optional in a future update. Thank you for your continued work on the app!

I've used the free version for years without issue. Recently the app would close during usage and upon completion of a ride would state it had recovered from an issue, with nothing recorded. Tried to fix the issue by unistalling and reinstalling the app. Now strava wont even recognize the code they send to log in. So totally locked out. A whole new low for strava.

Fix Your Distance Issues!!! I'm uninstalling this because the distance never syncs up with my Galaxy Watch 7/Samsung Health and it always says I ran a shorter distance. Maybe if this synced properly I would be willing to pay for it... but certainly not interested in even using it when it makes it seem like I'm doing worse I actually am.

Better than nothing certainly, but they've lost focus on the user and the fact they are likely exhausted when using the app. You need big areas to click on to reverse an accidental touch, rather than two accidental touches of the main screen area landing you trapped in a full screen map with no option than to click the back arrow, leaving the activity. The number of times I have to stop my run, sort it out and get going again

Easy to Use and navigation. Very good Distance accuracy and Time accuracy. We can see the records of STRAVA app users in the World and can send appreciation comments and can give Kudos to them . while using this app low Battery consumption. Every one's Personal Sports information Record . no charge ,Cost for to hold normal records. App have an appealing design . by using this App you can improve your sports technique by seeing your segments record . Very nice Best and Beautiful STRAVA App .

I tried everything to make this app work. I locked it so it doesn't auto-close, gave it every needed permission, even made sure I had enough battery so my phone doesn't go in battery saver mode (because the app won't work properly in battery saver mode as per the in-app message). After all this the app still fails to record my activity properly. I had simple expectations but the app doesn't meet them, and needs significant improvements. If only it had worked, I would've bought the subscription..

Hi, I’d like to follow up on my refund request submitted yesterday regarding an automatic deduction from my cash account. The funds were intended for my school expenses. I understand that I may have activated a free trial, but I did not intend to subscribe to the one-year plan. I was also not clearly notified or asked for confirmation before the payment was charged to my account. I find it frustrating that the payment was automatically deducted without explicit confirmation. Please.

There was a recent update that added more animations everytime I complete an activity, there doesn't seem to be an option to disable these and they are laggy and annoying. App also frequently crashes and does not track my activity accurately. Initiating a recording of an activity sometimes causes my playing media to pause.

I wa.t to subscribe but so far I am unable to connect strava to my whoop. In whoop it shows that strava is connected and it reads data fekm strava. However in strava when I tried to connect it showed me that an error occurred. I can't see the connection in strava. I emailed support many days ago and so far no reply. If I dknt get a reply I simply won't subscribe.

😠 App going downhill again. Too much junk, not enough control. Wish unwanted features could be switched off or moved to secondary pages. Progress is irrelevant nonsense, I don't use devices during tennis, gym, running squash etc. I'd hide challenges and workouts too, especially distractions in my feed (which is now 2 interactions sideways). Overall not a patch on Ride With GPS at creating routes, following routes live, or looking at performance in detail.

​"Strava is the absolute gold standard for anyone who loves running, cycling, or outdoor activities. The GPS tracking is incredibly accurate, and the 'Segments' feature adds an amazing, healthy layer of competition against your own past times and the local leaderboard. What really sets it apart is the community aspect—it feels like a positive, unfiltered social media platform where everyone actively cheers each other on. It’s highly motivating and easily a 5-star app!"

Good app when it works, but it keeps failing at the worst possible moment. I did a long distance cycling session for hours, and Strava suddenly failed to save my progress. All that distance, effort, and data just gone. This has happened more than once. It’s frustrating because tracking rides is literally the main purpose of the app. Imagine pushing through a long ride only to get hit with "activity not saved". Needs serious fixing for stability and sync reliability. Thankyou

Its not the first time I have a issues with Strava as other companies that trick users offering free trials and create tricky pages with small letters to take money out of users without authorisation. I absolutely despise it, its such a scam. we don't have a team online to help. hate it.

Much improved reliability since my 2 star review of 6 years ago. That focused on the poor Galaxy watch implementation. It's a lot better on the Pixel Watch 3 and I'll assume other watches are similarly improved. Areas for improvement would be in the auto hiding of start/end. By default you only want that when you're near your home.

Accidentally purchased yearly subscription over monthly and support is impossible to contact. Would have loved to sign up for a monthly subscription, but now reconsidering as they have not responded to me in over two weeks. Will gladly update this review if helped.

Everything is a beg to buy. Every screen you change to has a big ad click to buy, subscribe for this, do this. EVERY screen. You spend half your time just getting rid of the subscribe now screens. Other tools do much more and don't annoy you to death. Used to be good, now just a huge begging game where your activity is assaulted with begging.

April 2026, did not work on GrapheneOS. I was disappointed I couldn't use Strava as it was automatically blocked through the Play Integrity API despite its proven increased security. May 2026, elated to share that it's been resolved quickly and can return to recommending Strava. Share and strengthen you and your friend's fitness goals, journeys, and wins through the one app. I hope the subscription becomes affordable but open to different tiers to support.

I used to use this app all the time for tracking runs, bikes, etc and loved it. I deleted the app a little while ago. Now I'm in a new area and wanted to see what trails are around so I download Strava again only to be hugely disappointed. I don't see how free users utilize this app- you can't do anything! If you're happy with paying a subscription for this app then go for it but I can't bring myself to pay for something that I used to get for free. Strava is nice to have- not a necessity.

Auto pause feature does not work as intended, not even close. Everybody using this app pauses themselves. And the entire app and most of the communities are built around unhealthy obsessions with sports and number games. Strava would make you run to your death to make money.
freezes a lot. Also, doesn't let you add if you forget to start the app, you can only crop if you forget to stop. A bit clunky overall. I would never pay for such an unreliable tracker.

They recently updated this app and it is now pure garbage. I use it on Wear OS 4 and used to track every single exercise. It is so incredibly buggy now. I have to leave the app in the foreground or it stops tracking. Never used to happen. Sometimes it goes to the background automatically, but won't pause. But sometimes it does pause. I'm absolutely disgusted with the devs. 40 minutes into an exercise to find I only got 5 minutes of heartrate tracking is insanely enraging. I *hate* this app.

it's barely usable these past few weeks. hangs constantly. won't load any of my other activities while it spends hours uploading the latest one. doesn't stop recording when I press stop. the latest Android version is a mess. if you haven't upgraded yet, don't. it's a total mess!! hopefully the developers release a new version soon to fix all the bugs.

Glitchy mess. It wouldn't save two exercises, for the 1st one I was able to delete it since it was stuck in the save mode, for the second one I was unable to delete and had to uninstall and reinstall. I was thinking of paying for the premium version, but I can't if this app is so unreliable. What a joke. Don't waste your money, use the free version.

Hi Strava team, I understand the need for age limits, but I think there should be a safe solo mode for younger users. Some kids just want to track their cycling, distance, and progress for fun without using social features. A restricted account with private activities, no followers, and no public sharing would make Strava more accessible while keeping safety and privacy in mind. Thanks for considering it.

used to be better when the app didn't force you to search the exercise to get the model that shows what body part you worked. having to go to the list instead of just highlighting the body part is a total drag

the wear OS app is pretty bad, sometimes it stops running, sometimes doesn't continue running in the background, it doesn't allow you to pin your favourite sports or to show most recently used sports, also doesn't seem to sync up with the mobile app during workout, only after completed. Android app also constantly bugs out and I lose my activities, disgrace of an app, and that's using a premium subscription, won't even use it for free as it's too unreliable

It's clunky. Those in charge don't realize you may be ready to run or bike and have to press a button to get it started, so you get the app all ready and press the record button and the app fumbles like it has no idea what you're trying to do. I'm biking and have traffic around me. I want a button to start and no extra BS. I've had rides I thought recorded but didn't because of this. And rides I've done in the past are now too short, just trying to time a hill segment and Strava won't work.

It just keeps getting worse. I left a review recently about how the map no longer zooms in & focuses on me when GPS lock is achieved. It also seems like when I first open the app the first timeline screen never really loads, just black screen. Now this morning I finished my 4mi walk, opened Strava to end the activity, noticed the app was slow to open back up, & when it did it said I was at 2.7 miles. The app gave up somewhere along my walk. Same route as hundreds of other walks. Changing apps.

I am unable to follow the people and this error is showing on strava "the strava server has encountered an error. please contact support if the problem persists." Iam not able to follow people from last 1-2 days. please fix this!😡😡😡😡

Keeps listing false 'achievements' eg. A recent ride showed '9 achievements'. When I checked all results, none of my times for any segments were in my best 3. Always a major disappointment! Limited functions on free version eg you can only see 5 or 6 of your previous efforts for segments and these are greyed out, so barely visible.

great sports app for athletes both professional and amateurs alike. almost every sport is covered. however, quite some glitches, if you are a fast cyclist for example, Strava thinks that you're cheating and says that you must use an e-bike even when you 1. only have a normal road bike and 2. cannot even afford an expensive e-bike.

I love Starva as a stress free way ti track my workouts. They're fun and motivating challenges to join that help me keep going. I've currently logged 30 activities in the past 2 weeks because of Strava, where before I had barely any movement. I always look forward to logging my workouts on Strava, but I never feel pressured. Looking forward to seeing to my mood and health improve even more.

The login screen is completely broken for users running GrapheneOS which is far more secure than default android or apple. ​When I enter my password or one-time code, the submission button fails silently and does absolutely nothing. This is a known issue with Strava’s rigid backend, which hangs indefinitely when it can't run tracking or Play Integrity checks, rather than failing gracefully and letting the user log in. ​Please fix and I will change to 5 stars.

The app does not track distance properly!!! I switched to Strava from Adidas Running this spring when a known software glitch in Running caused it to stop syncing activities from my watch. Strava worked great for a while. Now it's counting 30 mile bike rides as 16 miles and 16 mile bike rides as 9. The loop I ride is 8 miles. 2 laps can't be 9 miles!!!!

my first run i ran 6 miles, it didn't measure properly and showed 4.76. I know I ran 6 because I live in the country and I ran 3 blocksout and then back in. edit: I gave it a second try. turns out if you're not actively on the tab when its recording it doesn't track distance, just time. so I ran 8mi, tracked 2.8. I'm wholeheartedly disappointed.
It's a great app to keep you accountable while storing all your historical activities. The app provides feedback and analytics. Plus, you can join groups and interact with other users.

Won't connect to Samsung Health. Can't use the website on mobile without it transferring you to the app which doesn't work well. Overall app is only useful if you don't already having a tracking app that you want to use. Was excited to try this app, but now I'm just disappointed.

As with all apps, the more features added, the more it breaks existing stuff. I keep seeing cycling segment PR's on the ride headline but clicking through they aren't there (and they aren't PR's anyway!). Year headings on personal segment times on Android are missing now. Starting Strava sometimes fails with just a spinning ring in the app. Luckily I use Garmin to record my rides and just transfer to Strava so I don't know what else might be broken.

Too many network errors while other apps have no issues. Contacted me in a very ICE manner that I had breached their rules, their rationale was based on an erroneous machine translation of the name of a road. Route mapping has become route napping. Just did 62, it logged 50. Average speed and performance on segments all void. Liaising with support is the same three times over: here is the support page that will explain the steps you need to take, but none worked. Why pay?

Strava is an excellent app for tracking running, cycling, and other fitness activities. The GPS tracking, performance stats, and activity sharing features make workouts more motivating and engaging. The app works smoothly and is especially useful for athletes and fitness enthusiasts. However, some advanced features and detailed insights require a subscription. Overall, it’s one of the best fitness tracking apps available.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Great data tracking, even for free users!As a basic user, I find this app incredibly useful. It syncs perfectly with my Amazfit watch via the Zepp app, meaning all my rides and activities upload automatically. I love that I can manually add other types of workouts too. It is a fantastic tool to keep all my fitness data in one place.

Liked the app at first, but doesnt seem to track treadmill runs at all. Then if I track them manually on my watch, I need to log into a browser to add them, as the "add manual" option doesnt appear to show anywhere in the app? Appreciate "serious" runners might always run outdoors, but im still running the same distance on a treadmill so id still like to see it recorded. Also the homepage widget on android never updates and needs to be deleted and readded after every run to show weekly progres

The watch app is utterly useless. When I'm tracking my workout, I want the app to be constantly open so that I can see my pace and other parameters. But after a while the app goes into the background and I cannot configure it to stay open. And the ads coercing you to buy are super frequent and annoying.

It's a decent app but it doesn't track the GPS all the time and randomly flicks the route to somewhere I haven't been, and then back again. So I really have no idea how much I have walked. If it zips my location half a mile somewhere and back, that's a mile that I didn't walk. Maybe good in cities with good GPS but where I live it's not reliable as a walk/run tracker. The elevation may not be correct if it zips my location to the top of a hill I just walked down then back to the bottom. Shame.

What happened to this app? I haven't used it in about 3 years but a friend sent me a link on a hike he wants to take me on. The link gave me errors on the browser, I installed the app, still got errors. So then I decided I'm just going to search and see if I can find it. No I have to pay to search! just for a search? You've got to be kidding me. And a search I'm having to perform because the link he copied directly from your app doesn't work. That's suspicious. I hope all greedy developers fail.

Strava is honestly one of the most motivating fitness apps I’ve used. It makes tracking workouts feel social, fun, and competitive in a healthy way. I love the community energy, the ‘kudos’ feature, and how easy it is to see my progress over time. It keeps me consistent and accountable especially on days I don’t feel like training. Highly recommend for anyone serious about fitness or just trying to stay active.

This app is nice and all, but there's an insane annoying amount of ads and for some reason the track recorder thingy doesn't record my track whenever my phone is off but the app is still running. I tested it out, and noticed the trail was inaccurate. Please fix this. Please also fix this annoying bug where I move the map a bit but then it moves back automatically. It's extremely annoying and would be amazing for it to be fixed.

The story of corporate greed. It started as functioning running app where you and friends can hang. It's one of the best apps in its field with smooth and interactive UI andmany advanced features but I only have one concern that is after the latest update many free features have been transferred to the Subscription plans. Bro why are you guys doing this you it was as good as it is before that with sufficient features now I have no choice but to use other apps. what is strava even doing??? the app has been absolutely bugged for several days atp and they still haven't fixed anything, I've already done and recorded several activities in the app and every single time, it would get stuck loading whenever I try to upload the activity and it even lost the data of two of them.

I've used Strava for years with a paid subscription. The subscription is very expensive but I always thought it was worth it due to Segment analysis. At first, I was happy about this app but then after a few days it somehow availed the free trial by itself and the app became so hard to use due to its constant loading at the home page, I cant even save my own progress. Even if i switch to wifi from mobile data, the loading doesnt seem to be fixed. Will change review upon fix, or uninstall if it doesnt get fixed.

As a DoorDasher, I rely on mileage trackers for tax reasons. I've used MileageWise for a month now, and I'm impressed. First off, it has a lifetime payment plan, which in an age of pouring money into monthly subscriptions, is unheard of! And it's the most popular option, so I'm not the only person on Earth who believes in only paying for things once! I do wish it was quicker on the draw for automatic logs, because I get a lot of anxious customers who don't give me the time I need to log trips!

Was able to upload one month worth of information unless I subscribed to a plan. I wanted to get a feel for the rest of the features before I paid for it while on the 14 day trial. Information that transferred had addresses that I didn't visit and didn't show on my timeline so to start this wasn't going to be a quick upload and done. The app itself, would not let me past choose a plan so since I couldn't even get a feel for the app, I uninstalled.

Very disappointed now. More accurate than other apps. Now I have no logs from June 2nd -30th (the notification bar, showed the app tracking). Support was responsive in trying to find settings that would have caused the issue, and helpful in trying to import Google timeline data, but for some reason that was unavailable. Now they say the development team will further investigate and "the issue will be addressed in an upcoming release" -they suggest using their AI wizard to recreate what's lost.

Actually it does what it does very well compared to other apps. But it needs to be more clear about which app does what MileageWise vs MileageLog. I thought it would do tracking for free (or there was a free tier). Not just a free trial. That said, it is one of the cleanest tracking apps out there. It "just works" and i'm glad to have found it.

So, it makes you pay. Naturally, I had some questions. They got back to me with answers within 5-10 minutes. I was pleased with their response, so decided to go ahead and pay the monthly. This is an excellent, feature-rich, yet fairly easy to use mileage tracker app. I love the various mileage recording options. They let me maximize (or optimize :) the mileage deductions. MileageWise is a really useful app. The majority of mileage-tracking apps that I have used in the past have crashed, leading to erroneous data. This app is awesome. It has features that enable you to add missed trips.
